**From the High Republic to the Original Trilogy and Beyond!**

Jabba the Hutt! Darth Vader! Mace Windu! Keeve Trennis, Thrawn and more! Introducing new characters, new twists and new turns across all of Marvel's Star Wars line! This star-studded issue puts the pieces on the board for what's to come in a galaxy far, far away!
title: Revelations (2023) #1

synopsis: Reversal of Fortune (sometimes also called Evasive Action: Reversal of Fortune) was a series of daily webstrips published on Hyperspace October 2004 through June 2005, written by Paul Ens and illustrated by Thomas Hodges. The story takes place in the last days of the Clone Wars. As of Celebration IV, a print version of Reversal of Fortune was being considered.
title: Reversal of Fortune

synopsis: "Ring Race" is a comic story written by Martin Fisher, drawn by Bob Molesworth, and edited by Gunther Nickel. The story was published in the first issue of Star Wars Rebels Magazine, which was originally released in the UK on January 28, 2015, and later in the US on October 20, 2015.
title: Ring Race

synopsis: When a ship full of Sith warriors arrived in Galactic Alliance space, the fight to destroy it accidentally uncovered a deadly menace: a long-hidden group of clones, secretly created as insidious weapons capable of wielding the Force and heedless of the differences between light side and dark side. Now the clones have escaped -- and evidence suggests they are flawed by genetic disease and violent madness. Jedi Knight Jaden Korr pursues the clones, hoping to heal them but prepared to destroy them. What he doesn't know is that Sith agents are hot on his heels, determined not only to recover the c
title: Riptide

synopsis: The rebels of the Ghost Crew must use their unique talents and work together to fend off the imperial forces coming their way.
title: Rise of the Rebels

synopsis: Star Wars: River of Chaos was a four-issue series of comics published in 1995 by Dark Horse Comics.
title: River of Chaos

synopsis: Several months after the destruction of the Death Star, Emperor Palpatine sends an Imperial pilot to spy on the governor of a distant planet, M'haeli. Although Palpatine knows of certain Rebel activity on M'Haeli, he does not know of the presence of one particular Rebel: Princess Leia Organa! Can Leia undermine the enemy from within, or is she doomed to drown in a river of chaos? This ground-breaking four-issue series takes readers behind enemy lines, to see the Rebellion through the eyes of an officer of the Empire!
title: River of Chaos #1

synopsis: The spy trade is a dangerous game, and if you lose you die. But there are greater dangers than death to those who cast false identities. There is the danger of forgetting who you are. In this second issue of Star Wars: River of Chaos, Leia is behind enemy lines with only a handful of Rebel spies at her command. But do they all want to break the Empire, or is there an Imperial in ally's clothing amongst them?
title: River of Chaos #2

synopsis: Leia has uncovered Ranulf as an Imperial spy! She and her covert Rebel team are holding him prisoner and preparing his execution, but when the Empire has the Rebel Mora in its merciless grasp, it's Ranulf who volunteers to rush to her rescue! Is it a plot to regain his freedom, or can an Imperial officer repeal the indoctrination of the Empire?
title: River of Chaos #3

synopsis: The war of good against evil has been waged for centuries. It began long before Ranulf joined the Imperials, long before he was ever born, and the war will continue long after he is dead. But now Ranulf is forced to fight one battle of this war, a battle that rages in his soul. When Governor Grigor's Imperial forces clash with Princess Leia's Rebels, Ranulf must choose between his Imperial allies and the Rebel he has come to love…and the outcome of his personal battle may change the current of this River of Chaos!
title: River of Chaos #4

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #1
synopsis: The Rebellion is here! The biggest movie of the year jumps from the big screen to the comic book page! All looks lost for the Rebellion against the Empire as they learn of the existence of a new super-weapon, the Death Star. New heroes Jyn Erso and Cassian Andor set out on a desperate mission to steal the plans to destroy the new super-weapon. Written by Jody Houser (Max Ride) and drawn by Emilio Laiso (Star Wars Annual; Hercules), join as the story is set up for the epic saga to follow.
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #1

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #2
synopsis: Jyn Erso continues her quest with Cassian Andor to help the Rebellion fight the sinister Galactic Empire. Following the trail to the ancient Jedha City to find Rebel extremist Saw Guerra, Jyn and Cassian get caught in a firefight with the Empire…only to then be taken prisoner by Saw’s followers!
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #2

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #3
synopsis: Jyn Erso continues her quest with Cassian Andor to help the Rebellion fight the Galactic Empire. The team has located Jyn's father, Galen Erso, a Death Star scientist! But the evil Director Krennic gets their first, and he's out for blood!
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #3

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #4
synopsis: Jyn Erso brings the news of the Death Star plans to the Rebellion…It seems her father built a way for the superweapon to be destroyed! But when the Rebellion refuses to help, Jyn is left only with a small crew of soldiers to fight.
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #4

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #5
synopsis: The Star Wars story continues! Rogue One, the small rebel crew comprising Jyn Erso and friends, has arrived on Scarif! But much danger lays in the path between the rebels and the Death Star plans - Including never-before-seen moments!
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #5

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #6
synopsis: Jyn Erso and Cassian Andor, along with their ragtag team of Rebel activists, are on the hunt for the Imperial Death Star plans. But the Empire is not going to back down without a fight. Will the Rebels succeed? Or with the Empire destroy them without batting an eye? Read the conclusion of the epic blockbuster film!
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #6

synopsis: Jyn Erso is used to everyone leaving her behind. On a mission to track down her father, she discovers some causes are worth sticking around to fight for.
title: Rogue One

displayTitle: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation
title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story Adaptation

synopsis: Cassian Andor is one of the top intelligence agents in the ranks of the Rebel Alliance, ably assisted by his reprogrammed Imperial security droid, K-2SO. But naturally, the two weren't always on the same side of the Galactic Civil War. Now, for the first time, read the story of the pair's first contentious meeting! It is very likely not to go well.
title: Rogue One - Cassian & K-2SO

synopsis: Rogue One introduced two dynamic partners to the Rebel Alliance: a morally grey spy who will do whatever it takes to get the job done and a former imperial spy Droid who says wherever is on his mind without a second thought about how his painfully honest remarks will be received. This comic is the story of how their partnership began. Looking forward to the new Cassian and K2 Disney+ TV show? Start here!
title: Rogue One - Cassian & K-2SO Special 1

synopsis: Jyn Erso is used to everyone leaving her behind. On a mission to track down her father, she discovers some causes are worth sticking around to fight for.
title: Rogue One

synopsis: An in-universe intelligence file assembled by the Rebel Alliance in the wake of the mission to steal the Death Star plans. Framed as recovered Alliance records, it profiles the operatives of Rogue One and their allies, from Jyn Erso and Cassian Andor to K-2SO, Chirrut Îmwe, Baze Malbus, and Bodhi Rook, alongside the Rebel leadership and the Imperial figures who opposed them, chief among them Orson Krennic and Galen Erso. Through personnel dossiers, mission reports, and intelligence on the Empire's secret superweapon, it retraces the covert operation depicted in *Rogue One: A Star Wars Story* that delivered the Death Star's schematics into Rebel hands.
title: Rogue One: Rebel Dossier

synopsis: Rogue One: Recon is a short immersive 360 experience created by ILMxLAB that is connected to "Rogue One: A Star Wars Story." Conflict, above all else, rules the galaxy. As an everyday X-wing pilot flying for the Rebellion, be immediately immersed in the vastness of space with only your R2 unit and wingman by your side. What starts as a routine mission for Mon Mothma takes a sharp turn as you stumble upon unexpected Imperial presence, and something they will do anything to keep hidden.
title: Rogue One: Recon

synopsis: Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker find themselves on another treacherous mission that could unintentionally give ultimate power to the wrong person.
title: Rogue Planet

synopsis: "Roll of the Dice" is a short story by Karen Miller that appeared in Star Wars Insider 135. The story takes place immediately prior to the events of the novel X-Wing: Mercy Kill, which Miller was allowed to read prior to its release as reference for her story. Her idea for the story was approved by March 28, 2012, and Miller had half of the first draft written the next day. The story was submitted by April 11.
title: Roll of the Dice

synopsis: Star Wars Roll Out is a series of whimsical animated shorts featuring new adventures in a charming and unique animation style. Join Rey, Chewbacca, BB-8, the porgs, and other favorite characters from a galaxy far, far away, in a bright and colorful art style perfect for children and adults!

displayTitle: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure
synopsis: In a new antenna-bending adventure, BB-8 tries to help his friends at the Resistance Base only to become lost deep in the jungle.
title: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure

displayTitle: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure - Chapter 2
synopsis: BB-8 runs away from a strange shape-shifting beast that can mimic his appearance, only to get trapped in the sticky web of a giant spider-like creature.
title: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure - Chapter 2

displayTitle: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure - Chapter 3
synopsis: Old friends and new allies team up to help BB-8 escape!
title: Roll Out: BB-8 And The Jungle Adventure - Chapter 3

synopsis: Chewbacca and the porgs set out across the water of Ahch-To, only to run afoul of a hungry sea monster.

synopsis: This is some rescue! The porgs team up to free Chewie from the clutches of a monstrous underwater creature.

synopsis: The blorp is absorbing everything and everyone in its path on Takodana! Maz Kanata must think fast to save her castle, her guests, and herself.

synopsis: Rey finds a rare part while scavenging on Jakku, only to have it stolen by thugs!

synopsis: Rey stumbles upon a treasure trove of rare parts and a mysterious flower, but she'll have to protect them both when the poachers return.

displayTitle: Roll Out: Rey And The Secret Treasure - Chapter 3
synopsis: Rey must think fast to save herself and her treasure from the giant vacuum-like weapon.
title: Roll Out: Rey And The Secret Treasure - Chapter 3

synopsis: After escaping (with help from the broken droids), Finn repays the favor and gets a second chance to prove himself to Captain Phasma.

synopsis: Sweet but chaotic Ben Solo keeps bungling the plans of his dad Han and his Uncle Chewbacca. When he secretly tags along on their mission to capture some crystal butterflies, a gang of rival Bug Hunters make Ben look like the least of their problems!.
title: Roll Out: Ben Solo And The Bug Hunters

displayTitle: Roll Out: Ben Solo And The Bug Hunters - Chapter 2
synopsis: Han Solo and Chewbacca have been captured by the Bug Hunters, but Ben Solo just might be able to set them free, while wrecking the evil gang's plans!
title: Roll Out: Ben Solo And The Bug Hunters - Chapter 2

displayTitle: Roll Out: Ben Solo And The Bug Hunters - Chapter 3
synopsis: Ben Solo, Han, and Chewbacca are on their way back to the Millennium Falcon, but the Bug Hunters are in pursuit and they've got a score to settle with Ben!
title: Roll Out: Ben Solo And The Bug Hunters - Chapter 3

synopsis: Rookies: No Turning Back is the second webstrip of the Rookies series on Hyperspace. It was both written and drawn by Pablo Hidalgo, based on a scenario by Curtis Smith in Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game. The series ran from June to October 2006.
title: Rookies: No Turning Back

synopsis: Rookies: Rendezvous is a daily webstrip on Hyperspace, written and drawn by Pablo Hidalgo. The series began in February 2006, and the story is set in the same year as the Battle of Yavin.
title: Rookies: Rendezvous

synopsis: Dark Horse is pleased to announce its 2006 Free Comic Book Day offering will be in the form of a special split issue featuring original Star Wars and Conan material. Each story is ten pages long and the content for both is appropriate for all ages. Our Star Wars tale "Routine Valor" is set during the Clone Wars and features Commander Cody and his troops in an indefensible position facing an unassailable enemy. Star Wars: Routine Valor features story by Randy Stradley with art by Doug Wheatley. In the Conan story "The Spear", a sorcerer receives a vision about a tremendous force washing over th
title: Routine Valor

synopsis: The explosive ore of the moon of the planet Themis can provide a valuable power source for the Republic – or for the Separatists! Some tough negotiations lie in store for our Jedi heroes in the latest issue's comic strip story – can Anakin and Obi-Wan hold their nerve in the face of provocation and find a way to win over the planet's dignitaries?
title: Runaway Starfighter

synopsis: "Rust Never Sleeps" is a comic first published in The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 156, written by Alan Moore. It was later reprinted and colorized in Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 2.
title: Rust Never Sleeps

synopsis: "SaBBotage" was a mini-comic that was created by Chris Eliopoulos and Jordie Bellaire that accompanied the first issue of the comic series Star Wars: Poe Dameron.
title: SaBBotage

synopsis: "Galactic Tales: Saber Truth" is a short story featuring the character General Grievous that was published in Star Wars Insider as part of the "Galactic Tales" series celebrating the 20th anniversary of Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones. The story is written by Richard Dinnick, and was published in Star Wars Insider 210 on May 3, 2022.
title: Saber Truth

synopsis: Sabine uses her skills and her art to take down a group of stormtroopers all by herself.
title: Sabine's Art Attack

synopsis: In this issue's all-new Star Wars: The Clone Wars comic adventure, the Separatists attempt to highjack the funeral of the valiant Roonan Halsey. With no transport or comlink, how can Obi-Wan possibly warn everyone in time? There's only one way to find out…
title: Sabotage

synopsis: "Sabotaged Supplies" is a comic strip published in the seventh issue of the Star Wars Rebels Magazine series by Egmont UK Ltd. It was written by Martin Fisher, illustrated by Ingo Römling and edited by Gunther Nickel. The comic was published on July 15, 2015.
title: Sabotaged Supplies

synopsis: Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice is a Star Wars Legends novel and the fifth volume in the Star Wars: Legacy of the Force series. It was written by Karen Traviss and first published by Del Rey in hardcover on May 29, 2007. The first paperback edition was released on April 29, 2008 and includes the novella Boba Fett: A Practical Man by Traviss.
title: Sacrifice

synopsis: "Salvaged" is a comic story in Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 9. It features the clone trooper pilot of a Republic starfighter aboard a salvage ship.
title: Salvaged

synopsis:
**Starring in Her First Ever Solo Series!**

Fresh off of a string of disappointments, Sana Starros returns to her family's ancestral home for some downtime. But family time is far from relaxing for a scoundrel and her family of ne'er do wells, especially when Stormtroopers crash dinner.
title: Sana Starros #01

synopsis: The Starros clan are on a collision course of a lifetime! What mysteries from Sana's past are coming home to roost? And whose party are they about to crash?
title: Sana Starros #02

synopsis: Sana has just run into one of the roughest bounty hunters in the galaxy: Deva Lompop! But what does she want with Sana? And why is Sana on a rancor?!
title: Sana Starros #03

synopsis:
**Dangerous Sibling Rivalry!**

Sana must take on her brother Phel or all is lost! But will it be Deva Lompop who destroys the Starros family?
title: Sana Starros #04

synopsis:
**The Starros Family Infiltrates an Imperial Destroyer. What Can Go Wrong?**

Sana Starros and bounty hunter Deva Lompop have boarded the King's Ransom to save one of their family. But once they find out they've been betrayed, the Starros clan will never be the same!
title: Sana Starros #05

synopsis: The people of Pabu are still reeling from the natural disaster that devastated their home. In an effort to help them rebuild, Phee has recruited Tech, Omega, Hunter, and Wrecker to accompany her on a series of missions to fund Pabu’s reconstruction. Clone Force 99 is in for more than they bargained for, however. Little do they know their next mission is going to get a little … complicated.
title: Sanctuary: A Bad Batch Novel

synopsis: "Sandbound on Tatooine" was a short story written for Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 10. It was written by Peter M. Schweighofer and illustrated by Jeff Rebner. Platt Okeefe, the infamous smuggler, had crashed on Tatooine, and encountered a number of local wildlife while escaping from the Dune Sea.
title: Sandbound on Tatooine

synopsis: "Scorched" is a short story by Delilah S. Dawson, with art by Joe Corroney, that was released on May 3, 2016 as part of Star Wars Insider 165. On February 17, 2017 it was published on StarWars.com. The story is a tie-in to the novel Bloodline, written by Claudia Gray. It features the character Greer Sonnel, an ally of Senator Leia Organa, who is featured in Bloodline. "Scorched" takes place before the events of Bloodline, providing a glimpse of Greer's life as a starship racer before entering politics.
title: Scorched

synopsis: It began as a simple planetfall on Ord Mantell for some rest and relaxation. Han wanted to gamble his reward money into a sum large enough to pay off Jabba the Hutt, while Leia preferred more sophisticated entertainment. But now the princess is missing, and a knee-high droid is challenging the smuggler to a transgalactic race, with Leia's life as the prize. In this branching, choose-your-path adventure, the reader guides Han Solo, mercenary smuggler and pilot extraordinaire, through a fast-paced tale of quick wits and tough decisions, sometimes analyzing the situation as Han sees it, sometimes trusting his flying skills, and sometimes leaving his destiny to luck itself.
title: Scoundrel's Luck

synopsis:
Shortly after the Battle of Yavin, Han Solo has lost the reward the Alliance paid him to the pirate Crimson Jack, leaving him deep in debt to Jabba the Hutt with a bounty on his head. On the planet Wukkar he meets Eanjer Kunarazti, who claims a local crime lord named Avrak Villachor murdered his father and stole over 163 million credits, and who hires Han to assemble a crew to break into Villachor's heavily guarded vault at Marblewood Estate for an even split. With the help of fixer Rachele Ree, Han recruits a team of thieves and specialists, including the ghost-thief twins Bink and Tavia Kitik, the sleight-of-hand artist Zerba Cher'dak, shipjacker Dozer Creed, explosives expert Kell Tainer, and Winter, a woman with perfect recall. Through a miscommunication, an estranged Lando Calrissian also joins as the crew's front man.

As Imperial Intelligence agents shadow the Falleen Black Sun Vigo Qazadi, a guest at Villachor's estate, the team discovers that the vault protects encrypted files central to Black Sun's power and a rare Alderaanian cryodex needed to decode them. Over the course of Villachor's public Festival of Four Honorings, the con artists case the estate, infiltrate his security, and stoke his paranoia, with Lando posing as a defector offering a working cryodex. Villachor grows convinced he is being tested by Qazadi or Prince Xizor and, on the day of the heist, seizes Han as a prisoner just as the whole crew springs into action, forcing them to improvise their way through the score.
title: Scoundrels

synopsis: Scourge is a Star Wars Legends novel by author Jeff Grubb that adapts the events of the Star Wars Roleplaying Game adventure Tempest Feud from Wizards of the Coast, which was co-written by Grubb and Owen K.C. Stephens. Scourge was published by Del Rey in paperback on April 24, 2012, while the Science Fiction Book Club released an exclusive hardcover edition.
title: Scourge

synopsis: Rebel pilot and rogue archaeologist wander side by side into the darkest shadows of the galaxy as Luke Skywalker reluctantly teams up with Doctor Aphra! The Doctor makes Luke an offer he can't afford to pass up…one that leads him to a very rare gathering at the heart of the infamous Screaming Citadel. Will Luke find what he's looking for? Can Aphra be trusted? Or will they both wind up victims of the Citadel's Queen?
title: The Screaming Citadel #1

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #31: The Screaming Citadel, Part II
synopsis: A rebel pilot and a rogue archaeologist delve into the darkest shadows of the galaxy side by side, as Luke Skywalker reluctantly teams up with Doctor Aphra! The not-so-good Doctor will make Luke an offer he can't afford to pass up...one that leads him to a very rare gathering at the heart of the infamous Screaming Citadel! Will Luke find what he's looking for? Can Aphra be trusted? Or will they both wind up victims of the Citadel's queen? And as this unlikely duo steps right into the lair of one of the most powerful, reclusive and dangerous women in the galaxy, can Han and Leia be far behind them?
title: Star Wars (2015) #31: The Screaming Citadel, Part II

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #32: The Screaming Citadel, Part IV
synopsis: A rebel pilot and a rogue archaeologist delve into the darkest shadows of the galaxy side by side, as Luke Skywalker reluctantly teams up with Doctor Aphra! The not-so-good Doctor will make Luke an offer he can't afford to pass up...one that leads him to a very rare gathering at the heart of the infamous Screaming Citadel! Will Luke find what he's looking for? Can Aphra be trusted? Or will they both wind up victims of the Citadel's queen? And as this unlikely duo steps right into the lair of one of the most powerful, reclusive and dangerous women in the galaxy, can Han and Leia be far behind them?
title: Star Wars (2015) #32: The Screaming Citadel, Part IV

synopsis: "Secrets of Sienar" is a comic strip written by Martin Fisher, drawn by Eva Widermann and edited by Gunther Nickel. The story was published in the thirteenth issue of the Star Wars Rebels Magazine series by Egmont UK Ltd, which was released in December 30, 2015.
title: Secrets of Sienar

synopsis: Lucasfilm, ILMxLAB, and THE VOID today announced a new hyper-reality experience called Star Wars: Secrets of the Empire, which promises to plunge fans into the Star Wars universe. Created by ILMxLAB, Lucasfilm's immersive entertainment division, in collaboration with THE VOID, it will open to the public at two new VOID Experience Centers at Downtown Disney at Disneyland Resort and Disney Springs at Walt Disney World Resort beginning this holiday season. Star Wars: Secrets of the Empire will allow fans to move freely in an untethered, social, and multi-sensory experience, including interaction with friends, fans, and Star Wars characters.
title: Secrets of the Empire

synopsis: Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and his apprentice, Obi-Wan Kenobi, are tasked with protecting a technologically gifted boy from the relentless bounty hunters pursuing him.
title: Secrets of the Jedi

synopsis: "Seeds" is a short comic story that was published in the magazine Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.49 in 2013.
title: Seeds

synopsis: "Seeing Red" is a short story written by Jay Watamaniuk and released on the official site for the video game Star Wars: The Old Republic on July 14, 2021. It sees Jekiah Ordo, a character from the recent Game Update 6.3, dealing with a crisis that threatens to tear the Mandalorian clans apart.
title: Seeing Red

synopsis: "Senate Perspective" is a comic strip published in the ninth issue of the Star Wars Rebels Magazine series by Egmont UK Ltd. It was written by Martin Fisher, illustrated by Ingo Römling and edited by Gunther Nickel. The comic was published on September 9, 2015.[1]
title: Senate Perspective

displayTitle: Servants of the Empire: Edge of the Galaxy
synopsis: Zare Leonis can’t wait to join the Empire as part of the imperial Academy someday. But when his sister suddenly goes missing, he begins to question his loyalty to the government he hopes to serve.
title: Servants of the Empire: Edge of the Galaxy

displayTitle: Servants of the Empire: Imperial Justice
synopsis: Zare Leonis may be a new student at the Imperial Academy, but his real mission, as he tries to prove to everyone he can be trusted, is to find out the truth, no matter the risk.
title: Servants of the Empire: Imperial Justice

displayTitle: Servants of the Empire: Rebel in the Ranks
synopsis: Zare Leonis’s time as an imperial cadet is testing him more than ever before, and he feels no closer to discovering what really happened to his sister.
title: Servants of the Empire: Rebel in the Ranks

displayTitle: Servants of the Empire: The Secret Academy
synopsis: Zare Leonis knows something about the Empire that he isn’t supposed to know. And he might be the only one who can use the information to bring the regime down for good.
title: Servants of the Empire: The Secret Academy

synopsis: Some games are played for life or death. Javul Charn is the most famous pop star in the galaxy – and the runaway bride of a violent lieutenant in Black Sun, the crime syndicate commanded by Prince Xizor. Or so Javul says. Soon after Dash Rendar, broke and desperate, agrees to be Javul's bodyguard, he realizes that openness is not her strong suit – and that murder is stalking her tour. Between the discovery of dead bodies in a cargo hold and an attack by an unidentified warship, Dash and co-pilot Eaden Vrill desperately try to understand who is terrorizing Javul's tour and why. When
title: Shadow Games

synopsis: Luke Skywalker and Lando Calrissian embark on a journey of discovery when a vision of the dark side brings the two back together again. As Ochi of Bestoon hunts for his prey, the future of the conflict between Jedi and Sith becomes even more uncertain.
title: Shadow of the Sith

synopsis: Darth Vader has a covert agent to take care of his dirty work: Jix. Vader dispatches Jix to take care of an Imperial governor who has defected to the Rebel Alliance. But Jix finds a different, more important traitor, and winds up in a one-man war against a legion of stormtroopers. This one-shot collects the hit serial from Topps' Star Wars Galaxy magazine.
title: Shadow Stalker

synopsis: While a grim Luke Skywalker reels from Darth Vader's revelation on the cloud city of Bespin, the tattered remnants of the Rebel fleet desperately seek sanctuary and time to muster new allies. Their forces are weak, and that has not escaped the notice of Emperor Palpatine—ultimate victory lies within his grasp. All it will take is another blow to crush this Rebel Alliance, one more straw to break their backs. A blow, Palpatine reasons, that could be struck by the vast, criminal underworld that lurks within the Shadows of the Empire…
title: Shadows of the Empire #1

synopsis: It is a time of crisis. Han Solo, frozen in carbonite, is being taken to the vile gangster Jabba the Hutt. As Princess Leia mounts a rescue mission and Darth Vader scours the Galaxy for Luke Skywalker, another sinister figure emerges. Cunning and ruthless, leader of a powerful crime syndicate, he will pit himself against Vader for the favor of their mutual master...the dreaded Emperor.
title: Shadows of the Empire

synopsis: Prince Xizor controls the largest merchant fleet in the galaxy—that makes him a valuable asset to the Empire and to the Emperor. And that makes him Darth Vader's hated enemy. As Luke, Leia, and Chewbacca head to Tatooine to rescue Han Solo, they get caught in the crossfire of the galaxy's deadliest rivalry… in the Shadows of the Empire'.
title: Shadows of the Empire #2

synopsis: They outnumber him and outgun him. By all rights, Luke Skywalker should be one dead Jedi-in-training, the target of a surprise attack by Jabba's swoop gang. But he's not… and even he isn't certain of the reason!
title: Shadows of the Empire #3

synopsis: What would it take for you to step into the den of a rancor? What treasure would tempt you to stare death in the face? Aboard Boba Fett's ship is a carbonite-encased Han Solo. Jabba's willing to pay plenty for Solo, making him a tempting treasure for every bounty hunter in the galaxy. But tempting enough to board the Slave I? Apparently, somebody thinks so!
title: Shadows of the Empire #4

synopsis: The bounty on Skywalker's head is good cold or warm, dead or alive. A live bounty will always bring a bigger reward than a dead one, but when the target leaves you no choice you take what you can get.
title: Shadows of the Empire #5

synopsis: Leia has been captured by Prince Xizor, underlord of the galaxy's most ruthless criminal organization, and Han Solo has been encased in carbonite by Darth Vader, a package Boba Fett intends to deliver to Jabba the Hutt. As the Rebels approach their most deperate[sic] hour, it appears as though their long battle may be at an end. But looks can be deceiving in the Shadows of the Empire!
title: Shadows of the Empire #6

synopsis: Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution was a sequel to the original story by the author of the Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire novel, Steve Perry. The series was the first foray into comics by the best-selling Perry. The story is set in 4 ABY, which sets it one year after The Empire Strikes Back, and more or less shortly after the events of Return of the Jedi. Xizor, the leader of Black Sun, has been killed by Darth Vader, and Guri, a beautiful Human replica droid and Xizor's personal assassin, is left wondering what her future will hold now that she no longer has anyone to answer to. Th
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution

synopsis: Xizor, the leader of the Black Sun organization, has been destroyed, and Guri, a Human replica droid and Xizor's personal assassin, is left wondering what her future will hold now that she no longer has anyone to answer to. Throughout the ongoing struggle for underworld supremacy, Guri endeavors to uncover hints of her Human side – if it even exists. Her search first takes her to the far side of the Rim looking for droid programming genius Massad Thrumble, in the hope that he can alter her programming.
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution #1

synopsis: Human replica droid Guri continues her quest to discover just how Human she really is. She braves the dangers of the tropical planet Murninkam looking for the surgical droid known as Doc, who has been abducted by Spinda Caveel, a brilliant but more-than-slightly mad scientist, and his deadly cohorts, the Pikkel Sisters. Meanwhile, bounty hunter Kar Yang is hot on the trail of Guri at the request of the shadowy Azool, who wants Guri for reasons that will finally become clear in this issue. A comic-book sequel to the best-selling Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire, written by Shadows novelist Stev
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution #2

synopsis: Human replica droid Guri has located the only one potentially capable of altering her assassin-driven programming, the surgical droid known as Doc. But now she's being pursued by a bounty hunter, Kar Yang. Savan, working in disguise as a Human female, is trying to secure her hold on the Black Sun organization -- and realizes that Guri is the key. Luke, Han, and Leia, who are meeting with Savan on Coruscant in order to work out a peaceful end to the tumultuous turnover in Black Sun, have no idea who she really is, though.
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution #3

synopsis: Now that Guri has found Doc, and the de-droiding procedure has been started, the search is on for Guri. Will Spinda Caveel, the Pikkel Sisters, Savan, or Han, Luke, and Leia be the first to find Guri and Doc? Will Doc be able to finish the Humanizing procedure on Guri before they are found? Just as we think things are going well, Luke gets a "feeling," and Leia discovers the truth behind Savan.
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution #4

synopsis: Guri, the most wanted droid in the galaxy awakens from surgery. Doc and Thrumble stand by to see if the surgery successfully removed the bad memories of Black Sun. Meanwhile, our gang is on the way to collect Guri and bring her to trial. What they don't know is that Savan and her thugs are there to capture Guri for themselves.
title: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution #5

synopsis: The Official Companion to the Novel Shadows of the Empire After his defeat on Cloud City, Luke Skywalker finds himself a pawn in a deadly game between Darth Vader and the mysterious and dangerous leader of the criminal organization Black Sun. Confronted by bounty hunters and his own worst fears, Luke must must [sic] lead a daring rescue mission to Emperor Palpatine's throne world of Coruscant, where he will confront the foes who lurk in the Shadows of the Empire…
title: Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook

synopsis: Emperor Palpatine's twenty-year reign of terror came to an abrupt and fiery end in the skies above the forest moon of Endor. A decisive victory for the Rebel Alliance, to be sure, but even with the loss of its leadership, the Empire's Moffs and regional governors retained their hold on important systems from the Core to the Outer Rim, thanks to the might of the Imperial Starfleet. Now, with a power vacuum atop the Empire, those Moffs will jockey for position and control, and the heroes of the Rebel Alliance will soon discover that a wounded and fractious Empire may be more dangerous than any t
title: Shattered Empire

synopsis: For the first time in the new Star Wars canon, journey with us into the time after the end of Return of the Jedi! Writer Greg Rucka (Punisher, Wolverine, Gotham Central) and artist Marco Checchetto (Avengers World, Punisher) take us past the destruction of the second Death Star into the chaos of a Shattered Empire.
title: Shattered Empire #1

synopsis: For the first time in the new Star Wars canon, journey with us into the time after the end of Return of the Jedi! Writer Greg Rucka (Punisher, Wolverine, Gotham Central) and artist Marco Checchetto (Avengers World, Punisher) take us past the destruction of the second Death Star into the chaos of a Shattered Empire.
title: Shattered Empire #2

synopsis: Concluding the first new-canon comic tale set between episodes VI and VII! As the Empire Shatters around them, Rebel heroes fight for a better tomorrow! Greg Rucka (Punisher, Cyclops, Wolverine) and Marco Checchetto (Punisher, Avengers World) take us into the more recent long, long time ago!
title: Shattered Empire #3

synopsis: Concluding the first new-canon comic tale set between episodes VI and VII! As the Empire Shatters around them, Rebel heroes fight for a better tomorrow! Greg Rucka (Punisher, Cyclops, Wolverine) and Marco Checchetto (Punisher, Avengers World) take us into the more recent long, long time ago!
title: Shattered Empire #4

synopsis: Jedi Master Mace Windu must return to his home planet to save the Republic from further devastation.
title: Shatterpoint

synopsis: The New Republic's triumph grows ever more precarious in the face of an arrogant race bent on war, as the bestselling Star Wars saga continues.... As Leia must deal with a new threat to the fragile alliance that binds the New Republic, Lando becomes a prisoner aboard a runaway spacecraft of unknown origin. Luke continues his quest to learn more about his mother among the Fallanassi, where his every belief about the use of the Force is about to be challenged. And while Leia ponders a diplomatic solution to the aggression of the fierce Yevetha race, Han pilots a spy ship into the heart of Yeveth
title: Shield of Lies

synopsis: Young children will find that outer space is no farther than their fingertips when they stroke a serpent's scaly stomach, touch a strange hairy plant, scratch and sniff an alien fungus—and more—while helping Artoo-Detoo and See-Threepio search for the crystal they need to fix their spacecraft.
title: Shiny as a Droid

synopsis: When a loyalist ship approaching Selonia is blasted out of space, Han Solo quickly realizes that rebel forces are turning planetary repulsors into weapons of immense destruction. Meanwhile, Luke Skywalker and Lando Calrissian manage to discover the terrifying secret of Centerpoint Station. It is the infamous Starbuster itself, and its next programmed nova, hard-wired in, is set to annihilate a chosen star, its inhabited worlds, and millions of innocent lives. There's only one way to stop it: blow up the station. But as a massive rebel fleet closes in on Centerpoint, Han, Luke and their friends
title: Showdown at Centerpoint

synopsis: A personal datalog by the Selkath Shuuru (~3637 BBY) providing lore tied to SWTOR's Manaan content, a companion to the "Since KOTOR: Manaan" history piece, recounting Manaan after the events of KOTOR.
title: Shuuru: Personal Datalog

synopsis: The novella "Side Trip" was the first collaboration between Timothy Zahn and Michael A. Stackpole. The novella consists of four parts, parts one and four being written by Zahn and parts two and three written by Stackpole.
title: Side Trip

synopsis: "Silver and Scarlet" is a short story by James S. A. Corey released in the novel Honor Among Thieves and Star Wars Insider 148. The audio version is included with the Honor Among Thieves audiobook narrated by Ilyana Kadushin.
title: Silver and Scarlet

synopsis: "Star Wars: Skeleton Crew" follows the journey of four kids who make a mysterious discovery on their seemingly safe home planet, then get lost in a strange and dangerous galaxy. Finding their way home, and meeting unlikely allies and enemies, will be a greater adventure than they ever imagined.
title: Skeleton Crew

displayTitle: This Could Be a Real Adventure
synopsis: Kids from an ordinary planet discover a surprising secret.
title: This Could Be a Real Adventure

displayTitle: Way, Way Out Past the Barrier
synopsis: A surprising first stop marks the beginning of an unexpected journey.
title: Way, Way Out Past the Barrier

displayTitle: Very Interesting, as an Astrogation Problem
synopsis: A mysterious stranger offers to help our heroes.
title: Very Interesting, as an Astrogation Problem

displayTitle: Can't Say I Remember No At Attin
synopsis: Our heroes face a surprise in a familiar setting.
title: Can't Say I Remember No At Attin

displayTitle: You Have a Lot to Learn About Pirates
synopsis: A seemingly pleasant world will test our heroes like no other.
title: You Have a Lot to Learn About Pirates

synopsis: Relationships fray under the pressures of a difficult journey.
title: Zero Friends Again

displayTitle: We're Gonna Be in So Much Trouble
synopsis: Our heroes have never felt further from home.
title: We're Gonna Be in So Much Trouble

synopsis: A journey filled with adventure comes to a surprising end.
title: The Real Good Guys

synopsis: Star Wars: Skeleton Crew follows the journey of four kids who make a mysterious discovery on their seemingly safe home planet, then get lost in a strange and dangerous galaxy. Finding their way home, and meeting unlikely allies and enemies, will be a greater adventure than they ever imagined.
title: Skeleton Crew Season 1

displayTitle: Adventures: Luke Skywalker And The Treasure Of The Dragonsnake
synopsis: The movie The Empire Strikes Back shows us only a small portion of Luke Skywalker's Jedi training under the tutelage of Master Yoda. In this story, witness a never-before-seen part of that training as Luke is given the task of finding -- and retrieving -- an object guarded by the deadliest creatures on the swamp world of Dagobah: the monstrous dragonsnakes! This is the most harrowing, dangerous, and muddiest mission that Luke has ever faced!
title: Star Wars Adventures: Luke Skywalker And The Treasure Of The Dragonsnake

synopsis: He's both feared and admired, respected and despised. Boba Fett is the galaxy's most successful bounty hunter. Now he finds himself hunted in the oldest game of all: survival of the fittest. The once powerful Bounty Hunter's Guild has been shattered into warring factions. Now the posting of an enormous bounty on a renegade Imperial stormtrooper is about to start a frenzy of murderous greed. Hoping to fuel rumors of his death, Boba Fett abandons his ship, Slave I, and sets out to claim the prize. Yet his every move leads him closer to a trap set by the cunning Prince Xizor. Fett will die before
title: Slave Ship

displayTitle: Smuggler's Run: A Han Solo and Chewbacca Adventure
synopsis: Flying through space on a secret mission for the Rebel Alliance, Han Solo and Chewbacca push the Millennium Falcon to its limits to outrun the enemies pursuing them at every turn.
title: Smuggler's Run: A Han Solo and Chewbacca Adventure

synopsis: "The Old Republic: Smuggler's Vanguard" is a short story written by Robert Chestney that was published exclusively on StarWars.com on March 25, 2010. On October 1, 2012, it was republished on the Del Rey Website. The story is set in 3660 BBY during the Great Galactic War, and focuses on the Mirialan smuggler Hylo Visz, tying into the Star Wars: The Old Republic video game.
title: Smuggler's Vanguard

synopsis:
**Featuring Backstory and Scenes Not Seen in Theaters!**

After leaving the Imperial Navy, a young Han Solo seeks adventure by joining a gang of galactic mercenaries, including a 196-year-old Wookiee named Chewbacca and a notorious gambler named Lando Calrissian. But there's more to the story of the galaxy's most beloved scoundrel than what you saw in theaters. Why does Beckett trust him? And what is the connection between the woman who stole Han's heart and one of Han's closest friends?
title: Solo (comics)

synopsis: Through a series of daring escapades deep within a dark and dangerous criminal underworld, Han Solo meets his mighty future copilot Chewbacca and encounters the notorious gambler Lando Calrissian.
title: Solo

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation
synopsis: **Featuring Backstory and Scenes Not Seen in Theaters!** After leaving the Imperial Navy, a young Han Solo seeks adventure by joining a gang of galactic mercenaries, including a 190-year-old [sic] Wookiee named Chewbacca and a notorious gambler named Lando Calrissian. But there's more to the story of the galaxy's most beloved scoundrel than what you saw in theaters. Why does Beckett trust him? And what is the connection between the woman who stole Han's heart and one of Han's closest friends?
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation

title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #1

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #2
synopsis:
**The Great Train Robbery!**

Han Solo and Chewbacca throw down but ultimately unite to escape the Empire and join Beckett's crew! But will this ragtag group of thieves be able to pull off the train heist of their lives? And what happens when Enfys Nest arrives to take the stolen coaxium out from under them? Featuring scenes not seen in theaters!
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #2

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #2
synopsis: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation is a comic-book miniseries adapting the movie of the same name. It was written by Robbie Thompson and illustrated by Will Sliney. Marvel Comics published its first issue on October 10, 2018.

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #3
synopsis:

Qi'ra is alive! But she's working for the ruthless gangster Dryden Vos, who forces Han, Beckett and Qi'ra into an impossible heist. But where will this trio find the fastest ship in the galaxy to make the dreaded Kessel Run? Enter Lando Calrissian and the Millenium Falcon!
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #3

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #5
synopsis:
**The Kessel Run!**

Han and his crew pulled off an impossible heist, but will the Empire stop them before their stolen unrefined coaxium explodes? Or will a monster waiting in the Maw prevent them making the Kessel Run in under twelve parsecs?!
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #5

Enfys Nest has Han Solo and his crew dead to rights. But will Enfys' surprising revelation persuade Han to change his plans for the stolen coaxium, or will a betrayal in his crew be his undoing?!
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #6

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #7
synopsis:
**The Final Showdown!**

Han Solo is out of options and out of allies, and Dryden wants him dead. Will Beckett's double-cross be Han's undoing? Will Han do the right thing despite that it could cost him and Chewbacca their lives? Find out in the thrilling conclusion to Solo: A Star Wars Story, featuring material not seen in the film!
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #7

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story Adaptation #7

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story: Expanded Edition
synopsis: Han Solo’s daring escape from Corellia also separated him from the love of his life. Years later, he finds himself tangled in a complicated deal with Crimson Dawn, just as he and Qi’ra reunite under unexpected circumstances.
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story: Expanded Edition

displayTitle: Solo: A Star Wars Story (Junior Novel)
synopsis:
A junior novelization of the film Solo: A Star Wars Story
Board the Millennium Falcon for a galactic adventure that explores the story of one of Star Wars' greatest heroes! Han Solo leaves his homeworld Corellia behind and dreams of becoming the greatest pilot in the galaxy. But before he can find the perfect ship he'll have to survive a dangerous job in the criminal underworld! Read along as Han encounters Chewbacca the Wookiee, smuggler Lando Calrissian and other heroes and villains. Author Joe Schreiber captures all the excitement and adventure of the hit film! Features original and deleted scenes that are exclusive to this edition including a comical chase scene on Kessel and more background on Chewbacca! An eight page full-color photo insert helps readers relive their favorite moments from the film.
title: Solo: A Star Wars Story (Junior Novel)

synopsis: Board the Millennium Falcon and journey to a galaxy far, far away with the most beloved scoundrel in the galaxy in this thrilling novelization of Solo: A Star Wars Story.
title: Solo

synopsis: Learn about the smugglers, scoundrels, gangsters, pirates, and plunderers from Solo: A Star Wars Story (including Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Lando Calrissian) in this unique in-world journal straight from Fort Ypso on the snowy mountain planet of Vandor.
title: Solo: Tales From Vandor

synopsis: The story of Han and Leia’s wedding told from the perspective of two people in love despite all odds.
title: Someone Who Loves You

displayTitle: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 1
synopsis: Getting cut in half by Obi-Wan Kenobi and being rejected by his former Sithmaster Darth Sidious isn't going to defeat Darth Maul. In fact, it only makes him mad enough to take on the galaxy, with an army of Mandalorians!
title: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 1

displayTitle: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 2
synopsis: With his Shadow Collective army stalled, Darth Maul calls on Mother Talzin for help and is sent reinforcements-the deadly Nightbrothers! Maul is ready again to take on the Separatist droid army, Count Dooku, and General Grievous, in a trio of simultaneous battles!
title: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 2

displayTitle: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 3
synopsis: Darth Maul has captured Darth Sidious's new apprentice! Mother Talzinand Maul attempt to sway Count Dooku to their cause, revealing surprising facts about Maul's past. But an attack by an elite Jedi strike force throws everyone's plans into chaos!
title: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 3

displayTitle: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 4
synopsis: Count Dooku is possessed by the spirit of Mother Talzin! Darth Maul's Shadow Collective is shattered! The Separatists attack! And Darth Sidious himself enters the fight!
title: Darth Maul: Son of Dathomir, Part 4

synopsis:
"Sons of the Sky" (German title "Himmelssöhne") is a comic strip written by Jeremy Barlow and drawn by Bob Molesworth. The story was published in the 33rd issue of the German Star Wars Rebels Magazine, which was released on July 5, 2017.
It has never received an official English translation.
title: Sons Of The Sky

synopsis: Spark of Rebellion: A Star Wars Rebels Cinestory Comic brings the characters and story you know and love into comic book form, featuring the early beginnings of the adventures featuring Ezra Bridger, Kanan Jarrus, and the remaining crew of The Ghost.
title: Rebels: Spark Of Rebellion (cinestory comic)

synopsis: Rey insists that she and her fellow Resistance fighters answer a distress call, despite direct orders to return from their previous mission.
title: Spark of the Resistance

synopsis: "Speaking Silently" is a Clone Wars short story written by Jason Fry with art by Tom Hodges. Originally planned as part of The Essential Guide to Warfare, it was cut before publication and reworked to be published in January 2013's Star Wars Insider 139.
title: Speaking Silently

synopsis: Nine years after Thrawn's death, the New Republic is fracturing and a shadowy plot threatens to convince the galaxy that the Grand Admiral has returned.
title: Specter of the Past

synopsis: "Spices & Spies" is a short comic that was published in Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.15.
title: Spices & Spies

synopsis: Deep in the swamplands of the planet Mimban lies a powerful crystal, capable of giving its possessor an invincible mastery of the Force. In the right hands, it could save the galaxy. But in the wrong hands, it could destroy it. While the explosion of the first Death Star still reverberates through the cosmos, Luke Skywalker finds that the war he thought he won was only a battle, and that the dark side of the Force still stalks the galaxy.
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye #1

synopsis: Imprisoned by the Imperial forces, confronted by two very violent, very massive creatures known as Yuzzem, Luke and Leia have only one hope of escape: the Mimban Crystal, capable of giving its wielder total, omnipotent mastery of the Force. But Luke's use of the crystal could draw the unwelcome attention of certain … dark powers.
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye #2

synopsis: Even as Darth Vader and his Imperial garrison approach Mimban from the air, Luke and Leia find that their biggest problems lie under the planet's crust. For deep in the soil lives Mimban's deadliest predator—perhaps the galaxy's deadliest predator—the gigantic, wormlike wandrella; and if the humongous wandrella can't catch them, the subterranean race of aboriginals—the Coway—just might!
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye #3

synopsis: Mimban is like a million other planets in the galaxy—a lot of trees, too much mud, and not much else. But one of Mimban's abandoned temples holds a powerful gem—the Kaiburr crystal—capable of magnifying and clarifying one's perception of the Force. Luke and Leia know it's there—somewhere—and so does Darth Vader. And that makes Mimban a whole lot livelier right now than those other planets!
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye #4

synopsis:
As a young but already seasoned fighter pilot, Luke Skywalker expected trouble when he volunteered to accompany Princess Leia on a diplomatic mission to Circarpous to enlist their Rebel Underground in the battle against the Empire. But the former farm boy from Tatooine hadn't counted on an unscheduled crash-landing in the swamplands of Mimban…a landing that destroyed their ships. Nor had he counted on any of the things they would find on that strange planet.
First, unknown to anyone, a secret base of Imperial Stormtroopers was thriving on Mimban. As if that weren't trouble enough, there was the Kaiburr crystal, a mysterious gem that would give the one who possessed it such powers over the Force that he would be all but invincible. In the wrong hands, the crystal could be deadly.
So Luke had to find the Kaiburr crystal, and find it fast.
Accompanied by Artoo Detoo and See Threepio—his two faithful 'droids—Luke and the Princess set out for the Temple of Pomojema and for a desperate confrontation deep beneath the surface of an alien world with the most fearsome villain in the galaxy!
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye

synopsis: As a young but already seasoned fighter pilot, Luke Skywalker expected trouble when he volunteered to accompany Princess Leia on a diplomatic mission to Circarpous to enlist their Rebel Underground in the battle against the Empire. But the former farm boy from Tatooine hadn't counted on an unscheduled crash-landing in the swamplands of Mimban…a landing that destroyed their ships.
title: Splinter of the Mind's Eye

synopsis: Master the art of starfighter combat in the authentic piloting experience Star Wars: Squadrons. Feel the adrenaline of first-person multiplayer space dogfights alongside your squadron, and buckle up in a thrilling Star Wars story.
title: Squadrons

synopsis: "Count to Three" is a short story written by Joanna Berry featuring the characters Shen and Havina Vonreg from Star Wars: Squadrons. It was released on October 13, 2020.
title: Squadrons: Count To Three

synopsis: "Hunted" is a CG short produced by Industrial Light & Magic featuring the character Varko Grey, who appears in the video game Star Wars: Squadrons.
title: Squadrons: Hunted

synopsis: "The Light You Bring" is a short story written by Joanna Berry and set during the campaign of Star Wars: Squadrons. It was released on October 8, 2020 and features a mission by Vanguard Squadron.
title: Squadrons: The Light You Bring

synopsis: The New York Times bestselling Star Wars series The New Jedi Order enthralls readers with its epic drama and thrilling adventure. Now readers will pierce the very heart of darkness, as those fascinating figures in that galaxy far, far away—Han Solo, Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa Solo, as well as their children—spring to vivid life to battle their deadliest adversaries. It is a solemn time for the New Republic, as the merciless Yuuzhan Vong continue their ruthless campaign of terror and destruction. The brutal enemy has unleashed a savage creature capable of finding—and killing&
title: Star by Star

synopsis:
**An Epic New Adventure Begins for Luke, Leia and Han in the Wake of Return of the Jedi!**

New York Times best-selling author Alex Segura launches the bold next era of Star Wars. Luke Skywalker must defend the New Republic from a bloodthirsty gang of mercenaries! Han Solo investigates a deadly underworld mystery, and saves a surprising ally! Leia Organa must grapple with a new alliance opposed to the goals of the New Republic!
title: Star Wars (2025)

synopsis:
**The Greatest Space Adventure of All Time Returns to Marvel!**

Luke Skywalker and the ragtag band of rebels fighting against the Galactic Empire are fresh off their biggest victory yet, the destruction of the massive battle station known as the Death Star. But the Empire's not toppled yet! Join Luke along with Princess Leia, smugglers Han Solo and Chewbacca, droids C-3PO and R2-D2 and the rest of the Rebel Alliance as they strike out for freedom against the evil forces of Darth Vader and his master, the Emperor. Written by Jason Aaron (Original Sin, Thor: God of Thunder) and with art by John Cassaday (Astonishing X-Men, Uncanny Avengers), this is the Star Wars saga as only Marvel Comics could make it!
title: Star Wars (2015) #01: Skywalker Strikes

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #13: Vader Down, Part III
synopsis: Two of the biggest titles in comics collide in the first crossover of the new Marvel age of Star Wars! When Darth Vader accidentally finds himself facing off against the Rebel Fleet on his own, he is sent crashing onto a nearby planet. Will the Rebels seize this opportunity to put an end to one of their greatest enemies - or will they be made to feel the full power of the Dark Side? The Sith Lord may be down, he isn't out!
title: Star Wars (2015) #13: Vader Down, Part III

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #14: Vader Down, Part V
synopsis: Two of the biggest titles in comics collide in the first crossover of the new Marvel age of Star Wars! Will the Rebels seize this opportunity to put an end to one of their greatest enemies - or will they be made to feel the full power of the Dark Side? The Sith Lord may be down, he isn't out!
title: Star Wars (2015) #14: Vader Down, Part V

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #16: Rebel Jail, Part I
synopsis: What comes next for the rebels after the events of Vader Down? Find out as the adventures of Luke Skywalker and friends continue! The Rebel crew are en route to a prison base with a very important captive. But, unlucky for them, they aren't the only ones with their eyes on this prize! Plus: Meet a rebel spy at the heart of the Empire, and join him on a mission of life and death!
title: Star Wars (2015) #16: Rebel Jail, Part I

synopsis: The first ongoing comic series set in the *Star Wars* galaxy, running from 1977 to 1986 across 107 issues plus three annuals. Issues 1-6 adapt the events of *A New Hope*, and issues 39-44 adapt *The Empire Strikes Back*; the *Return of the Jedi* adaptation was published as a separate four-issue miniseries outside the regular run. Between the film adaptations, the series told original adventures of Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, Princess Leia, Chewbacca, and the droids as the Rebel Alliance carried on its war against the Galactic Empire. Many issues also included a reader letter column titled *Star-Words*.
title: Star Wars (1977)

synopsis: It's the adaptation of A New Hope! Meet Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia, Obi-Wan and the villianous [sic] Darth Vader!
title: Star Wars (1977) #01

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #10: Behemoth from the World Below
synopsis: The Behemoth, a giant reptilian monster, is on the rampage and Han Slo [sic] may be a small village's only hope.
title: Star Wars (1977) #10: Behemoth from the World Below

synopsis: The Nagai invade the Alliance HQ on Endor! A computer has grounded the best Alliance pilots—Han and Luke—and Princess Leia must face the brunt of the attack alone! "First Strike" is a double-sized anniversary spectacular, written by Jo Duffy and penciled by Cynthia Martin.
title: Star Wars (1977) #100: First Strike

synopsis: Han's relationship with Leia is on the rocks—and that's not all! Han's ready to leave—but more than just jumping ship, he's going to take off for a whole different universe! And because you demanded it: the return of Femm Syhsa! [sic] "Far, Far Away" is written by Jo Duffy and penciled by Cynthia Martin. His relationship with Leia is not all that's on the rocks for Han Solo. This time it looks like he's not only going to jump ship, he's going to take off for a whole different universe! "Far, Far Away" written by Jo Duffy and penciled by Cynthia Martin.
title: Star Wars (1977) #101: Far, Far Away

synopsis: Luke and Leia return to Iskalon to tell Kiro's family of his death. Waiting for them... ruthless Nagai invaders. And Iskalon's staunchest defender is... Kiro himself!
title: Star Wars (1977) #102: School Spirit

synopsis: Leia alone on a world full of hostile aliens... and the only people she has to help her are four teenagers—and one badly wounded enemy! "TAI" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Cynthia Martin and inked by Art Nichols.
title: Star Wars (1977) #103: Tai

synopsis: Luke, Leia, Han and their friends fly to Zeltros to enlist their aid in the war against the Nagai, only to have the war follow them! "Nagais and Dolls" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Cynthia Martin and inked by Art Nichols.
title: Star Wars (1977) #104: Nagais and Dolls

synopsis: Who has captured whom on Zeltros? That's what everyone wants to know when three different civilizations invade that world! It's up to Luke Skywalker and the Hoojibs to save the day. If that weren't enough, Luke meets the villainous Toffs [sic] for the first time! "The Party's Over" is written by Jo Duffy and drawn by Cynthia Martin.
title: Star Wars (1977) #105: The Party's Over

synopsis: The planet Zeltros has been conquered by two invading armies! The Zeltrons' only hope now is Luke Skywalker, the telepathic Space Bunnies and a dozen cowardly cockroaches! "My Hiromi" is written by Jo Duffy and drawn by Cynthia Martin.
title: Star Wars (1977) #106: My Hiromi

synopsis:
Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, Han Solo, and Dani, together with a mixed team of commandos and their new Imperial and Nagai allies Knife and Den Siva, attack the Tof-held world of Saijo, site of the Tof field headquarters. The Nagai have revealed that the Tof prince Sereno, leader of the invasion, is there, and the team hopes to take him hostage and force a Tof surrender.

Infiltrating the Tof administrative palace, the men posing as prisoners and Dani and Leia disguised as veiled Tof ladies, they find not only Prince Sereno but also the Dark Lady Lumiya, who has thrown in with the Tof. As the attack begins, Tof fighters called up from the bulkship *Merriweather* are intercepted by Rogue Squadron under Wedge Antilles and by the *Millennium Falcon*, flown by Lando Calrissian and Chewbacca. Knife is wounded by Lumiya, and Leia's intervention draws Lumiya's fire before Leia is herself shot in the back by a Tof officer and left for dead, though she survives. Luke captures the reluctant Sereno, and the Tof turncoat aiding them is revealed to be Bey, long working undercover. As the fighting ends, Luke voices hope that the many factions and species allied against the Tof might one day coexist in peace.
title: Star Wars (1977) #107: All Together Now

synopsis: The all-new next issue of the original Marvel *Star Wars* series. A long time ago on a spinner rack far, far away, *Star Wars* comics were a lot different than they are now. With wild and intricately plotted storylines, Marvel's historic original run introduced an array of unique heroes and villains to play alongside George Lucas's incredibly popular science-fantasy characters. This Legends-era sequel to legendary comic book writer Archie Goodwin's "Crimson Forever" reunites Han Solo, Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia, Chewbacca, C-3PO and R2-D2 with Jaxxon, Amaiza Foxtrain, Domina Tagge and Valance the Hunter in a galaxy-threatening, nostalgic roller coaster.
title: Star Wars (1977) #108: Forever Crimson

synopsis: Han and Chewie are captured by space pirates while Luke takes on a monstrous Sea-Dragon!
title: Star Wars (1977) #11: Star Search!

synopsis: Luke is stranded on the water world Drexel and finds himself caught between two opposing forces each looking to possess his ship.
title: Star Wars (1977) #12: Doomworld!

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #13: Day of the Dragon Lords!
synopsis: On the water world of Drexel, Luke Skywalker decides to join Governor Quarg's group in their battle against the Dragon Lords, at least for now. Aboard Crimson Jack's space cruiser, Han Solo is talking his way out of trouble when Jack and his crew realize they have been duped into coming to Drexel. Quarg's surface jamming equipment disrupts the cruiser, giving Solo, Chewbacca, and Princess Leia Organa the chance to flee in the *Millennium Falcon*. The *Falcon* splashes down amid an epic battle between Quarg's hydra-craft pilots and the Dragon Lords, and one hydra craft attacks the ship just as its occupants climb out. Luke, piloting a hydra craft himself, turns on his new comrades to save the fallen Rebels. He finds only Chewbacca, who, believing Luke responsible for the attack, is enraged at the young Rebel.
title: Star Wars (1977) #13: Day of the Dragon Lords!

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #14: The Sound of Armageddon
synopsis: Space pirates are using a sonic jammer to take down any and all ships, whether they're Rebel or Imperial!
title: Star Wars (1977) #14: The Sound of Armageddon

synopsis: When the Millenium Falcon [sic] is disabled, Han will have to outduel space pirates to survive!
title: Star Wars (1977) #15: Star Duel

synopsis: When a nefarious bounter hunter [sic] mistakes another teenager for Skywalker, a battle ensues!
title: Star Wars (1977) #16: The Hunter

synopsis: While piloting the *Millennium Falcon* through space, Luke Skywalker's mind flashes back to his life on Tatooine. Luke, Biggs Darklighter, Deak, and other youngsters race their T-16 skyhoppers through the twisting maze of Beggar's Canyon when a wounded scout arrives to warn of an impending Sand People attack. While the others try to hold off the raiders, Luke and Biggs rush to warn the area's other residents. Biggs is wounded by a Tusken Raider, and Luke must fly him through the dangerous caverns of Diablo Cut in his skyhopper to get him to safety.
title: Star Wars (1977) #17: Crucible

synopsis: What has caused Luke to fall into a coma?
title: Star Wars (1977) #18: The Empire Strikes

synopsis: The crew of the *Millennium Falcon* has split up aboard the gambler's paradise known as The Wheel. While the droids C-3PO and R2-D2 take the unconscious Luke Skywalker to the medical center, the Wheel's administrator, Senator Simon Greyshade, arranges to have Princess Leia brought to his quarters. Chewbacca, having reached the Crimson Casino on the Wheel's upper level, is captured by the Wheel Security Force and forced into The Big Game. Han Solo is entered into the same gladiatorial contest, not knowing Chewbacca is also a contestant. It has all been arranged by Greyshade, who is cutting a deal with Imperial Commander Zertik Strom to see the Rebels killed, on the condition that Leia be allowed to remain with him on the Wheel.
title: Star Wars (1977) #19: The Ultimate Gamble

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #02: Six Against the Galaxy
synopsis: Part two of the six-part adaptation of *A New Hope*. Luke Skywalker is rescued from an attacking band of Sand People by Obi-Wan Kenobi. Once Luke, Obi-Wan, and the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO are safely inside Kenobi's hut, R2-D2 plays a recorded message from Princess Leia Organa asking Kenobi to bring the droid and its critical information to Alderaan. Luke at first refuses to go, citing his obligations at home, but on discovering that his farm has been destroyed and his aunt Beru and uncle Owen killed by Imperial stormtroopers, he agrees. They hire Han Solo and Chewbacca to fly them to Alderaan aboard the *Millennium Falcon*.
title: Star Wars (1977) #02: Six Against the Galaxy

synopsis: As part of a plot to discredit the Rebel Alliance, Senator Simon Greyshade stages a scene in which a pleasure yacht is attacked by an X-wing only to be repelled by Imperial TIE fighters. The footage is broadcast into the casinos on The Wheel, where patrons watch in horror at the apparent viciousness of the Rebels. Greyshade's scheme also calls for Han Solo and Chewbacca to each be killed while competing in separate events of the Big Game gladiator contest, but Solo confounds him by defeating his powerful opponent. Meanwhile, the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO have been seized by The Wheel and are scheduled to have their memories wiped when Master-Com rescues them. The three droids go to the medical center to check on Luke Skywalker, only to find that he has not only regained consciousness but has already escaped.
title: Star Wars (1977) #20: Deathgame

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #21: Shadow of a Dark Lord
synopsis: Princess Leia escapes from Simon Greyshade's suite, where she was held captive, and finds Luke Skywalker, now recovered from his trance-like state. Luke reveals that his trance was caused by briefly touching another mind through the Force, someone strong in the Force whom he suspects to be Darth Vader. Meanwhile, Greyshade meets with his new partner Commander Zertik Strom, The Wheel's administrator, who has secretly sent his security force to subdue Strom's troops and recover the money they stole. As Han Solo prepares to enter the main event of the Big Game, he realizes that Chewbacca is also entered, and the rules allow only one survivor. Far away on the planet Ultaar, Darth Vader finds a Rebel information-retrieval team wiped out by the bounty hunter Beilert Valance, and realizes that Valance is hunting the same prize he is: the boy who destroyed the Death Star.
title: Star Wars (1977) #21: Shadow of a Dark Lord

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #22: To the Last Gladiator
synopsis: Han Solo and Chewbacca are pitted against many other gladiators in the main event of the Big Game, and the two team up to stay alive, knowing that under the rules only one of them can leave the arena. Simon Greyshade, who has orchestrated events on The Wheel, offers Princess Leia a proposition: escape with him and his stolen treasure, and her Rebel friends will go free. As she weighs the decision, she watches the contest and sees Han apparently slain by his own Wookiee companion.
title: Star Wars (1977) #22: To the Last Gladiator

synopsis: As Simon Greyshade and Princess Leia prepare to leave The Wheel, Commander Zertik Strom, realizing he has been betrayed, sends his stormtroopers to stop them. Tired of being upstaged by Luke Skywalker's noble actions, Greyshade decides to let Luke and Leia leave with the droids aboard his ship while he stays behind to cover their escape. Strom confronts the wounded Greyshade seeking revenge, but Greyshade tosses a proton grenade that kills Strom while fatally wounding himself and crippling his droid Master-Com. Meanwhile, Chewbacca leaves the Big Game with Han Solo, who reveals that his apparent death was a ruse and he is unharmed. The two reach the *Millennium Falcon* and flee The Wheel just as they confront Darth Vader's battlecruiser. Piloting the space yacht, Luke stuns Vader through the Force just long enough for both Rebel craft to escape into hyperspace.
title: Star Wars (1977) #23: Flight Into Fury

synopsis: After Han Solo skillfully pilots the *Millennium Falcon* to destroy two TIE fighters, Leia remarks that Jedi pilots developed that same maneuver, and recounts a story about Obi-Wan Kenobi. Traveling as a passenger aboard a pleasure cruiser, Kenobi is told by Captain Quasar that Merson pirates have been spotted in the Merson asteroid belt. The attacking ships are quickly destroyed, but Obi-Wan suspects the Mersons were lured by a signal from within the cruiser. The other passengers accuse the known criminal Augustus Tryll, but Kenobi refuses to judge without proof. When more Merson ships appear, he realizes the signal they are tracking comes from an innocent fermentation device aboard the cruiser. Once he destroys it, the pirates lose the cruiser's trail and the danger passes.
title: Star Wars (1977) #24: Silent Drifting

synopsis: An Imperial blockade may spell doom for the Rebel base.
title: Star Wars (1977) #25: Siege at Yavin

synopsis: The Empire has something devious set up on Yavin and Luke and Leia are on a mission to uncover just what is going on.
title: Star Wars (1977) #26: Doom Mission

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #27: Return of the Hunter
synopsis: Luke is in danger and the only one who can save him is....C3PO [sic]?
title: Star Wars (1977) #27: Return of the Hunter

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #28: What Ever Happened to Jabba the Hut?
synopsis: Jabba the Hut is looking to get his payment from Han and Chewie, through deadly force if necessary.
title: Star Wars (1977) #28: What Ever Happened to Jabba the Hut?

synopsis: Darth Vader learns that Tyler Lucian, a Rebel deserter, knows the name of the pilot who destroyed the Death Star. The bounty hunter Beilert Valance is on the same trail but for the opposite reason: he wants to prevent Vader from ever learning it. The two confront each other near Lucian's hideout at Rubyflame Lake, and a fierce battle erupts between the two cyborgs. Vader prevails when Valance falls into the corrosive lake waters, allowing the dark lord to press on toward the hideout. But rather than wait for Vader, Lucian throws himself into Rubyflame Lake, killing himself and thwarting Vader's plans.
title: Star Wars (1977) #29: Dark Encounter

synopsis: The Millennium Falcon is captured by the Death Star, which has just destroyed another planet!
title: Star Wars (1977) #03: Death Star!

synopsis: Princess Leia Organa travels to the factory planet Metalorn on a mission to spread a message of hope to its residents. The world is completely controlled by the Galactic Empire under Governor Corwyth, with no word of the Rebellion reaching its citizens. Leia evades Imperial stormtroopers long enough to contact her former instructor Arn Horada, hoping he will spread word that the Rebel Alliance is actively opposing the Empire's tyranny. She is soon confronted by Baron Orman Tagge before she can establish any details, but manages to escape with the help of a youngster named Tammi.
title: Star Wars (1977) #30: A Princess Alone

synopsis: Luke Skywalker returns to Tatooine for the first time since leaving with Obi-Wan Kenobi, tasked with recruiting blockade-runner pilots to help the Rebel Alliance evacuate its base on Yavin 4. While there he stumbles on clues to a secret scheme of the House of Tagge working with the Empire, though not enough to reveal what they are planning. He continues on to Mos Eisley and reunites with Han Solo and Chewbacca in the very cantina where they first met months earlier. The reunion is cut short when Fixer arrives to warn Luke that the Empire knows he is on Tatooine and is actively hunting him.
title: Star Wars (1977) #31: Return to Tatooine

synopsis: Han Solo, Luke Skywalker, Chewbacca, and the droids are stranded in the Dune Sea of Tatooine after fleeing Imperial stormtroopers out of Mos Eisley. They strike a deal to travel with a band of Jawas in their sandcrawler, and soon uncover further evidence of the House of Tagge working with the Empire to build a long-range freezing mechanism, though the purpose of the technology remains a mystery. The Rebels evade their Imperial pursuers and head back toward Mos Eisley.
title: Star Wars (1977) #32: The Jawa Express

synopsis: As the *Millennium Falcon* and her crew depart Tatooine, they spot a House of Tagge Mining Explorer preparing to jump to hyperspace. The Rebels follow it to Feriae Junction, where it rendezvous with an Imperial battlecruiser and the Tagges transfer their mysterious Omega Frost technology to the Imperial craft, luring Luke Skywalker into a trap. With Luke captured, Han pilots the *Falcon* to Yavin 4 to warn the Alliance that the Empire intends to use the Omega Frost weapon against the Junction, a critical supply source. Aboard the Mining Explorer, Luke duels Baron Orman Tagge; the baron toys with him at first but soon realizes the youth is more capable than expected. Luke ultimately defeats Tagge by destroying his cyber-vision and leaving him helpless, then races back to the base to reveal the weapon's true target, since the warning Han carried was deliberate misinformation.
title: Star Wars (1977) #33: Saber Clash

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #34: Thunder in the Stars
synopsis: After his defeat in a lightsaber duel with Luke Skywalker, Baron Orman Tagge lies sedated aboard his brother Silas Tagge's ship. Han Solo leads the Rebel Fleet toward Feriae Junction believing the *Omega Frost* weapon is about to be unleashed on a supply world, but the target is really the Junction asteroid corridor the fleet must pass through. Luke escapes the Tagges only to have his ship sabotaged, and he abandons it before it crashes. Unable to warn the Rebels of the trap, he finds and destroys one of the towers generating the Omega Frost effect just as the fleet enters the weapon's range. The Rebels then turn on the Tagges' Mining Explorer and destroy it before Silas and Orman can escape.
title: Star Wars (1977) #34: Thunder in the Stars

synopsis: Darth Vader at last learns the name of the boy who destroyed the Death Star and sets a plan in motion for revenge. He enlists General Ulric Tagge and his younger sister Domina Tagge, a member of the neutral Order of the Sacred Circle. Each side of the Galactic Civil War is to send a single envoy to the world of Monastery to present its case: Vader represents the Empire, and Domina arranges for the Rebels to send Luke Skywalker. Leia and Han shadow the mission in the *Millennium Falcon*, monitoring Luke through R2-D2. As Luke worries he is out of his depth in the diplomatic role, Domina reassures him, concealing her true aim of avenging the apparent death of Orman Tagge.
title: Star Wars (1977) #35: Dark Lord's Gambit

synopsis: The *Millennium Falcon* is captured by an Imperial battlecruiser near Monastery, but Leia stages a distraction that lets the ship break free. In the confusion Baron Orman Tagge escapes his life-support capsule and stows away aboard the *Falcon*. On Monastery, Luke prepares to address the elders in the Temple of the Sacred Circle, only for Domina Tagge to reveal that they have already decided against him. When Luke objects, Vader seizes the moment to challenge him to a lightsaber duel, but Domina forbids combat in the temple and instead arranges the fight for the treacherous Crystal Valley. She has orchestrated the duel from the start, manipulating both Luke and Vader in the hope that they will destroy each other and rid her family of two rivals at once.
title: Star Wars (1977) #36: Red Queen Rising

synopsis: Luke Skywalker and Darth Vader enter the deadly Crystal Valley as arranged by Domina Tagge, whose plan is to see both her enemies killed. The *Millennium Falcon* arrives on Baron Orman Tagge's orders, and Orman enters the valley to confront Vader. Vader convinces him that Domina is not the innocent he believes and persuades him to duel Luke in Vader's place, using an illusion to make Luke think he is fighting Vader himself. Luke kills Orman, escapes the Crystal Valley, and is reunited with his Rebel comrades.
title: Star Wars (1977) #37: In Mortal Combat

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #38: Riders in the Void
synopsis:
While transporting medical supplies for the Rebellion, Luke Skywalker and Leia are attacked by an Imperial Star Destroyer. Their damaged ship shakes violently in hyperspace, and when they drop to sublight they find themselves in the starless void beyond their galaxy. The only visible "star" proves to be a mysterious organic vessel that swallows their transport whole. Inside, Luke and Leia are separated and attacked, Leia by tentacles and Luke by a lightsaber-wielding droid that treats the ordeal as a game.

When Luke defeats the droid, the entity controlling it panics on realizing the pair are real and tries to expel them into space.
