But when Luke refuses to release Leia's hand even at the risk of his own life, the entity understands that the two care for one another. It brings them to its control room and reveals its history: it was once a pilot and a mecho-organic warship, the sole survivors of a war whose enemy left behind a plague bomb that killed their entire population. The last survivor fled to the void, and over centuries the pilot and ship fused into one being that played games to stave off loneliness.

The ship, called Ship, agrees to return Luke and Leia home, but drops out of hyperspace into the same Star Destroyer that attacked them. It defends itself with anti-matter pods that annihilate the Destroyer and its TIE fighters. Deciding their galaxy is too much like the one it fled, Ship sends Luke and Leia on their way in their transport.
title: Star Wars (1977) #38: Riders in the Void

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #39: The Empire Strikes Back: Beginning
synopsis: The Rebel Alliance has established a new base on the ice planet Hoth, but an Imperial probe droid discovers the location and transmits the data before it can be destroyed. Luke Skywalker, attacked by a wampa and dragged to its cave, escapes and, with Han Solo's help, returns to Echo Base. Captain Piett relays the probe droid's signal to Darth Vader, who immediately orders the fleet to the Hoth system.
title: Star Wars (1977) #39: The Empire Strikes Back: Beginning

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #04: In Battle with Darth Vader
synopsis: Obi-Wan vs Darth Vader! Plus, can Han and Luke rescue Leia?
title: Star Wars (1977) #04: In Battle with Darth Vader

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #40: The Empire Strikes Back: Battleground Hoth
synopsis: Recovering from his wampa injuries in the Echo Base medical center, Luke shares a tender moment with Leia before Han and Chewbacca arrive; Leia and Han bicker, and she kisses Luke to make Han jealous. Aboard the super Star Destroyer *Executor*, Vader receives the probe droid's data confirming the Rebel base on Hoth and orders the fleet there. Admiral Ozzel brings the fleet out of lightspeed too soon, alerting the Rebels, who raise an energy shield against orbital fire; Vader strangles Ozzel with the Force for the blunder and promotes Piett in his place. The Empire lands AT-AT and AT-ST walkers under General Veers to knock out the shield generators, while Luke leads snowspeeders against the walkers and Bren Derlin commands the ground defense. As Han preps the *Millennium Falcon* to get Leia off-world, Luke's gunner Dak is killed and their speeder crashes, an Imperial walker bearing down to crush it.
title: Star Wars (1977) #40: The Empire Strikes Back: Battleground Hoth

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #41: The Empire Strikes Back: Imperial Pursuit
synopsis: Luke escapes the downed snowspeeder as an AT-AT approaches, fires a harpoon into its underside, climbs up, cuts open a hatch with his lightsaber, and drops in a thermal detonator to destroy the walker. At Echo Base, Darth Vader personally leads snowtroopers through the Rebel headquarters as Han rushes Leia and C-3PO to the *Millennium Falcon* and takes off. Two Star Destroyers give chase, and Han flies between them so closely that they sideswipe one another; he then leads a squad of TIE bombers into a crowded asteroid field. Meanwhile Luke, rather than joining his squadron at the rendezvous, takes R2-D2 and his X-wing to Dagobah, where he lands in a swampy bog and a strange small creature watches him from afar.
title: Star Wars (1977) #41: The Empire Strikes Back: Imperial Pursuit

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #42: The Empire Strikes Back: To Be a Jedi
synopsis:
On Dagobah, Luke watches his X-wing sink into the swamp and sets up camp, only to meet a diminutive green creature who rummages through his gear and offers to lead him to the Jedi Master Yoda. At the creature's dwelling Luke grows impatient, and the gnome reveals himself to be Yoda. Believing Luke too old and reckless, Yoda is persuaded by the voice of Obi-Wan Kenobi to train him, and begins an intense regimen of physical and mental exercises to deepen Luke's use of the Force.

Elsewhere, Han, Chewbacca, Leia, and C-3PO evade Imperial ships through the asteroid field, taking shelter in what turns out to be the mouth of a giant space slug and escaping just in time. Han then flies the *Millennium Falcon* straight at the Star Destroyer *Avenger*, veering off at the last moment to latch onto the back of its hull. Vader confers with Emperor Palpatine, who names Luke Skywalker a new threat; Vader proposes turning him rather than killing him, and the Emperor agrees. When the Imperial fleet dumps its garbage, Han detaches and drifts away with the refuse before jumping to lightspeed. Vader hires bounty hunters to find the *Falcon*, and one of them, Boba Fett, follows Solo in *Slave I* as Han sets course for Bespin and his old friend Lando Calrissian.
title: Star Wars (1977) #42: The Empire Strikes Back: To Be a Jedi

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #43: The Empire Strikes Back: Betrayal at Bespin
synopsis: Han Solo and Leia Organa arrive at Cloud City hoping for refuge with Han's old friend Lando Calrissian, but the Empire has arrived first and takes the Rebels captive. Darth Vader tortures Solo to lure Luke Skywalker to Bespin, then freezes Han in carbonite to test the process before using it on his true prize. On Dagobah, Yoda continues training Luke in the ways of the Force, but the young Jedi has visions of his friends suffering in Cloud City and resolves to rescue them despite Yoda's objections.
title: Star Wars (1977) #43: The Empire Strikes Back: Betrayal at Bespin

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #44: The Empire Strikes Back: Duel a Dark Lord
synopsis:
Adapting the climax of *The Empire Strikes Back*, Luke Skywalker and R2-D2 arrive in Cloud City and search for their friends. Darth Vader hands the carbon-frozen Han Solo over to Boba Fett and alters his bargain with Lando Calrissian, ordering Leia and Chewbacca taken aboard his ship. Guilt-ridden, Lando turns his own men against the stormtroopers, freeing Leia, Chewbacca, and the partially rebuilt C-3PO, but they reach the landing pad too late to stop Fett from leaving with Han. They flee Cloud City aboard the *Millennium Falcon*.

Luke confronts Vader in the carbon-freezing facility, and their duel carries them onto a gantry above an exhaust shaft, where Vader severs Luke's right hand and reveals that he is his father. Refusing to accept it, Luke throws himself into the shaft and is left hanging from a sensor vane beneath the city. He reaches out through the Force to Leia, who orders Lando to turn the *Falcon* around. They recover him and rejoin the Rebel fleet, where Luke receives a cybernetic hand and must reckon with his failure to save Han and the truth of his parentage.
title: Star Wars (1977) #44: The Empire Strikes Back: Duel a Dark Lord

synopsis: Imperial Admiral Damon Krell launches a prototype infiltrator probe droid at a damaged Rebel blockade runner. The probe smashes through the hull, slaughters the crew, and seizes command of the ship's automated systems, intending to pilot it into the Rebel fleet and detonate. Luke Skywalker's X-wing is destroyed by the runner's guns, so he and R2-D2 space-walk aboard and Luke cuts his way inside with his lightsaber. He cannot tamper with the probe without risking its self-destruct, but the droid recognizes him and, under standing orders to bring Skywalker to Darth Vader alive, redirects its course. Luke has R2 activate a single escape pod and sends a warning to the fleet before they eject. The probe droid diverts the runner back to Krell's Star Destroyer, but with the reactor primed for meltdown, the runner collides with the Imperial ship and destroys them both.
title: Star Wars (1977) #45: Death Probe

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #46: The Dreams of Cody Sunn-Childe
synopsis: While Lando Calrissian and Chewbacca race to rescue Han Solo, the *Millennium Falcon* suffers a hyperspace malfunction that drops them into another dimension. There they find a mysterious floating city, the psychic creation of the long-lost Rebel hero Cody Sunn-Childe, who abandoned his violent past after discovering his mystical power and built his City of Dreams as a refuge from the Galactic Civil War. Lando rejects Sunn-Childe's pacifism, insisting violence is sometimes necessary, and repairs the *Falcon* to leave. Before he can depart, an Imperial fleet under Captain Plikk breaks through the dimensional fabric and attacks the city, draining its power in the assault. Though Sunn-Childe could easily destroy the fleet, he holds to his pacifist beliefs and lets himself and his city be destroyed. Lando departs, leaving the Imperial fleet stranded in the other dimension as punishment.
title: Star Wars (1977) #46: The Dreams of Cody Sunn-Childe

synopsis: The Rebel Alliance captures a damaged new-model Imperial warbot on Xeron and brings it to the droid-populated space station known as Kligson's Moon for repair, hoping to study it. Kligson agrees to fix the droid but intends to keep it, so R2-D2 and C-3PO must accompany the warbot to observe. The former Imperial droid trooper Zee-Exthree has his own plans, scheming to cannibalize the warbot to repair his own war droid and lead a revolt against Kligson. Anticipating the treachery, Kligson uses a decoy to lure Zee-Exthree and his followers into the open and destroys them with his own warbot. With the battle over, R2-D2 and C-3PO record the warbot's schematics and return to the Rebels, while Kligson relocates his station to a secret location beyond the Empire's reach.
title: Star Wars (1977) #47: Droid World

synopsis: Darth Vader and Leia face off in a gambit involving X-Wing fighters the Rebellion sorely needs and priceless jewels!
title: Star Wars (1977) #48: The Third Law

synopsis:
Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker, and the droids answer a beacon on a jungle world left by Prince Denid of Velmor, who crash-landed there years earlier in a wreck that killed his betrothed, Loren, and left his friend Jedidiah, once a potential Jedi Knight, addle-brained. Denid must return to Velmor to claim the rite of succession or his Imperial-sympathizing younger brother, Anod, will seize the throne. With Luke disguised as a bounty hunter and Leia posing as Loren, they escort Denid home just in time to interrupt Anod's coronation.

This frustrates Anod, the schemer Zelor, and Imperial Captain Zeta Traal, each pursuing power of their own. At a ceremonial Mrid Hunt, Traal plots to have Denid and Leia killed; in the ensuing battle Anod wounds them but is killed by Traal, and Luke defeats Zelor in a sword duel. Jedidiah sacrifices himself to stop Traal from capturing Luke. With the conspirators dead or captured, Denid takes the throne and pledges support to the Rebellion, and Luke gives Jedidiah a hero's funeral in space.
title: Star Wars (1977) #49: The Last Jedi

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #05: Lo, The Moons of Yavin!
synopsis: With the stolen plans, the Rebels begin their attack on the Death Star.
title: Star Wars (1977) #05: Lo, The Moons of Yavin!

synopsis: What is the Crimson Fever [sic] and why is it so deadly that it forces Luke and the others to stop their pursuit of the captured Han in order to stop it?
title: Star Wars (1977) #50: The Crimson Forever

synopsis: General Rieekan learns from Rebel spies that the Galactic Empire is constructing a new superweapon known as the *Tarkin*. Recognizing the danger, he sends the survivors of the original Death Star, Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, Chewbacca, and the droids C-3PO and R2-D2, to the planet Hockaleg in the Patriim system to infiltrate an Imperial construction crew and get aboard the *Tarkin*. Darth Vader observes the Rebels' arrival and sets a trap to finally capture Luke, unaware that some of his own officers are secretly plotting against him in retaliation for the brutal punishments he has inflicted on their fellow Imperials.
title: Star Wars (1977) #51: Resurrection of Evil

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #52: To Take The Tarkin
synopsis: Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, and Chewbacca, along with C-3PO and R2-D2, have infiltrated the Imperial superweapon *Tarkin* to sabotage it. As they work, Darth Vader arranges a confrontation with Luke while a group of Imperial officers led by General Biel plots to destroy the Sith Lord. The officers' scheme fails, and Vader turns his focus to capturing the Rebels. Having crippled the *Tarkin*'s firing mechanism, the Rebels escape but are set upon by TIE fighters, including one flown by Vader. Lando Calrissian, having stowed away aboard the *Millennium Falcon*, comes to their aid. With Vader's fighter disabled, Biel tries to fire the *Tarkin*'s superlaser at the Rebels in hopes of catching Vader in the blast, but the weapon self-destructs as it fires, killing everyone aboard.
title: Star Wars (1977) #52: To Take The Tarkin

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #53: The Last Gift From Alderaan!
synopsis: Princess Leia Organa and Captain Chedaki take a blockade runner to investigate Imperial activity in the Shiva system. As they board a shuttle for the surface, a space mine damages the ship and kills Chedaki, forcing Leia to launch the crippled shuttle to spare the rest of the crew. She crashes on Shiva IV and is attacked by a savage raiding party, only to be rescued by a group of natives led by Aron Peacebringer and Kéral Longknife. Leia returns with Aron to the capital, Illyriaqüm, and tries to warn him of the threat posed by the Galactic Empire, but the warlord does not believe such an empire exists. During a gala of Calian nobility, Aron and Leia are secretly captured by Imperial stormtroopers and brought before General Sk'ar as prisoners.
title: Star Wars (1977) #53: The Last Gift From Alderaan!

synopsis:
On the planet Shiva IV, General Sk'ar holds Aron Peacebringer and Leia Organa captive, and Aron learns that his own protégé, Ygal Delois, has turned traitor. Delois reveals Sk'ar's plan to wipe out the Calian Confederacy with an anti-matter bomb. Leia and Aron break free, kill their guard, and steal an air-ship, flying toward Sk'ar's battle cruiser. After downing a squad of TIE fighters they crash onto the cruiser's lower decks and battle stormtroopers, soon joined by Luke Skywalker, Chewbacca, Lando Calrissian, and Kéral Longknife.

While the others fight, Leia hunts down and begins defusing the anti-matter bomb. Lando confronts Sk'ar, whose personal force field deflects blaster fire, and as Sk'ar flees aboard a shuttle, Chewbacca hurls a gun mount that destroys the craft and kills him. Leia deactivates the bomb, and the heroes celebrate in Illyriaqüm until C-3PO warns that an Imperial Star Destroyer is approaching. Aboard the *Millennium Falcon*, they bait the Star Destroyer toward a black hole; Luke uses the Force to steer the *Falcon* safely around its edge, while the pursuing Star Destroyer cannot slow in time and is destroyed.
title: Star Wars (1977) #54: Starfire Rising

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #56: Coffin in the Clouds
synopsis: Star Wars #56 in the Marvel Star Wars series of comics. It was also the story that introduced the character of Shira Brie, later known as Lumiya.
title: Star Wars (1977) #56: Coffin in the Clouds

synopsis:
Captain Hugo Treece, the Imperial administrator of Cloud City, throws Lando Calrissian off the edge of the city's rotunda, but his former aide Lobot dons a life-jet, dives after him, and carries him safely down to Ugnorgrad, the marsh-islet city of the Ugnaughts. An Ugnaught news crew broadcasts the rescue on their program *Action Tidings*, and when Imperial stormtroopers raid their floatboat caravan, Lando improvises a sling to catapult a film reel into a skimmer's fan and destroy it.

Meanwhile Luke Skywalker, R2-D2, and Shira Brie land on a deserted Cloud City and discover that the Ugnaught workers, enslaved by Treece, have staged an uprising and planted bombs throughout the city. King Ozz tells Lando the truth and enlists his help to overthrow Treece. As Imperial troops attack, Lando, Lobot, and the Ugnaughts join Luke and Shira in the fight. Luke uses the Force to trip the bombs' primer switches in a spectacle that convinces everyone he could detonate them, driving Treece and his stormtroopers to retreat. Afterward, Lando has Lobot hack Treece's illegal bank account and transmit a record of the stolen funds to Darth Vader's private account.
title: Star Wars (1977) #57: Hello, Bespin, Good-bye!

synopsis:
Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian, and Chewbacca blast off from the planet Arbra in the *Millennium Falcon*, bound for a meeting with a seedy merchant named Orion Ferret at a space station known as Bazarre.

Meanwhile, Princess Leia rejoins the Rebel fleet stationed above Arbra and attempts a bold maneuver to conceal the fleet from Imperial scouts. Using a Kerts-Bhrg field generator, the Rebels construct a pyramid-shaped force field that envelops the entire fleet and move the ships within the corona of Arbra's sun, trusting the generator to shield them from the deadly ultraviolet rays. As Leia boards a shuttle to return to the surface, the generators malfunction. An absorption circuit in the control generator overloads, flooding the maintenance bay with noxious fumes and knocking out all the service personnel. Only the droids C-3PO and R2-D2 are unaffected, and they space-walk out to run a conduit cable into the generator and stabilize the failing power levels, averting disaster. As the crisis passes, the *Millennium Falcon* emerges from lightspeed and arrives at Bazarre.
title: Star Wars (1977) #58: Sundown!

synopsis:
Lando Calrissian and Luke Skywalker arrive at the gigantic merchant station Bazarre to meet a shifty administrator named Orion Ferret. Lando has dealt with Ferret before and does not trust him, but they need his connections to secure four black-market Imperial TIE fighters. After taking his payment, Ferret lends them a shuttle programmed with coordinates for Patch-4, a garbage world where the fighters have been hidden. Luke and Lando leave Chewbacca on the station and fly out to the planet of trash.

With them gone, Ferret tries to sabotage the mission, reasoning that killing the pair lets him keep the money, the fighters, and the *Millennium Falcon*. To keep Chewbacca from interfering, he imprisons the Wookiee in a gelatinous spritzer solution, but Chewbacca escapes, chases him across the station, and disables his getaway shuttle with his bowcaster. On Patch-4, Luke and Lando dig out the half-buried TIE fighters and are attacked by a giant worm-like Watchbeast. Retreating into a network of tunnels, they meet a homeless nomad named Serja Kesselrook and a colony of renegades who have made the trash world their home. Serja explains that the Watchbeast, Ceasar, belongs to Ferret, who controls it with a sonic pacifier aboard the shuttle. Luke sneaks past the beast while Lando distracts it, retrieves the pacifier, and lulls the creature to sleep. They pick up Chewbacca and bring the four TIE fighters back to the Rebel base on Arbra.
title: Star Wars (1977) #59: Bazarre

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #06: Is This the Final Chapter?
synopsis: The conclusion of the adaptation of A New Hope! Can the Rebels destroy the Death Star?
title: Star Wars (1977) #06: Is This the Final Chapter?

synopsis:
Flying the captured TIE fighters, members of Rogue Squadron infiltrate the Imperial outpost on Spindrift and strafe its command towers. An Imperial Major and his underling Gerdy try to flee across a suspension bridge, but a TIE fighter destroys the far end and forces their surrender. They are stunned to find the pilots are Rebel officers Luke Skywalker, Thorben, Shira Brie, Gemmer, and Wald. After securing the base and recovering coordinates for a new Imperial armada, the Rebels return to Arbra.

Back at base, Shira Brie asks Leia for the return coordinates so she can visit her homeworld, Shalyvane, on a personal matter, but Leia refuses to reveal the top-secret location. Luke volunteers to escort Shira and take responsibility for bringing her back, with Thorben and Wald joining them. On Shalyvane, Shira leads them to the Circle of Kavaan in the capital of Chinshassa and tells a story of how her people were slaughtered by the savage Em'liy when she was a girl. As she finishes, a horde of Em'liy warriors appears. In the ensuing firefight Shira slips away to her TIE fighter, swings back around, and decimates the attackers. The group returns to Arbra, and Shira never reveals the true purpose of her visit.
title: Star Wars (1977) #60: Shira's Story

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #61: Screams in the Void
synopsis:
An Imperial armada led by Admiral Mils Giel's flagship transports a captured Teezl toward the Imperial capital. The Empire intends to exploit the creature's abilities to enable instantaneous communication across the galaxy. Aware of the plan, the Rebel Alliance sends four pilots, Luke Skywalker, Shira Brie, Hanc Thorben, and Alph, in modified TIE fighters to pose as a returning Imperial patrol and attack the armada from within.

Giel orders the Teezl to jam all frequencies except the Imperial war band, cutting off the Rebels' modified channel. Luke locates the flagship holding the Teezl but finds a TIE fighter in his line of fire. Unable to confirm whether it is friend or foe by comms, he reaches out with the Force, which tells him the fighter is an enemy. He destroys it and then disables the ship carrying the Teezl, killing the creature. Returning to Arbra, Luke learns that Thorben recovered a visual recording of the battle, and the footage reveals that the pilot Luke shot down was fellow Rebel Shira Brie.
title: Star Wars (1977) #61: Screams in the Void

synopsis:
News of Shira Brie's death spreads through the Rebel base at Salvation. Luke Skywalker is consumed by grief and feels the Force betrayed him by letting him fire on her fighter. The other personnel look down on him, and a belligerent soldier named Milo Fourstar accuses him of deliberately killing Shira because she spurned his advances.

Seeking answers, Luke asks Lando for the *Millennium Falcon* to return to Shira's homeworld. Under orders to refuse, Lando instead hints that the ship will be left unguarded for fifteen minutes. Luke and Chewbacca fly to Shalyvane and land at Chinshassa, where Em'liy warriors immediately attack. Hiding in an underground warren, they find an injured old Em'liy named G'hinji, who reveals that Chinshassa has never had a Human settlement, meaning Shira's story was a lie. Remembering a bloodletting ritual he once saw Shira perform at the Circle of Kavaan, Luke slices open his palm and lets his blood drip onto the ruined cairns. In a brilliant flash of light, Darth Vader appears before them.
title: Star Wars (1977) #62: Pariah!

synopsis:
On Shalyvane, Luke Skywalker, Chewbacca, and the Em'liy G'hinji face the image of Darth Vader, only to realize it is a holographic recording. Vader reveals that the late Rebel pilot Shira Brie was in fact an Imperial spy before the emitter explodes. Unwilling to take the claim on faith, Luke and Chewbacca set out for the databank vault on Krake's Planet.

Aboard the *Executor*, Vader meets with Admiral Mils Giel, who recently failed to secure the Teezl. Respecting Giel's integrity, Vader spares his life but demotes him to lieutenant. Meanwhile, Luke and Chewbacca infiltrate the Krake Data Vault, a facility built from the mass of a giant Mind Spider. Luke tricks an Imperial officer into downloading records and confirms Vader spoke the truth: Shira was born on Coruscant and genetically raised to be a perfect spy, personally selected by Vader to infiltrate the Alliance. Crushed but somewhat consoled that the Force had not betrayed him after all, Luke fights off a squad of stormtroopers and escapes with Chewbacca, who destroys the outpost with the *Millennium Falcon*'s deflector screens. Deep within the *Executor*, Shira Brie's barely-alive body floats in a bacta tank.
title: Star Wars (1977) #63: The Mind Spider

synopsis:
Learning that King S'Shah of Serphidi is negotiating to host an Imperial base, Princess Leia sends Luke Skywalker, Cinda Tarheel, and Berl on an information-gathering mission. Reaching the Belial system, they are ambushed by Imperial TIE fighters. Tarheel's fighter is crippled and crash-lands on a mountain butte, and Luke and Berl follow her down. As they confirm she is alive, lizard-mounted Serps attack, killing Berl with an energy-lance and taking Tarheel prisoner to King S'Shah's castle.

While tracking them, Luke saves an aging Serp named Elglih from a carnivorous plant and learns Elglih is loyal to the Rebellion and wants S'Shah deposed. The only lawful way to unseat the king is through a deadly contest called a Doom-Joust, which Elglih enters, naming Luke his squire. On the day of the joust, Elglih admits he is too frail to win, so Luke takes his place and challenges S'Shah directly. Luke dismounts the king, but as S'Shah readies a hidden micro-jolt weapon, Elglih's warning lets Luke spin around and kill him. Elglih becomes the new monarch, pledges to keep the Empire away from Serphidi, and Tarheel is freed as the two pilots return to Arbra.
title: Star Wars (1977) #64: Serphidian Eyes

synopsis:
Luke Skywalker is placed on trial for the negligent death of Captain Shira Brie. A panel led by Leia, General Rieekan, and General Duron Veertag reviews the evidence, clears him of all charges, and reinstates him at the rank of commander.

Afterward, a pilot named Gemmer complains that a souvenir rock from the old Golrath base keeps projecting images at night. The Rebel leaders discover that Golrath's volcanic minerals record light images of their surroundings and replay them as holograms once cooled. Fearing the Imperials, who now hold Golrath, could extract the location of the Rebel base at Arbra, Leia personally leads an expedition to destroy any trace of it. Golrath Station is now commanded by the demoted Lieutenant Mils Giel, who learns of the mineral's properties and summons TIE fighters to defend the base.

X-wings and Y-wings clash with the Imperial fighters over Golrath while Leia dives through the battle and descends on a parabelt rocket harness to overload the reactor core. Giel corners her and seals the exits, willing to die to kill the Rebel leader, but Leia distracts him with her harness and escapes, radioing Lando to meet her. Enraged at his failure, Giel fires a parting shot at a passing X-wing before evacuating just as the base explodes. The ship he hit is Luke's, and the damage strands him behind as the rest of the fleet jumps to hyperspace, leaving him to face a fresh squad of TIE fighters.
title: Star Wars (1977) #65: Golrath Never Forgets

synopsis:
His X-wing damaged, Luke Skywalker is forced to land on the backwater world of Beheboth and wanders into a moisture-farming community in the district of Garrotine. Bartering for food in a cantina, he is drawn into a brawl with farmers who mistake him for a brigand until a woman named Darial Anglethorn intervenes. She explains that roving brigands have been stealing the settlement's precious water and hires Luke as a bodyguard, driving him to her walled moisture farm.

That night everyone on the farm, including Luke, mysteriously passes out. They wake to find the moisture vaporators destroyed and Darial gone. Luke tracks her across the deserts to the stronghold of a pirate named Gideon Longspar, whose men capture him. Longspar reveals he holds Darial and an alien entity called the Tirrith, a hive-minded being of sentient mystical energy. Having captured half of the entity, Longspar has forced it to poison the vaporators, turning the night air into a knockout gas so his men can steal the water. Luke frees Darial and tricks Longspar into destroying the pod holding the captured Tirrith particles. On the entity's advice, Darial reluctantly destroys the stronghold's water tanks, sweeping the guards away and letting them escape. Grateful, the reunited Tirrith charges the clouds with electrostatic energy and brings a badly needed rainfall to Beheboth.
title: Star Wars (1977) #66: The Water Bandits

synopsis:
While the Rebel leaders plan a rescue for the captured Han Solo, C-3PO, Chewbacca, Plif, and several other Hoojibs venture into unexplored tunnels near the hidden base on Arbra to search for R2-D2. The droid had wandered off toward a faint cry for help, but the cry was a ruse to lure him to the lair of the Darker, a creature made of the dark energies of the ancient Arbran civilization and trapped in an abandoned underground city by a force field the Arbrans built.

Entering the city, Threepio and Chewbacca find that the Darker has captured Artoo, intending to use his components to build a device that will disable the force field imprisoning it. When Chewbacca tries to intervene, the Darker manipulates his emotions and turns him against Threepio. Threepio manages to free Chewbacca from the creature's control and release Artoo, then realizes the Darker can be destroyed by the very field that holds it. He directs Chewbacca to seize the creature and hurl it into the force screen, killing it. The impact begins collapsing the cavern, and the group flees into a connecting tunnel just before the city is reduced to rubble.
title: Star Wars (1977) #67: The Darker

synopsis: Their search for the frozen Han Solo narrowed to three bounty hunters, the Rebels split into teams. Leia and C-3PO track Dengar to Mandalore, where they land near the capital and stumble onto a slaver caravan herding enslaved Mandalorians. When Mandalorian soldiers who look like Boba Fett attack the slavers, Leia finds herself fighting alongside them. Their leader turns out to be *Fenn Shysa*, not Fett, and he brings the pair back to his camp, recounting how he and his surviving comrades have fought to free their people from the Empire since the Clone Wars. Shysa reveals he holds Dengar captive but refuses to hand him over, having planned to trade the hunter to the Empire for his imprisoned friend Tobbi Dala. Leia frees Dengar herself, distracting Shysa with a kiss before knocking him out, and escapes with the captive hunter. Unknown to her, Dengar has signaled the Empire, and she and C-3PO are ambushed by stormtroopers and an armored walker.
title: Star Wars (1977) #68: The Search Begins

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #69: Death in the City of Bone
synopsis: Betrayed by Dengar, Leia and C-3PO are captured and taken to the City of Bone, where the Imperial commander known as the Suprema vows to crush the Mandalorian resistance and kill *Fenn Shysa*. But one of their stormtrooper escorts is Shysa in disguise, and he frees them, striking a bargain: he will help Leia recapture Dengar if she helps rescue his imprisoned friend Tobbi Dala. After freeing Dala, the group triggers a full assault on the fortress. Leia and Dala destroy the control box binding the enslaved Mandalorians, who rise up in a riot. Cornering the Suprema, the Rebels watch him mortally wound Dala, who with his last strength opens the city doors so his people can escape. As Leia and Shysa flee by airspeeder, Dala seals the doors behind them, and the pursuing Shrike Squadron crashes into the city, setting off explosions that kill both the Suprema and Dala. Shysa forces Dengar to reveal a lead pointing toward a hideout near the Anga system. Elsewhere, Lando and Chewbacca close in on the bounty hunter Bossk.
title: Star Wars (1977) #69: Death in the City of Bone

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #07: New Planets, New Perils!
synopsis: Han and Chewie head out to pay their debt to Jabba, when they're attacked by pirates!
title: Star Wars (1977) #07: New Planets, New Perils!

synopsis: Lando, Chewbacca, Luke, and R2-D2 fly the *Millennium Falcon* to Stenos, chasing leads on Bossk and IG-88 in hopes one will point them to Boba Fett and the frozen Han Solo. Uneasy that both trails end in the same place, Lando fears a double-cross, prompting Luke to recount an earlier misadventure on the same world. In the flashback, the heroes came to Stenos to contact a silent Rebel outpost, only to find it occupied by Rik Duel's gang of smugglers, who claimed to have joined the Rebellion and to be searching for a sacred statue of Vol prized by the native Stenaxes. After the group unearths the statue, Duel betrays them, revealing he works for Governor Quorl Matrin. Stormtroopers summoned by Chihdo attack, but a swarm of Stenaxes arrives to reclaim their idol and saves the Rebels. Matrin then ambushes the fleeing Duel gang at their ship, only for the Stenaxes to descend on the Imperials as well. Back in the present, Luke suggests they keep the ship out of sight of the city.
title: Star Wars (1977) #70: The Stenax Shuffle

synopsis: Landing on Stenos to hunt for Bossk and IG-88, Lando and Luke leave Chewbacca and R2-D2 with the *Millennium Falcon* and head into the capital. In a cantina, Lando is recognized by his old enemy Barpotomous Drebble, who offers a 10,000-credit bounty for his capture, igniting a blasterfight as patrons scramble for the reward. Luke collapses part of the building with his lightsaber to cover their escape, then spots Bossk and gives chase, only to circle back into another bounty-hungry crowd. Separated from Lando, Luke encounters Rik Duel and Dani, who survived their last Stenos ordeal by pinning the blame on Governor Matrin. Duel enlists Luke's help finding his missing partner Chihdo. Meanwhile Lando believes he has found Han, spotting a carbonite slab being hauled through the streets, and he and Luke follow it into a large building. The frozen figure turns out to be Chihdo, used as bait: Bossk, IG-88C, and a band of bounty hunters surround the two Rebels.
title: Star Wars (1977) #71: Return to Stenos

synopsis: Surrounded by bounty hunters led by IG-88 and Bossk, Luke and Lando are saved when Rik Duel and Dani open fire from hiding, but the hunters regroup and capture Lando and Dani, marching them to a carbon-freezing chamber. Barpotomous Drebble intends to freeze Lando and ship him home, and a bribe and a seduction attempt both fail, though Dani manages to knock out a guard before the pair are recaptured. Luke and Rik ambush the hunters from the rooftops but are quickly overwhelmed, until Chewbacca arrives with a Stenax he has recruited to the cause. Stenax spears cut down the hunters, and Chewbacca stops Bossk and IG-88C from escaping. With Han's trail once again a dead end, the Rebels return to the *Millennium Falcon*, leaving Rik Duel to puzzle over how to free the still-frozen Chihdo. As they depart Stenos, they discover a stowaway: Dani, infatuated with Luke, has decided to come along.
title: Star Wars (1977) #72: Fool's Bounty

synopsis: Star Wars #73 features a story by Mary Jo Duffy, pencilled by Ron Frenz and inked by Armando Gil [sic], in which we witness a team-up between Princess Leia and a woman who has designs on none other than Luke Skywalker.
title: Star Wars (1977) #73: Lahsbane

synopsis: The search for Han Solo is cut short as the rebels find themselves facing death on a planet of Water Breathers.
title: Star Wars (1977) #75: Tidal

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #76: Artoo-Detoo to the Rescue
synopsis: Lando Calrissian and Mon [sic] are trapped in the Empire-controlled citadel of Gamander [sic]. And their lives may depend on the strangest undercover agent of all—R2-D2.
title: Star Wars (1977) #76: Artoo-Detoo to the Rescue

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #77: Chanteuse of the Stars...
synopsis: Appearing for one issue only—the songstress who will melt your heart and shatter your wine glass. We mean none other than Leia Organa, "Chanteuse of the Stars." The rebel alliance may hinge on her ability to entertain a group of Ambassadors, and with her singing voice, (Dazzler she ain't!) the rebels may have to throw down their arms and surrender!
title: Star Wars (1977) #77: Chanteuse of the Stars...

synopsis: A flashback sequence involving an old friend of Luke's from the planet of ice, Hoth.
title: Star Wars (1977) #78: Hoth Stuff!

synopsis: All new adventures of the world famous characters from the greatest science fantasy epic ever! You won't believe the car chase this issue with Lando and a drunken Wookiee, as the search for Han Solo continues!
title: Star Wars (1977) #79: The Big Con

synopsis: Han and Chewie take a new job defending a moisture farmer from a local gang known as the Cloud-Riders.
title: Star Wars (1977) #08: Eight for Aduba-3

synopsis: The search for Tay Vanis ends! C-3PO [sic] finds true love at last, but there's a tragic twist! "Lady," written by Jo Duffy, pencilled by Ron Frenz and inked by Tom Palmer, is the untold tale that takes place right before the Return of the Jedi.
title: Star Wars (1977) #80: Ellie

synopsis: What a homecoming! When Han, Princess Leia and Artoo return to Tatooine to tie up some unfinished business, it's hardly business as usual—when some of Tatooine's deadliest surviving citizens form the welcoming committee! "Jawas of Doom" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Ron Frenz, and inked by Tom Palmer.
title: Star Wars (1977) #81: Jawas of Doom

synopsis: Shortly after the Battle of Endor, Luke Skywalker trains with the Ewoks on the forest moon before Admiral Ackbar tasks him with visiting several worlds to invite their leaders to a planetary council for the new government. Accompanied by the Hoojib Plif, Luke's first stop is Iskalon, a world he last saw devastated by the Empire. There he finds Rik Duel's ship, the *Moonshadow*, salvaging the wreckage, with the infatuated Zeltron Dani again throwing herself at him. Luke and Plif are disgusted by Duel's scavenging, and a sea creature drawn up by the angered Iskalonians attacks the salvagers. The aquatic Iskalonians emerge to drive off the airbreathers until Kiro recognizes Luke and halts them. Their leader Mone, still distrustful of outsiders even after the Empire's defeat, asks Luke to leave and refuses to send any representative to the Alliance. Bored with his role on the planet, Kiro chooses to leave with the group anyway, boarding the *Moonshadow* since there is no room aboard Luke's X-wing.
title: Star Wars (1977) #82: Diplomacy

synopsis: Lando goes out to meet an old flame and ends up in the middle of a planetary war!
title: Star Wars (1977) #83: Sweetheart Contract

synopsis: When Solo comes across a rare artifact, it will lead into the rediscovery of a planet long since destroyed…but why does this planet still exist?
title: Star Wars (1977) #84: Seoul Searching

synopsis: Bet you didn't know that Chewbacca had a wife and family! But he may never get to see them again—when bloodthirsty bounty-hunters attack him on his way back to the Wookiee planet! And if Lando and Han can't help—who can?
title: Star Wars (1977) #85: The Hero

synopsis: Who are they? What part of the galaxy do they come from? What do they want? Han, Luke, and Leia better find out—or face total disaster!
title: Star Wars (1977) #86: The Alderaan Factor

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #87: Still Active After All These Years
synopsis: Beginning another cosmic adventure that goes beyond "Return of the Jedi!" What could keep Luke Skywalker from being reunited with the Rik Duel gang? How about a still active imperial Doomsday weapon? We just had to call this story "Still Active After All These Years!" Guest-starring Kiro, the Water-Breather. Time is running out! The imperial doomsday machine is about to cause armageddon—galaxy-wide! It's up to Luke and his band of con men to save the cosmos! And Danny [sic] the Zeltron and Kiro are in this one, too! "Countdown!" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Ron Frenz [sic], and in
title: Star Wars (1977) #87: Still Active After All These Years

synopsis: Marvel continues to present all-new post-Jedi adventures! It's a diplomatic mission, just perfect for Princess Leia and Mon Mothma to handle—until they stumble upon Imperial forces fighting to control a strategic stronghold! And the Imperial forces are winning! "Figurehead" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Bob McLeod, and inked by Tom Palmer! The return of one of our most requested characters! From the Star Wars canon comes... Shira Brie! Shira is Luke Skywalker's second deadliest enemy! We'll leave it to you to find out Luke's deadliest enemy—but only if you buy this phenomenal
title: Star Wars (1977) #88: Figurehead

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #89: I'll See You in the Throne Room
synopsis: Luke finds himself fighting more for revenge than for freedom…is he on the path to the dark side?
title: Star Wars (1977) #89: I'll See You in the Throne Room

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #09: Showdown on a Wasteland World!
synopsis: When the High-hounds attack, a mythical monster is summoned that may mean the end for a village as well as Han and Chewie!
title: Star Wars (1977) #09: Showdown on a Wasteland World!

synopsis: An all-new post-Jedi adventure, presented as only Marvel can present it! What will happen to Luke and Leia now that the rebels have won the war? It's a major turning point in the Star Wars saga—and it guest-stars Rik Duel! And the Kiro gang! And—hold onto yer hats—the Ewoks! Rik, Dani and Chihdi [sic] promise to be good, but no sooner do they land with Luke and Leia on Endor when they're up to their ears in trouble again! What is the future of the Alliance? Luke, Leia and the rebel leaders must decide—and fast! "The Choice!" is written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Bob McLeo
title: Star Wars (1977) #90: The Choice

synopsis: The villain's name is Knife, and he's out to enslave the world of the Wookiees and Chewie's brother-in-law is helping him! "Wookiee World" written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Tony Salmons and inked by Tom Palmer.
title: Star Wars (1977) #91: Wookiee World

synopsis: Prince Denin petitions help from Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia and the Rebel Alliance to fight the remnants of the Imperial forces. But this help is overshadowed by strange dreams involving Yoda, Ben Kenobi and Darth Vader!
title: Star Wars (1977) #92: The Dream

synopsis: Luke, Leia and Han find themselves up to their ears in fur-coated trouble when they get caught in the middle of a bloody conflict between two fanatic factions of a race of cat-people! "Catspaw!" written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Sal Buscema and inked by Tom Palmer.
title: Star Wars (1977) #93: Catspaw

synopsis: Han, Luke, Leia, Chewie, Lando, C-3PO [sic] and R2-D2 are up to their ears in Ewoks and trouble when war breaks out between the Ewoks and Lahsbees! "Small Wars," written by Jo Duffy and premiering the work of the new Star Wars penciler, Cynthia Martin! Inked by Tom Palmer.
title: Star Wars (1977) #94: Small Wars

synopsis: There's a new Dark Lord in the galaxy, and she's out for Luke's head! Not only must Luke fight Lumiya, he must also deal with who and, more dangerously, what she really is!
title: Star Wars (1977) #95: No Zeltrons

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #96: Duel With a Dark Lady
synopsis: There's a new Dark Lord in the galaxy, and she's out for Luke's head! Not only must Luke fight Lumiya, he must also deal with who and, more dangerously, what she really is! "Duel With a Dark Lady!" written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Cynthia Martin and inked by Bob Wiacek.
title: Star Wars (1977) #96: Duel With a Dark Lady

synopsis: Luke, Kiro and Dani the Zeltron must fight alone against an army of Nagai invading a remote planet! This issue, a major character dies—could it be Luke's last stand? "Escape" is written by Jo Duffy and penciled by Cynthia Martin.
title: Star Wars (1977) #97: Escape

synopsis: Han heads out on a mission to a former Imperial prison planet, with Nien Nunb as his co-pilot on the Millennium Falcon. When he gets there, he is surprised to see that his job is to escort a woman and her child. But when the Imperials show up, things get dangerous for Han.
title: Star Wars (1977) #98: Supply and Demand

displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #99: Touch of the Goddess
synopsis: Talk about pouring gasoline on a fire—Lando's in trouble, and the only way Han and Luke can save him is to start a war! Plus: the long-awaited return of Captain Drebble [and] Lemo! And discover how Sanda clears up the mystery of the Dancing Goddess
title: Star Wars (1977) #99: Touch of the Goddess

synopsis: Star Wars (スター・ウォーズ Sutā Wōzu) is a video game released in Japan in 1987 by Namco under their Namcot label. It was developed for the Family Computer (Famicom for short). The game is a side-scrolling platformer in which the player controls Luke, as he travels in order to join the Rebellion against the Empire.
title: Star Wars (1987)

synopsis: Star Wars is a video game released for the Nintendo Entertainment System in October 1991. The head designer was Akila Redmer with contributions from Kalani Streicher, Kelly Flock, and Mike Ebert.
title: Star Wars (1991)

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #02: Skywalker Strikes, Part II
synopsis: The greatest space adventure of all returns to Marvel! Luke Skywalker and the ragtag rebels opposing the Galactic Empire are fresh off their biggest victory so far, the destruction of the massive Death Star. But the Empire's not toppled yet! Join Luke, Princess Leia, Han Solo, Chewbacca, C-3PO, R2-D2, and the rest of the Rebel Alliance as they fight for freedom against Darth Vader and his evil master, the Emperor! But when a Rebel assault goes wrong, Han and Leia must think fast to make their escape, while Luke comes face-to-face with Darth Vader! In the explosive aftermath, a humbled Luke returns to Tatooine to learn more about his mentor, Obi-Wan Kenobi. Meanwhile, Leia and Han undertake a vital and dangerous secret mission. But can they succeed without Luke?
title: Star Wars (2015) #02: Skywalker Strikes, Part II

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #20: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi
synopsis: While searching for answers in his quest to become a Jedi, Luke Skywalker has uncovered a Journal written by Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi. The Journal details Kenobi's adventures while watching over young Luke on Tatooine, including when Jabba the Hutt hired the Wookiee Black Krrsantan to hunt Obi-Wan down for defeating his thugs during the Great Drought. What follows is an excerpt from that Journal.
title: Star Wars (2015) #20: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi

synopsis: Star Wars is a comic book series published by Dark Horse Comics that began in January 2013. It concluded with issue 20 on August 13, 2014. The series takes place between Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope and Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back, dating itself to two months after the Battle of Yavin. However, it heavily conflicts with the preexisting timeline of the Rebel Alliance post-Yavin; per The New Essential Chronology and other sources, the Alliance was trapped on Yavin by the Yavin blockade for the first six months after the battle, but the comics instead depict the Alliance base
title: Star Wars (2013)

displayTitle: Star Wars (2013) #01: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part One
synopsis: This is Star Wars as you remember it... and as you have never seen it before! We're taking you back to those heady, adventure-filled days following the destruction of the Death Star, when the Empire ruled, the Rebels were on the run, and the galaxy was a dangerous place where anything might happen!
title: Star Wars (2013) #01: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part One

synopsis: Luke Skywalker and Wedge Antilles have infiltrated an Imperial Destroyer. But now nine thousand stormtroopers are searching for them, and Darth Vader is aware of their presence. Completing their mission and escaping with their lives may be an either/or proposition! Meanwhile, the Rebel fleet prepares for an Imperial assault!
title: Star Wars (2013) #10

synopsis: Princess Leia's stealth squadron is together again, just in time to fight for their lives. The Star Destroyer Devastator launches an all-out attack on the Rebel fleet! Meanwhile, Vader's secret agent realizes what it could mean to fail the Dark Lord. And, on Coruscant, Han Solo's luck may be changing—unless…well, unless it isn't…
title: Star Wars (2013) #11

synopsis: After a battle with the Empire, it is a time of reunions, departures, and reassessments. Han and Chewie return from their mission to Coruscant, Luke deals with love lost, and Leia must accept greater responsibilities within the Rebellion. But first she has an important announcement for the Rebel commanders...
title: Star Wars (2013) #12

synopsis: After a defeat by Rebel forces and the escape of prisoner Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader decides to clean house. From one end of the galaxy to the other, Vader's "new broom"—a squad of elite stormtroopers backed by the Dark Lord's own lightsaber—is sweeping the ranks of the Empire!
title: Star Wars (2013) #13

displayTitle: Star Wars (2013) #14: Five Days of Sith, Part Two
synopsis: Ben Kenobi haunts Darth Vader's dreams, even as the name Skywalker occupies his waking thoughts. The Rebel victory over his former flagship has exposed spies and incompetence within the Empire, and Vader has decided an off-the-record mission of vengeance is needed to clear his head—and restore his reputation!
title: Star Wars (2013) #14: Five Days of Sith, Part Two

synopsis: Welcome to Arrochar! Enjoy our clean mountain air. Gaze at our majestic vistas. Build a secure base for your Rebel fleet. All we ask is that Princess Leia marry our Prince! You can take the girl out of the Rebellion, but can you take the rebel out of the girl?
title: Star Wars (2013) #15

synopsis: Establishing the new Rebel base on Arrochar, Wedge Antilles teaches the local pilots Rogue Squadron's secret tactics, Luke Skywalker joins the Arrocharian militia on a "routine" mission, and Princess Leia balances her duty to the Rebellion with her upcoming duties as a royal wife. So why is everyone so uneasy?
title: Star Wars (2013) #16

synopsis: As Princess Leia and her prince prepare for their wedding day on Arrochar, the uneasy feeling among the Rebels—especially Han Solo—grows. Meanwhile, Luke, on an alpine mission with local troops, discovers the Alliance's new "friends" aren't so friendly!
title: Star Wars (2013) #17

synopsis: Princess Leia's wedding day has arrived—and so has the Empire! As Darth Vader's fleet bears down on the planet, the Rebels scramble to defend themselves—they have been double crossed!
title: Star Wars (2013) #18

synopsis: Luke, Leia, Han, and Chewie are together again aboard the Millennium Falcon. Disguised as smugglers, they have a crazy caper to pull off—if they can avoid the dread bounty hunter IG-88!
title: Star Wars (2013) #19

displayTitle: Star Wars (2013) #02: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Two
synopsis: A cagey young Imperial officer seems to anticipate the Rebels' every move, so Princess Leia forms a "stealth squadron" (including Luke Skywalker) to expose a suspected spy within their ranks. Meanwhile, Han Solo and Chewbacca run afoul of bounty hunter Boba Fett, and the Emperor sends Darth Vader on a secret mission! Stars Han Solo, Princess Leia, Luke Skywalker, and Darth Vader! Set during classic trilogy events!
title: Star Wars (2013) #02: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Two

synopsis: Leia risks it all for a friend she hasn't seen since childhood! Han, Luke, and Chewie risk it all for Leia! Brian Wood and Carlos D'Anda say farewell to that galaxy far, far away before they send our heroes to an unknown fate!
title: Star Wars (2013) #20

displayTitle: Star Wars (2013) #03: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Three
synopsis: Princess Leia formed a secret squadron of stealth X-wings to help expose a spy within the Rebel ranks. But taking command puts her at odds with Luke Skywalker at a time they need solidarity more than ever, especially when the spy has alerted the Empire to Han Solo's latest venture!
title: Star Wars (2013) #03: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Three

displayTitle: Star Wars (2013) #04: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Four
synopsis: As Princess Leia's stealth squadron lays a trap for the spy within their ranks, she soon realizes that the Rebels are ill prepared for the Empire's zealous pursuit. Meanwhile, Han Solo flees from the Empire through the lower levels of Coruscant, and Luke Skywalker enjoys being grounded with a female friend
title: Star Wars (2013) #04: In the Shadow of Yavin, Part Four

synopsis: When Princess Leia is caught between two squadrons of TIE fighters, her undercover scouting may be at an end. But Luke Skywalker is doing his best to disobey Leia's own orders, and to once again save the princess. Time is short and the Empire is closing in!
title: Star Wars (2013) #05

synopsis: When he and Chewbacca are cornered by Imperials, Han Solo resorts to an improvised plan, with the most dangerous results imaginable! Meanwhile, half a galaxy away, Princess Leia and Wedge Antilles prepare for certain death, unaware that Luke Skywalker has an improvisation of his own. And Darth Vader is watching...
title: Star Wars (2013) #06

synopsis: Princess Leia takes the Rebels' secret mission to the next level! Playing it safe has gotten them nowhere, and they are running out of resources and time. What's the fastest way to get inside info on the Empire? Have your top pilots get themselves captured!
title: Star Wars (2013) #07

synopsis: Luke Skywalker and Wedge Antilles intentionally allow themselves to be captured by the Empire to discover how Colonel Bircher is tracking the Rebels' movements. Meanwhile, Darth Vader sends a spy into his own ranks to find out the same thing. And Han and Chewie go blaster to blaster against Boba Fett!
title: Star Wars (2013) #08

synopsis: On her own, out in the wide galaxy, while her friends struggle to escape the Empire and evade bounty hunters, Princess Leia steers her X-wing toward the heart of all her grief, a place she longs for, but should probably never revisit: the shattered ruins of her homeworld of Alderaan.
title: Star Wars (2013) #09

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #07: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi: "The Last of His Breed"
synopsis: Luke Skywalker discovered Obi-Wan Kenobi’s journal in his hut on Tatooine. This standout issue shows us a glimpse of what life was like for a Jedi in a time without the Jedi Order.
title: Star Wars (2015) #07: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi: "The Last of His Breed"

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #15: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi
synopsis: Obi-Wan Kenobi is supposed to be keeping a low profile on Tatooine while he watches over young Luke Skywalker, but Owen Lars gets tired of his meddling in their affairs. Plus, Qui-Got Jinn, Jawas, Tuscan Raiders, and Jabba the Hutt!
title: Star Wars (2015) #15: From the Journals of Old Ben Kenobi

synopsis: Star Wars is a Star Wars comic book series written by Charles Soule and published by Marvel Comics, beginning in January of 2020. It features the characters Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca, C-3PO, and R2-D2, and takes place between the events of Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back and Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi. It is a relaunch of the concluded comic series of the same name that ran from 2015 to 2019. Eventually, Marvel announced they would resume their publishing line on May 27, 2020, and the series resumed publication in August 2020 with issue 5.
title: Star Wars (2020)

synopsis:
**To Escape the Empire's Trap, the Rebels Spring One of Their Own!**

What's behind Commander Zahra's quest for revenge against Leia Organa? The powerful, dark answer revealed this issue!

And the Rebel's Fourth and Seventh Divisions will pay the price for Zahra's revenge.

See Luke Skywalker, Wedge Antilles and Shara Bey battle against an onslaught of TIE fighters in the greatest dog fight of the series.
title: Star Wars (2020) #08

synopsis:
**"Operation Starlight" Begins, as the Rebel Pathfinders Undertake a Desperate Mission to the Imperial Core.**

In the Imperial Museum on Coruscant, an ancient droid holds the key to salvation of the Rebel Alliance.

The Rebels' elite operations team, the Pathfinders, must pull off a daring heist right under the nose of the Emperor himself, with Lando and Lobot along for the ride!
title: Star Wars (2020) #09

synopsis: Marvel's relaunch of their flagship ongoing Star Wars series takes place in between The Empire Strikes Back and Return of the Jedi. Luke deals with the loss of his hand and the crushing news that Darth Vader is his father. Leia looks for a way to rescue Han. Lando tries to fill Solo's shoes and navigate the team dynamics of having just betrayed Han to the Empire, while also looking for a way to get even and reclaim Cloud City! A spectacular start to a great series.
title: Star Wars (2020) #01

synopsis:
**Reeling From the Loss of One of Their Own, the Rebels Finally See a Path Forward!**

The Rebels attempt to create a new communications code that the Empire will never be able to crack, in hopes that they might be able to safely reunite their scattered fleet and rejoin the fight.

However, the code has a cost, one that Lando Calrissian is not willing to pay!

Meanwhile, Starlight Squadron, the group of elite pilots tasked with finding the scattered divisions of the fleet, heads out on its first deadly mission.
title: Star Wars (2020) #10

synopsis:
**Operation Starlight Goes Supernova!**

The mission to reunite the Rebel Fleet is countered by a deadly trap sprung by the cunning Commander Zahra!

How will Wedge Antilles, Shara Bey and the other elite pilots of Starlight Squadron survive?

And then there's Lando Calrissian... forced to choose between the Rebellion and one of his oldest friends!
title: Star Wars (2020) #11

synopsis:
Leia Organa and Kes Dameron share stories of their lost loves!

Learn how Poe Dameron's parents met!

Learn the real reason Han Solo is so important to the Rebellion!
title: Star Wars (2020) #12

synopsis:
**Prelude to War of the Bounty Hunters: "The Hunt for Han"**

Chewbacca has heard from an old friend with intelligence on the location of notorious bounty hunter Boba Fett, known to be in possession of Han Solo. Along with Luke Skywalker, the loyal Wookiee sets off in search of his lost friend, on an adventure to Nar Shaddaa, the Smuggler's Moon! But will this mission prevent plucky astromech droid Artoo Detoo from passing along a crucial message to Luke, one that will affect the fate of the entire Jedi Order...?
title: Star Wars (2020) #13

synopsis:
**War of the Bounty Hunters Tie-In!**

The Rebels finally have a lead on the location of Han Solo! It's time to save the captain of the Millennium Falcon from his frozen carbonite tomb! A general, a Jedi, and a Wookiee will mount a desperate rescue mission... But they have no idea what actually awaits them.
title: Star Wars (2020) #14

synopsis:
**"Friends and Enemies"**

Luke Skywalker embarks upon an epic mission with Wedge Antilles and the brave pilots of Starlight Squadron to rescue a lost division of the Rebel Fleet. Leia Organa, Chewbacca and Lando Calrissian attempt to save Han Solo from the evil clutches of Boba Fett. But what hidden vision will the Force send to Luke about the troubles to come?
title: Star Wars (2020) #15

synopsis:
Leia Organa, Chewbacca and Lando Calrissian battle Boba Fett for the ultimate prize, Han Solo!

But they are not the only people who have come to the remote world of Jekara seeking the smuggler. Darth Vader, Jabba the Hutt and more are in the mix as well... Jedi Luke Skywalker may be the Rebels' only hope...

...but where is he?
title: Star Wars (2020) #16

synopsis:
Sith Lord Darth Vader has discovered that his son, Jedi Knight Luke Skywalker, is finally within striking distance. He will pursue him to the very ends of space...Who is hunting whom?

Meanwhile, Leia Organa battles the forces of Crimson Dawn to regain her lost love, Han Solo.
title: Star Wars (2020) #17

synopsis:
Reeling from the events on Jekara and forced to confront the reality of her own choices and losses, Leia Organa meets Qi'ra to discuss the person they both have in common, heroic smuggler Han Solo!

How has he influenced their lives to that point?

And how will it change their fate moving forward?
title: Star Wars (2020) #18

synopsis:
**Luke's Quest for Answers Takes a Dangerous Turn!**

As the Rebellion tries to pull itself together for a last-ditch effort to defeat the evil Galactic Empire, Luke Skywalker realizes it is time for his journey to become a Jedi to continue.

After near-death at the hands of Darth Vader, he knows he has much to learn if he will ever defeat the Dark Lord of the Sith.

But the Jedi Order is gone, and his teachers have vanished... where can Luke turn to find the Jedi legacy he so desperately needs?
title: Star Wars (2020) #19

synopsis: "No - I am your father." In the wake of the events following The Empire Strikes Back, it is a dark time for the heroes of the Rebellion. The Rebel fleet, scattered following a disastrous defeat at the Battle of Hoth. Han Solo, lost to the bounty hunter, Boba Fett, after being frozen in carbonite. And after being lured into a trap on Cloud City and bested in a vicious lightsaber duel against the evil Darth Vader, Luke Skywalker learned the horrible truth about his past. Vader did not kill Luke's father Anakin, Vader is Luke's father! Now, after narrowly escaping the dark lord's clutches, and wounded and reeling from the revelation, Luke, Princess Leia, Lando Calrissian, the Wookiee Chewbacca and the droids C-3PO and R2-D2 must fight their way back to the Rebel Alliance, for the fate of the entire galaxy is at stake! After so many losses is victory still possible? But, what Leia, Luke and their ragtag band of freedom fighters do not realize is that they have only traded one Imperial trap for another! Enter the cunning and vengeful Imperial Commander Ellian Zahra, at the helm of the Tarkin's Will!
title: Star Wars (2020) #02

synopsis:
**Dangerous Lessons!**

Luke Skywalker has found a key piece of instruction in his journey along the Jedi path, the voice of his teacher, Jedi Master Yoda.

But the lesson Luke must learn will not be taught by Yoda, and it will take (and give) more than the young Skywalker could ever have imagined.
title: Star Wars (2020) #20

synopsis:
**Stranded on a Star Destroyer!**

After a mission gone wrong, ace Rebel pilot Shara Bey (Poe Dameron's mother) was left for dead aboard the Tarkin's Will, a huge Star Destroyer.

Shara survived and has been hiding deep inside the massive ship ever since, evading Commander Zahra's notice.

But her time is up. Can she live long enough to escape?
title: Star Wars (2020) #21

synopsis:
**The Dawn Alliance, Part 1 of 3: "The Last Division"**

The time has come for the Rebel Alliance fleet to bring its final lost division home... but to do it, Leia Organa, Mon Mothma and the other Alliance leaders will need to rely on intelligence provided by the notoriously untrustworthy criminal organization Crimson Dawn. Meanwhile, Starlight Squadron and a group of rogue Pathfinders led by Kes Dameron head out on an unauthorized rescue mission to save one of their own...!
title: Star Wars (2020) #22

synopsis:
**The Dawn Alliance - Part 2 of 3: The Last Division**

The fate of the Rebel Alliance fleet is at stake, as its last remaining divisions face off in battle against the brutal tactics of Commander Ellian Zahra and her crew on the Tarkin's Will. For Zahra, this fight is personal; her revenge will not be complete until every life lost on the Death Star is avenged. Epic space warfare in the true Star Wars manner!
title: Star Wars (2020) #23

synopsis: Commander Ellian Zahra, former protege of Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin, has used brilliant tactics and savage brutality to nearly eradicate the Rebel fleet. Now she and her final target, General Leia Organa, will hunt each other across a wild planet, to the bitter end.
title: Star Wars (2020) #24

synopsis:
A Celebration across the Galaxy for Star Wars' 25th issue! With stories set from the Prequel to the Sequel eras, experience new tales of Obi-Wan & Anakin, Darth Vader, Poe Dameron and Kylo Ren. Join Charles Soule, along with his previous artistic collaborators, for this era-spanning celebration!

Contains
- The Lesson (Obi-Wan & Anakin)
- The Lesson (Darth Vader)
- See You Around, Kid
- A Eulogy for Snap
title: Star Wars (2020) #25

synopsis:
The twenty sixth issue of the Marvel comic series Star Wars (2020).
The Rebel Fleet is reunited at last, and Leia Organa, Mon Mothma and the other Alliance leaders send a strong message to the galaxy that the resistance to Palpatine's rule remains as strong as ever. Meanwhile, Lady Qi'ra of Crimson Dawn has activated her sleeper agents across the galaxy, instructing them to cause chaos for the Empire by any means necessary!
title: Star Wars (2020) #26

synopsis:
The twenty seventh issue of the Marvel comic series Star Wars (2020).
The Empire's greatest secret is in the hands of two Crimson Dawn double agents, who are racing to deliver it to the Rebel Alliance to buy themselves safe haven. But the Empire knows what they have stolen, and the entire Imperial Navy is in hot pursuit!
title: Star Wars (2020) #27

synopsis: The price of freedom is examined, as the Rebel Alliance comes to understand the true scope of the threat against it. Threepio enters into aggressive negotiations, and Luke Skywalker embarks upon a desperate mission that will test his growing Jedi skills to the limit.
title: Star Wars (2020) #28

synopsis:
With their supply lines in jeopardy due to unfulfilled promises from Crimson Dawn, General Leia Organa knows it is just a matter of time before the Rebels run out of crucial supplies.

Fortunately, Amilyn Holdo has a solution...a hunt for a vast hoard of fuel thought lost for centuries.

It's time...for a treasure hunt!
title: Star Wars (2020) #29

synopsis:
**"Return to Cloud City!"**

The end of the war and the final defeat of the Rebel Alliance are inevitable as the cunning Imperial Commander Zahra unleashes her fury against the splintered and scattered survivors.

But where have the Rebellion's greatest heroes gone when their cause needs them the most?

Luke Skywalker's only hope at becoming a Jedi Knight was lost during his battle with Darth Vader...and so he, Princess Leia and Lando Calrissian must return to Cloud City to find it! But Leia has her own covert mission to accomplish there...and Lando's mysterious reason for returning could very well ruin their only chance at success!
title: Star Wars (2020) #03

synopsis: Some of the Rebel Alliance's best and brightest have gotten lost in an unknown region of space while hunting for a lost treasure. How will Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, Chewbacca, Lando Calrissian and Amilyn Holdo ever survive the horrors...of No-Space?
title: Star Wars (2020) #30

synopsis: Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa and an elite crew of Rebel heroes are lost in the farthest reaches of No-Space after a mission gone wrong. A desperate maneuver is their only chance to find a way home, but what will it cost them?
title: Star Wars (2020) #31

synopsis:
**The Force Is Broken?**

The efforts of Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn to destroy the Sith have sent deep ripples running through the Force, disrupting Luke Skywalker's connection to the deep powers of the galaxy. How far will he go to find what he has lost?
title: Star Wars (2020) #32

synopsis: Luke Skywalker's connection to the Force has been disrupted; one of his most powerful tools in his journey to become a Jedi is gone. He must undertake an epic quest to find what he has lost...or he, and the galaxy, will fall to darkness.
title: Star Wars (2020) #33

synopsis:
**The Broken Saber of Skywalker!**

Luke Skywalker searches for one of the rarest substances in the galaxy...kyber, the living crystal that powers lightsabers! Does it hold the answer to the unpredictable thing the Force has become (see Hidden Empire #5)? What long-lost secret is he about to uncover?
title: Star Wars (2020) #34

synopsis:
**Luke Skywalker...In the Clutches of Dr. Cuata!**

The would-be Jedi's lightsaber is all but destroyed!

Enter kyber crystal expert Dr. Cuata! Only he can repair it...but at what price?

Luke's life will hang in the balance...and only another Jedi can save him!
title: Star Wars (2020) #35

synopsis:
**Disaster Looms for the Rebel Alliance!**

With the discovery that the Empire is building a second Death Star, the Rebels are on the verge of despair.

But Leia Organa has devised an impossible mission to remind both the Rebels and the galaxy how to hope.

It's the Falcon's time to shine, as the fastest hunk of junk in the galaxy flies into the belly of the beast!
title: Star Wars (2020) #36

synopsis:
**Lobot Lost!**

Something has gone horribly wrong with Lobot, putting the Rebellion at terrible risk! Lando Calrissian will do anything to save his friend, even if it means betraying the Rebels. Will the Scourge of the Droids destroy any chance to cure Lobot, or will he be lost forever?
title: Star Wars (2020) #37

synopsis:
In Jabba the Hutt's palace on Tatooine, interrogation droids move to wipe the memory of a translator known as "the Talky droid" for a minor mistake. The droid protests and turns the tables, seizing control of the palace's other droids and even Jabba's guards, turning them against their master.

Meanwhile, Lando Calrissian, delighted to find his old outfits still aboard the *Millennium Falcon*, disguises himself as a bounty hunter and talks his way into the palace on the pretext that the cyborg Lobot needs urgent medical care. Inside, he learns that all the droids have "gone bad," and he passes Boba Fett gunning them down and the carbonite-frozen Han Solo on the wall before a trapdoor drops him and Lobot into a rancor's cell.

There they find the Talky droid hiding. It reveals that the palace's droids are being assimilated by a unifying foreign intelligence and demands Lando smuggle it out in one piece before it will repair Lobot. As they argue, the rancor wakes, leaving Lando and Lobot trapped between the enraged beast and the corrupted droids.
title: Star Wars (2020) #38

synopsis:
**Droid Death Trap! - A Dark Droids Tie-In!**

Lando Calrissian has finally gotten his hands on the ancient droid with the ability to save his old friend Lobot from certain death.

But before that can happen, they must escape Jabba the Hutt's palace on Tatooine, which is swarming with evil droids hungry...for droid blood!
title: Star Wars (2020) #39

synopsis:
**"Catastrophe in the Clouds!"**

Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian and Leia Organa have returned to Cloud City! They each left things on Bespin they desperately need, a weapon, a friend, and crucial information.

But the city is swarming with Imperials, under occupation by an Empire desperate to steal its resources.

Things did not go well for the trio the last time they visited this place. This time... might be even worse.
title: Star Wars (2020) #04

synopsis:
**Droid Disaster! - A Dark Droids Tie-In!**

The worst has happened.

Lobot, one of the greatest heroes the Rebel Alliance has ever known and close friend to semi-reformed scoundrel Lando Calrissian...is lost.

Lando returns to the Rebels to ask for their help...but can he convince them he's not a traitor?
title: Star Wars (2020) #40

synopsis:
**Lando Lost! - A Dark Droids Tie-In!**

With the final mission to rescue Lobot from the terrors of The Scourge underway, galactic ne'er-do-well Lando Calrissian must finally reckon with the sins of his past. Will he be the scoundrel he's always been or the hero he could be?
title: Star Wars (2020) #41

synopsis:
**Sith Rising!**

Luke Skywalker knows his destiny is leading him toward a confrontation with Darth Vader. But Vader is a Dark Lord of the Sith, and Luke is barely even a Jedi. To have any chance at survival, he must find a way to train himself in the battle techniques he will soon face. He must find...another Sith!
title: Star Wars (2020) #42

synopsis:
**A Sith in the Mind's Eye!**

Luke Skywalker is trapped deep behind enemy lines, hunted by a rogue Sith who sees the nascent Jedi Knight as his chance at new ascendancy.

How will Luke survive...and who will he be if he does?
title: Star Wars (2020) #43

synopsis:
**The Trial of Lando Calrissian!**

After the events of Dark Droids, Lando is at a crossroads. Saving his friend Lobot has cost him more than he expected as now Admiral Ackbar and Mon Mothma are putting Lando on trial. With the Rebellion locked in battle with the Empire, how will this trial's end impact the Rebellion's future?
title: Star Wars (2020) #44

synopsis:
**The Greatest Trial in the Galaxy Continues!**

Lando Calrissian is on trial for crimes against the Alliance. The evidence is clear, and his friends have turned against him, from Luke Skywalker to Leia Organa. Lando's only hope...is Darth Vader?
title: Star Wars (2020) #45

synopsis:
**Operation: Rescue Mon Mothma!**

The fate of the Rebel Alliance has become intertwined with accused traitor Lando Calrissian, if he falls, so will the Rebellion. Leia Organa must mount a desperate rescue mission if there is any hope of defeating the evil Galactic Empire!
title: Star Wars (2020) #46

synopsis:
**Lando Is All In!**

The trial of Lando Calrissian for treason against the Rebel Alliance comes down to its stunning finale!

The evidence is not in Lando's favor, nor is the fact that he's offered a full confession to the crimes of which he is accused.

Does he have any cards left to play?
title: Star Wars (2020) #47

synopsis:
**Last Chance for Solo!**

Leia Organa has put the galaxy's freedom before all else, including her lost love, Han Solo. With the growing threat of the second Death Star looming over the Rebel Alliance, should she try to save him...while she still can?
title: Star Wars (2020) #48

synopsis:
**Leia and the Rebel Alliance!**

The last remnants of Alderaan have come under threat from an old enemy! Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa could save these few survivors of the destroyed planet, but the Princess of Alderaan must choose between her people...and the Rebel Alliance itself!
title: Star Wars (2020) #49

synopsis: The next steps on Luke Skywalker's search for Jedi wisdom begin here! Following a mysterious vision he received on Cloud City, Luke travels to a lost planet looking for a woman he believes may hold the key to his Jedi future and that of the entire Rebellion. But will he arrive at his destination in time to learn what he needs... or will the evil Darth Vader get there first?
title: Star Wars (2020) #05

synopsis:
**Giant-Sized Star Wars Epic!**

A fifty-page extravaganza! Jedi Master Luke Skywalker must teach his student Ben Solo a powerful lesson about the true balance between light and dark. He offers up a tale from the days of the Rebel Alliance that touches on all eras of Star Wars history, and brings this epic run to a thrilling, incredible conclusion!
title: Star Wars (2020) #50

synopsis: The issue features the story "The Destiny Path: Part VI," in which Luke Skywalker is guided by Verla to find a new lightsaber on Tempes.
title: Star Wars (2020) #06

synopsis:
**She Will Have Revenge!**

Commander Ellian Zahra has been tasked by the terrifying Darth Vader with the job of tracking down the remnants of the Rebel Fleet, scattered since the Battle of Hoth. From the bridge of her flagship the Tarkin's Will, she hunts the galaxy, eradicating all resistance, her secret goal to destroy Leia Organa. But why such hate for the Princess of Alderaan? The truth, will be revealed!
title: Star Wars (2020) #07

synopsis:
**Action and Adventure Continues in the Post-Return of the Jedi Era!**

What secret did the Nagai pilot tell Luke Skywalker? What is the Crown of Verity? What tragedy awaits Luke on the winter planet of Gadrilim?
title: Star Wars (2025) #02

synopsis:
**Return to Cloud City!**

Han Solo, Beilert Valance and Lando Calrissian must help an old friend. Witness the long friendship of Han, Chewie and Lando in exciting flashbacks to bygone eras! And a mysterious figure from the past re-emerges using a new model cloud car!
title: Star Wars (2025) #03

synopsis:
**Leia Organa - In the Clutches of the Zantarrk Stormtroopers!**

An assassin unmasked, and it's not who you expect! The Millennium Falcon, cornered! And a message from the Clone Wars era sends shock waves in the present!
title: Star Wars (2025) #04

synopsis:
**The Past Returns!**

As Luke, Rynn, Han and Valance race to find a group of stranded traders, they uncover a startling, and deadly, relic from the past, and have to face off against a horde of battle droids! Leia and Mon Mothma face an unexpected and powerful attack! Who is the new leader of the Nagai resistance? Cornered on a desolate planet, our heroes are left with one shocking option to survive!
title: Star Wars (2025) #05

synopsis:
**New Republic on the Brink of War!**

Luke, Han and Valance face off against a swarm of Clone War-era threats! Princess Leia plays the only card she has left against the mounting threat! Rynn Zenat discovers a clue that sheds light on the struggles of the mysterious Nagai!
title: Star Wars (2025) #06

synopsis:
**Revolution!**

Han, Luke and Valance are caught in the crossfire as the Nagai uprising takes hold! A betrayal shakes the power structure of an entire sector! And a New Republic hero is stranded with nowhere to turn!
title: Star Wars (2025) #07

synopsis:
**An Epic New Adventure Begins for Luke, Leia and Han in the Wake of Return of the Jedi!**

New York Times best-selling author Alex Segura launches the bold next era of Star Wars.

Luke Skywalker must defend the New Republic from a bloodthirsty gang of mercenaries! Han Solo investigates a deadly underworld mystery, and saves a surprising ally! Leia Organa must grapple with a new alliance opposed to the goals of the New Republic!
title: Star Wars (2025) #01

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #21: The Last Flight of the Harbinger, Part I
synopsis: Marvel's epic exploration of the Star Wars galaxy continues! Take a walk on the dark side with Sgt. Kreel and an elite group of Imperial soldiers aboard the Star Destroyer Harbinger! It's a nigh-indestructible weapon of war. It's also the target of the Rebels' new top-secret plan. Never afraid of defying the odds, Luke Skywalker, Han Solo and Leia Organa are determined to make this the Harbinger's last flight! But Kreel and his SCAR Squad specialize in taking down rebels, hard! Let battle commence!
title: Star Wars (2015) #21: The Last Flight of the Harbinger, Part I

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #22: The Last Flight of the Harbinger, Part II

title: Star Wars (2015) #25: The Last Flight of the Harbinger, Part V

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #26: Yoda's Secret War, Part I
synopsis: With one member of the rebel crew captured and imprisoned, we turn once more to the journals of Ben Kenobi and a legendary adventure with Jedi Master...Yoda!
title: Star Wars (2015) #26: Yoda's Secret War, Part I

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #27: Yoda's Secret War, Part II
synopsis: With one member of the rebel crew captured and imprisoned, we turn once more to the journals of Ben Kenobi and a legendary adventure with Jedi Master...Yoda!
title: Star Wars (2015) #27: Yoda's Secret War, Part II

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #28: Yoda's Secret War, Part III
synopsis: With one member of the rebel crew captured and imprisoned, we turn once more to the journals of Ben Kenobi and a legendary adventure with Jedi Master...Yoda!

displayTitle: Star Wars (2015) #29: Yoda's Secret War, Part IV
