synopsis: News, interviews, and the latest Star Wars merchandise and publishing!
title: Insider #186

synopsis: The latest news, in-depth features, and exclusive interviews for Star Wars fans! Continue the year-long countdown to Skywalker Saga's finale, as we tackle the tumultuous events of Star Wars: Attack of the Clones. With the return of Star Wars: The Clone Wars on the horizon, we profile famous clone troopers, and ask what went wrong for the Jedi Knights as the Republic came crashing down.
title: Insider #187

synopsis: Issue 188 of Star Wars Insider continues our countdown to the closing chapter in the Skywalker Saga, with an in-depth look at the finale of the prequel trilogy-Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith! We also track the history of Star Wars board games, from Kenner's "Destroy the Death Star" to Fantasy Flight's immersive RPG; find out more about General Grievous, and bring you a special guide to getting the most out of Star Wars Celebration 2019. With all the latest news, in-depth features, and exclusive interviews, Star Wars Insider is your gateway to a galaxy far, far, away.
title: Insider #188

synopsis:
**Star Wars Celebration Special!**

Our countdown hits Episode 4: A New Hope! Explore the real-world places behind the hot wastes and wretched hives of Tatooine! Step back into 1978, as we look at the first wave of Kenner Star Wars toys! Greg Hildebrandt discusses his iconic art!
title: Insider #189

synopsis: Featuring the latest news, in-depth articles, and exclusive interviews, Star Wars Insider is your link to a galaxy far, far, away! We reach the mid-point in our exploration of the Skywalker saga, featuring exclusive new interviews with Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back bad guys Jeremy Bulloch (Boba Fett) and Julian Glover (General Veers), we reveal how Fett and his bounty hunting cohorts were created, and look at the complex relationships between heroes and father figures in Star Wars. Plus, go backstage at Star Wars Celebration Chicago, and discover Bacta-basics with the galaxy's medical tech.
title: Insider #190

synopsis: Star Wars Insider journey through the Skywalker saga reaches the climax of the original trilogy, as we examine Star Wars: Return of the Jedi! Take a sideways look at the Rebellion era through the lens of Nintendo's Super Star Wars video game trilogy, discover the bio-diversity of the galaxy, and check out the boldest Star Wars tattoos! Plus, the launch of Star Wars Insider Skywalker Saga Survey-your chance to vote for your favorite heroes, villains, events, and more from Episodes I to IX.
title: Insider #191

synopsis: The Force Awakens in The Skywalker Saga: Our ongoing journey through the Star Wars movies continues! Han Solo - A Matter of Life and Death: from Corellia to Starkiller Base, we trace the life of the reluctant good guy. False Economies of Scale: Why do the Empire and First Order keep on building big bad weapons? Bar Wars: A tour of galactic watering holes filled with scum and villainy. Plus all the latest Star Wars news, interviews, books, and merchandise.
title: Insider #192

synopsis: Our ongoing journey through the Skywalker Saga reaches its penultimate chapter! Plus: visit Ahch-To, as we make the trip to the real-life filming locations in Ireland. Discover the Secret Life of Porgs, as we find out more about their biology and how they were created for the movie. Learn the history behind some of the galaxy's more exotic names, and how you can create your own. And separate the truths from the white lies of the Star Wars universe in a special fact-or-fiction file!
title: Insider #193

synopsis: Must-read interviews, news, articles and reviews from a galaxy far, far away! This issue is the Episode IX movie special - join us as we dig in to The Rise of Skywalker, the grand climax of the Star Wars saga! Plus, we reveal the results of Insider's Skywalker Saga Survey, take a look at the Meaning of Masks from Vader to Kylo Ren, and discover Life After Skywalker - how to cope with the end of a saga!
title: Insider #194

synopsis: The best of the official Star Wars Insider magazine content, celebrating the complete Star Wars experience, from movies to books, video games to comic books, and more! Featuring rare cast and crew interviews, and exclusive behind the scenes pictures, this is an essential read for Star Wars fans of all ages.
title: Insider #195

synopsis:
**Go Behind-the-Scenes of the Latest Star Wars Film Installment, The Rise of Skywalker!**

Featuring
title: Insider #196

synopsis:
Celebrate the 40th anniversary of Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back in style, with a host of exclusive interviews and fascinating features.
This edition of Insider features
title: Insider #197

synopsis:
**The Countdown to Insider #200 Begins!**

Greg Grunberg, ace Resistance pilot Snap Wexley, opens up about his love for the saga. Elizabeth Grullon discusses her experience playing Jedi: Fallen Order's villainous Second Sister. We count down Emperor Palpatine's top 5 apprentices. Plus, all the latest news from every sector of the Star Wars galaxy!
title: Insider #198

synopsis:
Fiction is back! Star War Insider #199 features the first in an ongoing series of Star Wars: The High Republic stories, exclusive to the magazine! The first is by acclaimed Star Wars author Charles Soule, a prequel to his Star Wars: The High Republic novel!

And that's not all!
title: Insider #199

synopsis: Star Wars Insider was the official periodical Star Wars magazine and traced its lineage back to the Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter and its successor, Bantha Tracks.
title: Insider #2

synopsis: Star Wars Insider #200 is an unmissable homage to all that we love about Star Wars. Anthony Daniels writes a one-off edition of his unique 'Wonder Column' which appeared in the magazine's early issues; cast and creatives involved in the saga reveal their personal reasons for loving the galaxy far, far away; and past Insider Editors reminisce on the last 200 issues.
title: Insider #200

synopsis:
Fresh from the celebratory awesomeness of #200, we have some real treats in store for you in #201! This issue includes the first part of an all-new Star Wars: The High Republic short story from Cavan Scott! We begin our two-part guide to the first season of Star Wars: The Mandalorian – packed with fascinating facts and trivia! Joonas Suotamo, Neil Scanlan [sic], and Tom Spina uncover the behind-the-scenes story of the mighty Chewbacca, and we have an exclusive interview with Star Wars: The Clone Wars Matt Lanter.
Plus, celebrate 40 years of the original Star Wars radio adaptation; Cut Chemist, hip-hop producer and Star Wars fan explains how the saga has inspired his music, and authors Rae Carson and Michael Kogge discuss novelizing the epic final chapter of the Skywalker saga.
All this and much, much more in Star Wars Insider #201!
title: Insider #201

synopsis:
In Star Wars Insider #202 we're celebrating Star Wars fiction – starting with an exclusive interview with Timothy Zahn on the 30th anniversary of the release of his novel Star Wars: Heir to the Empire! Author Claudia Gray discusses her High Republic novel, Into the Dark and Kristin Baver reveals how she wrote the ultimate Star Wars biography, Skywalker: A Family at War. And of course, there's our exclusive fiction, part two of The High Republic: First Duty by Cavan Scott!
Droid builder Lee Towersey and R2-D2 performer Hassan Taj take us beneath the dome of the galaxy's most resourceful astromech; we go behind-the-scenes of Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy's Edge, ILMxLAB's stunning VR experience; relive the adventures of Mando and Grogo [sic] in our companion to Season Two of The Mandalorian; and much, much more!
title: Insider #202

synopsis:
Travel, as the saying goes, broadens the mind, and so to inspire some wanderlust we've put together a guide to the Californian filming locations from the original Star Wars trilogy. For those looking to go further afield, we speak to the legendary original trilogy co-producer Robert Watts about his globe-trotting adventures, scouting locations for the Star Wars saga and Indiana Jones trilogy.
Creature and special makeup effects supervisor Neal Scanlan reveals how he came out of semi-retirement to bring his creative flair to the Star Wars galaxy, and we investigate how The Mandalorian (2019–present) became an instant, worldwide pop culture phenomenon. Plus, the first part of an all-new and exclusive The High Republic story by Justina Ireland!
All this and much more in Star Wars Insider #203!
title: Insider #203

synopsis: This issue of Star Wars Insider is spectacularly era-encompassing, from the first part of our exclusive interview with prequel producer Rick McCallum to our summary of the top ten life lessons we learned from The Rise of Skywalker (2019). We also speak to Bonnie Piesse, soon to reprise her role as Beru Lars for the upcoming Obi-Wan Kenobi series, about her experiences filming the prequels. And with the latest instalment [sic] of our exclusive The High Republic fiction from Justina Ireland, every day is Star Wars Day!
title: Insider #204

synopsis:
**Interview with Paul Sun-Hyung Lee (Star Wars: The Mandalorian)**

Features brand new Star Wars: The High Republic exclusive fiction.

Discover how a new audience reacted to seeing Star Wars: A New Hope for the first time!

Plus much more!
title: Insider #205

synopsis:
This issue focuses on the best of Boba Fett.

Features an exclusive interview with Temuera Morrison!

**Lucasfilm at 50** - Writer Kristin Baver looks back on five decades of amazing adventures. Includes brand new and exclusive Star Wars: The High Republic fiction. Plus much more!
title: Insider #206

synopsis:
A long time ago (in 1971), George Lucas founded Lucasfilm, the motion picture company that celebrates its 50th anniversary on December 10, 2021. In doing so, he joined a long line of cinematic innovators that began with the Lumière brothers in 1895 with their groundbreaking invention, the Cinématographe, that had people queueing around the block for a glimpse at the wonder of moving pictures.

With such an auspicious occasion to commemorate, this issue of Star Wars Insider brings you a fascinating account of the company's 50-year story from Lucasfilm historians Pete Vilmur and Lucas O. Seastrom, but that's not the only anniversary we're celebrating. We trace the unique history and impact of Star Wars: The Old Republic, the MMORPG that arrived a decade ago and is still going strong. We also speak to Drew Struzan about his iconic Star Wars poster art, get codebreaking with Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (2019) actor Dominic Monaghan, and find out why a part in The Mandalorian (2019-present) was such a big deal for guest star Diana Lee Inosanto.

Plus, all-new and exclusive Star Wars: The High Republic fiction with part one of 'Shadows Remain' by Justina Ireland!
title: Insider #207

synopsis:
In this issue, Insider has the privilege of speaking with several members of the ILM VFX art department to learn how they turn one-line ideas into believable worlds, creatures, and vehicles for the galaxy far, far away.

The High Republic author Daniel José Older tells us about his upcoming novel, Midnight Horizon; actor Dame Harriet Walter talks about her time on the set of Star Wars: The Force Awakens (2015); we explore the story of Bo-Katan Kryze, the Mandalorian warrior sworn to recover the Darksaber and rule her people; and we reach the shattering conclusion of our The High Republic Starlight short fiction series with part two of Justina Ireland's "Shadows Remain." But don't worry, there are many more stories yet to come!
title: Insider #208

synopsis:
This issue of Star Wars Insider is all about the criminal underworld of the galaxy far, far away, from the lawmakers and lawbreakers of the saga to the insidious evil of Kessel spice.

Jabba himself weighs large in these pages (and that's not a slight on his size, there'll be no body-shaming here, even of ultra-violent gangsters!), and Insider was lucky enough to speak to the trio of puppeteers who brought the sinister slug to life in Return of the Jedi (1983). There's an exclusive interview with Emily Swallow, The Mandalorian Armorer, and there's all-new and exclusive short fiction starring bounty hunters Zam Wesell and Jango Fett. It would be a crime not to start reading right away!
title: Insider #209

synopsis:
Welcome to Star Wars Insider #210
This issue celebrates the 20th anniversary of the release of Star Wars: Attack of the Clones (2002)! Insider speaks to Rena Owen, the actor who played Kaminoan cloner Taun We, about the making of the movie and her recent return in The Bad Batch (2021-present), and takes a look at how Attack of the Clones drew upon medieval storytelling for the epic romance between Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala. There's also an exclusive interview with prequel concept artist Iain McCaig, including some inspirational advice for budding Star Wars artists.
Plus, get the lowdown on Star Wars: Galactic Starcruiser from Disney's top Imagineers, explore every episode of Star Wars: Visions (2021), and General Grievous is back in a thrilling and exclusive new story by Richard Dinnick!
May the Fourth be With You!
title: Insider #210

synopsis:
**With a New Adventure for Obi-Wan Kenobi Coming to Disney+ in 2022, We Trace the Major Events in the Life of the Jedi Knight Turned Tatooine Hermit.**

The Academy Award winning set decorator Roger Christian talks about his work on Star Wars: A New Hope!
title: Insider #211

synopsis: In this issue of Star Wars Insider, there's an exclusive interview with Carey Jones, the actor inside the Krrsantan costume in The Book of Boba Fett (2021), and we also take a speculative look at how life on Endor might have been changed for the Ewoks by the presence of the Empire. Other highlights include interviews with Hugh Quarshie (Captain Panaka in 1999's The Phantom Menace), Star Wars: Shadow of the Sith author Adam Christopher, and Industrial Light & Magic matte painting veteran Craig Barron. Plus, there's exclusive new fiction by Rodney Barnes starring Jedi Master Mace Windu.
title: Insider #212

synopsis:
Twenty-five years ago, on a ranch not so far from San Francisco, a famous filmmaker decided he hadn't quite finished with that space movie he'd released two decades previously. It could do with a spruce up, to fix a few shots he'd never been fully happy with and to ensure the film would be enjoyed in tip-top condition for generations to come. And while he was at it, he might as well add a little stardust to its two sequels too! So began the saga of the Star Wars Special Editions. It was a big job for those involved, and this issue we celebrate their dedication to the task with an in-depth chat with some of the creatives that made it possible.
We'll also be taking you on a tour of Great Britain's Star Wars filming locations, retrace the turning points in Obi-Wan Kenobi's life from Padawan to Force spirit, and you're certain to enjoy our all-new short story by top Star Wars author George Mann featuring everyone's favorite galactic chef, Dexter Jettster.
title: Insider #213

synopsis:
Darth Vader is probably the angriest character in any science fiction and fantasy franchise. Would a few anger management sessions have helped? Too little, too late? After all, the flaws in his temperament were brewing even when he was going by the name of Anakin Skywalker and getting into fights with young Rodians on Tatooine. In this issue of Star Wars Insider, we'll examine what we thought we knew about Darth Vader through the lens of the character's deeds in the Kenobi series, take a wider look at the events of the show in general, and discuss the perils of being chosen to train as a Jedi in the first place.
Plus, we discover how much fun it can be to play a Jawa in a Star Wars production; speak to Jim Swearingen, the design guru behind Kenner's original toy range; and return to The High Republic with all-new short fiction from George Mann.
title: Insider #214

synopsis: In this issue of Star Wars Insider, we investigate how Beru and Owen Lars, a modest couple from Tatooine, coped with the twin demands of protecting Luke Skywalker from the Empire and an even more daunting task: parenthood! We also speak to Troy Kotsur, who played a Tusken Raider in The Mandalorian and developed the galactic sign language Din Djarin used to communicate with them, and chat to Obi-Wan Kenobi composer Natalie Holt about scoring the series. Actor Guy Henry reveals how he became Grand Moff Tarkin in Rogue One: A Star Wars Story, and droid and creature performer Chris Bartless talks about his on-set experiences on The Mandalorian and The Book of Boba Fett. Plus, all-new and exclusive The High Republic fiction from George Mann!
title: Insider #215

synopsis: The 194th issue of Titan Magazines' Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published on February 7, 2023.
title: Insider #216

synopsis: In #217 we begin our celebrations of the 40th anniversary of the original trilogy's epic conclusion. We explore how Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983) seeded themes, ideas, and plots that have unfolded in movies and television series ever since. Speaking of which, we also have interviews with Giancarlo Esposito (Moff Gideon in The Mandalorian), Grant Feely (Luke Skywalker in Obi-Wan Kenobi), and Industrial Light and Magic's illustrious special-effects wizard John Knoll. What with our Andor Season 1 companion, and behind-the-scenes chat with the authors of Star Wars: Timelines, there's a lot packed into this 100-page issue!
title: Insider #217

synopsis: The 196th issue of Titan Magazines' Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published on May 2, 2023.
title: Insider #218

synopsis:
Here at Star Wars Insider we're huge fans of Lucasfilm Animation's Star Wars: The Bad Batch (2021-), so in issue 219 we go behind the scenes to learn how an episode of this incredible series is made, from initial story meetings to final edits. And it's such a complex process that we could only fit in half of the story into this issue!
Among the other delights you'll find in this issue, we talk to Phil Szostack about the history of The Art of Star Wars books, meet some of the saga's Twi'leks, and The High Republic author Zoraida Córdova tells us about her Star Wars novels. Plus, we end our Phase II short fiction with a final Tales of Enlightenment story from George Mann.
title: Insider #219

synopsis: Star Wars Insider was the official periodical Star Wars magazine and traced its lineage back to the Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter and its successor, Bantha Tracks.
title: Insider #22

synopsis:
Once upon a long time ago, when we used to talk about Return of the Jedi (1983) we were only talking about the film itself. These days, however, it seems there are Jedi returning all over the place!
First there was Luke Skywalker, taking on bad guys before gaining a little green student in Star Wars: The Mandalorian. Then Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi filled in some of the blanks in that crazy old wizard's wilderness years. And now Star Wars: Ahsoka brings another former Jedi back into action, with Rosario Dawson taking center stage in the new Disney+ series.
In this issue of Insider, we speak to the live-action Ahsoka Tano and it's clear to see the actor's joy in playing the much-loved character. We also chat to comic-book legend Walt Simonson, who wrote or drew (or both) some of the most memorable Star Wars stories in the original Marvel Comics series, and we catch up with The High Republic author Lydia Kang, who will soon be writing Phase III short fiction for this very magazine!
And talking of short fiction, this issue see the first in a trilogy of stories set around the events of Return of the Jedi, beginning with a tale of great loss in a moment of sweet victory from acclaimed Star Wars author Alexander Freed.
All this, and much, much more, in Insider #220!
title: Insider #220

synopsis:
In this issue of Insider, we celebrate the space moms and mother figures of Star Wars by exploring the trials, tribulations, and sacrifices they made for their (sometimes ungrateful, we're looking at you, Ben Solo!) younglings.

Insider went behind the scenes of Star Wars Celebration Europe 2023's LIVE! Stream to discover how the Lucasfilm Online team put on such an entertaining show. We also talk to designers of Kenner's original toy line and its revival in the form of Hasbro's Retro Collection. But that's not all: Industrial Light & Magic legends Dennis Muren and Ken Ralston tell us about their work on Return of the Jedi, while Skywalker Sound's David Collins talks about galactic soundscapes of the modern era, and The High Republic author Tessa Gratton reveals her Star Wars story.

Plus, "Lost in the Woods," an all-new and exclusive original trilogy story from S.T. Bende!
title: Insider #221

synopsis:
The big news this issue is the return of The High Republic short fiction to Insider, and we welcome author Lydia Kang to the fold with the first part of her new run of stories – illustrated by Star Wars concept artist Jake Lunt Davies! – that kick off Phase III of the Lucasfilm Publishing initiative. There's also the final story in our Star Wars: Return of the Jedi -adjacent "Galactic Tales," and this time the focus falls on some desperate inhabitants of Jabba the Hutt's palace in an all-new story by Richard Dinnick.
This issue we speak to Admiral Ackbar himself, puppeteer Tim Rose, who performed the character in Return of the Jedi. There's a fascinating interview with Star Wars: Ahsoka composer, Kevin Kiner, and his collaborators (and children) Sean and Deana. Plus, an indispensable The Mandalorian season three companion and the concluding part of our interview with Industrial Light & Magic's Dennis Muren and Ken Ralston.
title: Insider #222

synopsis:
In #223, in preparation for the final series – coming to Disney+ in the New Year – we catch up with the events of Season Two of The Bad Batch, that left Clone Force 99 down another member, and Omega in the hands of the Empire! To lighten the mood, we get glamorous with a feature on the couture of the galaxy far, far, away, reviewing the Star Wars fashions that are a long way from the used aesthetic of your average scruffy-looking, nerf-herding scoundrel. We interview Tait Fletcher, the performer inside the Paz Vizsla armor in The Mandalorian, and find out about the art of modelmaking from former Industrial Light & Magic expert Fon Davis.
Plus, the latest installment of our The High Republic short fiction series, and an investigation into why the Emperor's Death Star stratagem failed so spectacularly in Return of the Jedi!
title: Insider #223

synopsis:
In this issue of Star Wars Insider we profile the kind of individuals who either strove to rule the galaxy far, far, away; were thrust into such a position; or struck their own paths leading crime gangs and other nefarious groups. On a lighter note, we get arty with creature concept designer Jake Lunt Davies; meet Ethan Sacks, the writer behind Marvel Comics' thrilling Bounty Hunter series; and get the Max Rebo Band back together to remember their heady days on the set of Return of the Jedi as the biggest musical act in the galaxy.
We also begin a two-part retrospective of The Bad Batch with the creative team behind its three-season run, and speak to the showrunner and writers of Young Jedi Adventures. Plus, the Adrens board a derelict Nihil ship in an all-new and exclusive The High Republic short story from Lydia Kang!
title: Insider #224

synopsis:
May the 4th be with You!
2024 is the 25th anniversary of when it all came storming back into our lives. Star Wars: The Phantom Menace arrived in theaters on May 19, 1999, and this issue we remember the incredible expectation and excitement that swept over fans ahead of the movie's release. Elsewhere in this issue, we take another trip into the past to explore the history of Star Tours, the Disney Parks attraction that has been taking guests on breakneck voyages to the galaxy far, far away since 1987. Plus, we have an interview with the legendary Billy Dee Williams, the actor who lent Lando Calrissian his suave sophistication and sense of style.
title: Insider #225

synopsis:
This issue we're celebrating a quarter century of LEGO Star Wars with an in depth look at how the range was originally devised, and the ins and outs of creating the very latest sets. We also explore the natural history of purrgil, the hyperspace traveling space whales recently seen in Star Wars: Ahsoka. We talk to author Alexander Freed about his storytelling contributions to Star Wars, and actor Amanda Lawrence about her role as Commander Larma D'Acy in the sequel trilogy.

Plus, we talk to Leslye Headland, the creator of Star Wars: The Acolyte, about the incredible new series currently streaming on Disney+, and there's all-new and exclusive The High Republic fiction by Lydia Kang.
title: Insider #226

synopsis: This issue we're delighted to speak to the actor who turned a bit part into a Star Wars legend, Denis Lawson! We also talk to Dee Bradley Baker, who voiced countless clone troopers including Rex, the ultimate clone sidekick to several Jedi; while Meredith Salenger tells us about reprising her outsider role as Bariss Offee in Tales of the Empire. Star Wars: Skeleton Crew showrunners Jon Watts and Christopher Ford introduce us to an all-new bunch of heroes. Plus, we celebrate 40 years of Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure, and all new The High Republic fiction from Lydia Kang.
title: Insider #229

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 23 bills itself as the premiere issue of Star Wars Insider, as the magazine was previously known as The Lucasfilm Fan Club Magazine for issues 1 through 22. Issue 23 was published in Fall 1994.
title: Insider #23

synopsis:
Welcome to the first issue of Star Wars Insider for 2025!
Anthony Daniels recently hosted a revival of Star Wars: A Musical Journey for several nights at the Walt Disney Concert Hall in Los Angeles. In this issue of Insider we speak with the C-3PO actor about the original concert series, its new iteration, and his personal thoughts on Williams' incredible music.
Also in this issue, Joonas Suotamo tells us about shooting his big action scene as Kelnacca in Star Wars: The Acolyte; the effects artists at Tippett Studios reveal how they created a stop-motion creature for Star Wars: Skeleton Crew; and Star Wars: A New Hope set decorator Roger Christian details how some of Star Wars most iconic objects came to be. All this and exclusive The High Republic fiction from Lydia Kang too!
title: Insider #230

synopsis:
John Knoll and John Goodson discuss bringing traditional visual effects skills to the delightful Star Wars: The Skeleton Crew
Star Wars: Visions executive producer James Waugh talks about Lucasfilm Animation’s collaboration with some of the finest filmmakers on the planet
and Tom Spina writes about the art of concept maquettes and the artists behind them.
Not to mention the penultimate installment of The High Republic short fiction, an interview with Charles Soule, and a preview of Andor Season Two.
title: Insider #231

synopsis:
News, exclusive fiction, and more!

**The Force of Sport!** Former wrestler Arda Ocal explores the ties between Star Wars and the world of sports.

**Combat Ready or Clankers?** Historian Dr. Chris Kempshall discovers just how effective the Trade Federation's battle droids really were.

**Exclusive Star Wars: The High Republic Fiction:** The epic finale of Tales from the Occlusion Zone!
title: Insider #232

synopsis:
It's Halloween season, so who better to place front and center in this issue than the Dark Lord of the Sith himself, as we look forward to the release of Adam Christopher's new novel Master of Evil with an author interview and an exclusive extract from the book. The novelization of Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith is also getting a deluxe re-release this year, and we talk with author Matthew Stover about his much-loved adaptation.

On the lighter side, Star Wars: Skeleton Crews Robert Timothy Smith recalls the fun experience playing the blue Myykian Neel in the Disney+ series, and artist John R. Mullaney reveals how his stunning cross-section illustrations for DK's Star Wars reference books are made.

There's also an exciting new short story by Landry Walker featuring the Crimson Corsair, a teaser for next issue's celebration of the tenth(!) anniversary of Star Wars: The Force Awakens. Time flies, especially in hyperspace!
title: Insider #234

synopsis:
News, exclusive fiction, and more!

**Featuring:** A celebration of the 10th anniversary of The Force Awakens with
- An exclusive look at Industrial Light & Magic's special effects
- An original fiction tie-in story

**Plus:** Interviews with Skeleton Crew's Ryan Kiera Armstrong and Andor's Robert Emms.

**And:** Andor Season 2 composer Brandon Roberts.
title: Insider #235

synopsis:
News, exclusive fiction, and more!

**Featuring:**

- **Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord!** Sam Witwer discusses his return as the villainous former Sith.
- **Rogue One: A Star Wars Story 10th Anniversary!** Gareth Edwards, John Knoll, and Garry Whitta talk about the making of the movie.
- **Interviews:** Andor and Rogue One's Alistair Petrie (General Draven) and LEGO Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy director Chris Buckley.
- **Exclusive Star Wars short fiction** by Lucasfilm's Phil Szostak.
title: Insider #236

synopsis:
**The Final Ever Issue of Star Wars Insider**

Inside this issue
- **Kathleen Kennedy:** Exclusive interview with the departing Lucasfilm President!
- The Mandalorian and Grogu preview
- The SFX of Star Wars
- **Interviews:** Andor editor John Gilroy, and the Star Wars creature shop's Neal Scanlan on Andor Season Two
- Exclusive Star Wars short fiction!
title: Insider #237

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 24 is the twenty-fourth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in December 1994.
title: Insider #24

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 25 is the twenty-fifth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Spring 1995.
title: Insider #25

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 26 is the twenty-sixth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in July 1995.
title: Insider #26

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 27 is the twenty-seventh issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Fall 1995.
title: Insider #27

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 28 is the twenty-eighth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Winter 1996.
title: Insider #28

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 29 is the twenty-ninth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Spring 1996.
title: Insider #29

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 30 was published in Summer 1996. This is the first issue to feature the writing of Scott Chernoff, who would become a fixture for the next 30 issues and go on to become the magazine's Managing Editor.
title: Insider #30

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 31 is the thirty-first issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Fall 1996.
title: Insider #31

synopsis: Published in Winter 1997, issue 32 celebrated the theatrical release of The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition as well as the twentieth anniversary of the Star Wars franchise. This issue unveiled a new design for the magazine, including a new Star Wars Insider logo, which lasted through issue 76 in Spring 2004.
title: Insider #32

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 33 is the thirty-third issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in Spring 1997.
title: Insider #33

synopsis: Published in Summer 1997, issue 34 marked the conclusion of Star Wars Insider's original quarterly publication schedule; with issue 35, the magazine began publishing bi-monthly.
title: Insider #34

synopsis: Issue 35 was the first published by Fantastic Media and was released in September 1997. Starting with issue 35, Star Wars Insider began bimonthly publication. Issue 35 was also the first Star Wars Insider issue to offer multiple cover variants to readers: a newsstand version featuring a Tusken Raider and a subscriber-exclusive version featuring a TIE fighter pilot.
title: Insider #35

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 36 is the thirty-sixth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine, published in January 1998.
title: Insider #36

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 37 is the thirty-seventh issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in April 1998.
title: Insider #37

synopsis: Published in June/July 1998, this issue is notable as the first in which regular writer Scott Chernoff became Managing Editor.
title: Insider #38

synopsis: Published in August/September 1998, issue 39 features interviews with Jake Lloyd, Warwick Davis, Kenny Baker, and Doug Chiang.
title: Insider #39

synopsis: Published in October/November 1998, two cover variants were available for issue 40: a newsstand version featuring a Y-wing battle and a subscriber-exclusive cover featuring a battle droid.
title: Insider #40

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 41 is the forty-first issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in December 1998.
title: Insider #41

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 42 is the forty-second issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in February 1999.
title: Insider #42

synopsis: Published in April 1999, three cover variants were available for issue 43: a newsstand version featuring Qui-Gon Jinn, a subscriber-exclusive version featuring Darth Maul, and a Previews Exclusive version similar to the subscriber version but with different coloring for the Star Wars Insider logo.
title: Insider #43

synopsis: Published in June/July 1999, the barcode on the cover mislabeled this issue as issue 39.
title: Insider #44

synopsis: Published in August 1999, two cover variants were available for issue 45: a newsstand version featuring the Naboo battle scene and a subscriber-exclusive cover featuring bounty hunter Aurra Sing.
title: Insider #45

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 46 is the forty-sixth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was published in October 1999.
title: Insider #46

synopsis: Published in January 2000, issue 47 featured the first "flesh and blood" image of Star Wars Legends character Mara Jade, as portrayed by model Shannon McRandle for Decipher's Star Wars Customizable Card Game.
title: Insider #47

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 48 is the forty-eighth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands on April 4, 2000.
title: Insider #48

synopsis: Released to newsstands the week of May 15, 2000, this issue celebrated the twentieth anniversary of the original trilogy film Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back. As such, the design for issue 49 took its inspiration from the 1980 publication Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back - Official Collectors Edition.
title: Insider #49

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 56 was released to newsstands on October 2, 2001. Issue 56 was the last produced by Christopher Perkins, who had taken over as Star Wars Insider chief editor with issue 55. This issue featured a reverse flip cover on the back of the magazine with related content focusing on filmmaker Kevin Smith, known for his role in the character duo Jay and Silent Bob.
title: Insider #5

synopsis: Released to newsstands in July 2000, issue 50 introduced actor Hayden Christensen as the new Anakin Skywalker in the Star Wars prequel trilogy.
title: Insider #50

synopsis: Issue 51 was the first published under the new direction of Wizards of the Coast and the last produced by former Star Wars Fan Club president Dan Madsen, who oversaw the creation of Star Wars Insider in 1987 under the title The Lucasfilm Fan Club Magazine. It's also the last issue for longtime Editor-in-Chief Jon Bradley Snyder. Managing Editor Scott Chernoff, who had been running the magazine, stayed on for a while but stepped back to Contributing Editor status after this issue.
title: Insider #51

synopsis: Issue 52 was the first produced by editor Jeff Quick, following the departure of Dan Madsen, the original Star Wars Insider publisher and former president of The Official Star Wars Fan Club, and longtime Editor-in-Chief Jon Bradley Snyder. At the same time, longtime Managing Editor Scott Chernoff, who had been overseeing the magazine, offered some continuity to the transition, becoming a Contributing Editor. Issue 52 was published on February 27, 2001.
title: Insider #52

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 53 is the fifty-third issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands in April 2001.
title: Insider #53

synopsis: Released to newsstands on June 14, 2001, this marked the final Star Wars Insider issue produced by Jeff Quick, who had assumed chief editorial duties of the magazine with issue 52 upon Wizards of the Coast becoming the new publisher of Star Wars Insider.
title: Insider #54

synopsis: Released to newsstands on August 7, 2001, issue 55 was the first overseen by chief editor Christopher Perkins, who had previously served the magazine as Senior Editor beginning with issue 53. This issue came packaged with an exclusive Obi-Wan Kenobi trading card from the Topps trading card set 2001 Topps Star Wars Evolution.
title: Insider #55

synopsis: Released to newsstands on October 2, 2001, issue 56 was the last produced by Christopher Perkins, who had taken over as Star Wars Insider chief editor with issue 55. This issue featured a reverse flip cover on the back of the magazine with related content focusing on filmmaker Kevin Smith, known for his role in the character duo Jay and Silent Bob.
title: Insider #56

synopsis: Released to newsstands on December 4, 2001, issue 57 was the first overseen by chief editor Dave Gross.
title: Insider #57

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 58 is the fifty-eighth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands on February 5, 2002.
title: Insider #58

synopsis: Released to newsstands on April 2, 2002, this issue celebrated the twenty-fifth anniversary of the Star Wars franchise in the lead-up to the theatrical release of the prequel trilogy film Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones.
title: Insider #59

synopsis: Released to newsstands on June 4, 2002, this issue came packaged with promotional trading cards for the Wizards of the Coast Attack of the Clones trading card game and the Topps Attack of the Clones silver foil-stamped movie card set, while the newsstand version was available in an exclusive polybag. It's also the last issue to feature the writing of longtime contributor (and former Managing Editor) Scott Chernoff, who left to pursue a career in writing for television.
title: Insider #60

synopsis: Released to newsstands on August 6, 2002, this was the final Star Wars Insider issue published by Wizards of the Coast.
title: Insider #61

synopsis: Released to newsstands in October 2002, this marked the first Star Wars Insider issue published by Paizo Publishing as well as the start of the magazine being released eight times a year, an increase from Star Wars Insider's former bimonthly publication schedule, which began with issue 35 in September 1997. Issue 62 came packaged with an exclusive Topps trading card from the set 2002 Topps Star Wars: Attack of the Clones Widevision.
title: Insider #62

synopsis: Released to newsstands on November 5, 2002, this issue came packaged with an exclusive Luke Skywalker card from Wizards of the Coast's Star Wars Trading Card Game.
title: Insider #63

synopsis: Released to newsstands on December 3, 2002, issue 64 marked the return of Bantha Tracks, the official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter, which was published quarterly on the back side of subscriber-exclusive editions of Star Wars Insider through issue 75.
title: Insider #64

synopsis: Released to newsstands on January 21, 2003, the subscriber-exclusive edition of issue 65 included the second volume of Bantha Tracks, the Star Wars Fan Club newsletter. This issue also came packaged with a fold-out map of the Star Wars galaxy.
title: Insider #65

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 66 is the sixty-sixth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands on March 4, 2003.
title: Insider #66

synopsis: Released to newsstands on April 15, 2003, the subscriber-exclusive edition of issue 67 included the third volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter. This issue also came packaged with a special poster to commemorate the twentieth anniversary of the original trilogy film Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi.
title: Insider #67

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 68 is the sixty-eighth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands on June 3, 2003.
title: Insider #68

synopsis: Released to newsstands in July 2003, the subscriber-exclusive edition of issue 69 included the fourth volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter.
title: Insider #69

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 71 was released to newsstands in October 2003. The subscriber-exclusive edition of issue 71 included the fifth volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter.
title: Insider #7

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 70 is the seventieth issue of Star Wars Insider magazine. It was released to newsstands in September 2003.
title: Insider #70

synopsis: Released to newsstands in October 2003. The subscriber-exclusive edition included the fifth volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter.
title: Insider #71

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 72, released to newsstands on December 2, 2003.
title: Insider #72

synopsis: Released to newsstands on January 13, 2004. The subscriber-exclusive edition included the sixth volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter.
title: Insider #73

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 74, released to newsstands on February 24, 2004.
title: Insider #74

synopsis: Released to newsstands on April 6, 2004. The subscriber-exclusive edition included the seventh volume of Bantha Tracks, the Official Star Wars Fan Club newsletter.
title: Insider #75

synopsis: Released to newsstands on May 18, 2004. This was the final Star Wars Insider issue published by Paizo Publishing.
title: Insider #76

synopsis: Released to newsstands on August 3, 2004. This marked the first Star Wars Insider issue published by IDG Entertainment as well as the first overseen by chief editor Brett Rector.
title: Insider #77

synopsis: Released to newsstands on October 5, 2004. This issue came packaged with promotional card P1 from the Topps trading card set 2004 Topps Star Wars Heritage.
title: Insider #78

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 79 was the seventy-ninth issue released of Star Wars Insider. It was released in January and February in 2005 under two separate covers.
title: Insider #79

title: Insider #80

synopsis: Released in March 2005. It started shipping to subscribers on March 11, and began shipping to newsstands on March 28.

title: Insider #85

synopsis: It began shipping to subscribers the week of February 13, and shipped to newsstands on February 21, 2006. It saw the departure of editor-in-chief Brett Rector and associate art director Gregory Harsh, with Frank Parisi taking over as editor in the next issue.
title: Insider #86

synopsis: Star Wars Insider 87, released in April 2006.
title: Insider #87

synopsis: Released in mid-June 2006. This issue centers around the Sith and the dark side of the Force. It came in four different "Special Sith Issue" covers by Aaron McBride and Christian Alzmann, each featuring a different Sith Lord. Almost all of the pages were black as opposed to the normal white.
title: Insider #88

title: Insider #90
