displayTitle: X-Wing vs Tie Fighter - Balance of Power Campaigns
synopsis: Real-time against real pilots; this is Star Wars space combat as it was meant to be! Featuring challenging real-time dogfights of 2 to 8 players, Star Wars™: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter is one of the most historically significant space combat simulators ever made.
title: Star Wars X-Wing vs Tie Fighter - Balance of Power Campaigns

synopsis: Star Wars: Yoda Stories is a 1997 Star Wars Legends video game developed by LucasArts for PC and Game Boy Color. It was preceded by Indiana Jones and His Desktop Adventures, and no further Desktop Adventures titles were released.
title: Star Wars: Yoda Stories

synopsis: Star Wars: Yoda's Challenge Activity Center is a video game made by Lucas Learning in 1999. The premise was that after the Battle of Naboo, many things were left to accomplish. The player is a promising kid brought to Yoda by Obi-Wan Kenobi, aiding in a quest to complete the tasks.
title: Star Wars: Yoda's Challenge Activity Center

synopsis: Command an elite squad through a gritty and authentic story in Star Wars Zero Company, a single-player turn-based tactics game set in the twilight of the Clone Wars.
title: Star Wars Zero Company

synopsis: This issue, meet Jedi Yoshi Raph-Elan. On his way to a meeting of Jedi, Yoshi is attacked by an unknown enemy. Stranded without even a lightsaber, he must find out what this new threat wants. Along the way, he meets the beautiful princess Lourdes, and learns a lesson about trusting ancient prophecies. And it's all brought to you by Doug Petrie (Buffy: Ring of Fire and writer for the Buffy TVS TV series), Randy Green (Witchblade), and Andy Owens (Buffy and Angel).
title: Republic #27: Starcrash

synopsis: An original story set approximately five years after the events of Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.
title: Starfighter

synopsis: Star Wars: Starfighter: Crossbones was a three-issue series of comics published by Dark Horse Comics between January 9, 2002 and March 13, 2002. It is set in 24 BBY. This spin-off was based on the successful video game Star Wars: Starfighter. This series has not been collected as a trade paperback, but was eventually collected in Star Wars Omnibus: Menace Revealed. It tells the story of the pirate captain Nym and his hunt of his nemesis Sol Sixxa on the planet Maramere.
title: Starfighter: Crossbones

synopsis: Following on the heels of LucasArts' Starfighter video game comes Crossbones, a limited series featuring the intimidating pirate captain Nym in his own solo adventure. The story begins eight years after the Battle of Naboo and Nym is down and out on Rodia. In the hopes of earning some quick credits, Nym agrees to hunt down Sol Sixxa, a pirate terrorizing the watery world of Maramere. But first, Nym must lead a rag-tag Rodian tribe to victory in a violent clan war...
title: Starfighter: Crossbones #1

synopsis: The solo adventure featuring Nym, the rowdy alien mercenary from LucasArts' Starfighter video game, continues in part two of the Crossbones limited series. After accepting a mission to hunt down the deadly pirate Sol Sixxa, Nym travels to the planet Maramere—located in the heart of a massive Trade Federation operation. After dodging the Trade Federation, Nym does a bit of wreck diving in Maramere's watery depths, searching for clues to Sol Sixxa's whereabouts...
title: Starfighter: Crossbones #2

synopsis: In the conclusion of the three-part series, mercenary alien Nym confronts the murderous villain Sol Sixxa in an epic ship-to-ship battle on the oceans of Maramere. High-seas action, Star Wars-style, culminates in a fight to the death. But when the Trade Federation arrives, will either pirate survive?
title: Starfighter: Crossbones #3

displayTitle: Insider: The High Republic: Starlight Stories
synopsis: High Republic-era short tales told from the satellite station Starlight Beacon, centuries before the films at the height of the Jedi Order; gathers the Insider "Starlight" shorts for the first time with illustrations and architect interviews.
title: Star Wars Insider: The High Republic: Starlight Stories

synopsis: "First Duty" is a 2 part High Republic story written by Cavan Scott that was published in Star Wars Insider #201 and #202.
title: Starlight: First Duty

synopsis: "Hidden Danger" is a 2 part High Republic story written by Justina Ireland that was published in Star Wars Insider #203 and #204.
title: Starlight: Hidden Danger

synopsis: "Starlight: Go Together" is a 2 part short story High Republic story written by Charles Soule that was published in Star Wars Insider #199 and #200.
title: Starlight: Go Together

synopsis: "Past Mistakes" is a 2 part High Republic story written by Cavan Scott that was published in Star Wars Insider #205 and #206.
title: Starlight: Past Mistakes

synopsis: "Starlight: Shadows Remain" is a short story written by Justina Ireland as part of the Star Wars: The High Republic publishing initiative. It is the final chapter in Phase 1 of The High Republic. The first part was published in Star Wars Insider 207 on December 14, 2021, while the second will release in Star Wars Insider 208 on January 18, 2022.
title: Starlight: Shadows Remain

synopsis: An anthology filled with original stories chronicling the epic confrontations between the Jedi and Sith.
title: Stories of Jedi and Sith

synopsis: "Suited" is a short comic story published in Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.10 and later in Star Wars: The Clone Wars Magazine 3.
title: Suited

synopsis: Sun Star Against Shadow Stone was a children's book based on the Ewoks animated series episode of similar name. Published by Dragon Picture Books in the UK in September 1987, the book was illustrated with images from the actual episode. The title on the book reads "3. Sun Star Against Shadow Stone", indicating it is the third book in the series. The title of the book is also different in that Sunstar and Shadowstone are broken to be two words instead of one.
title: Sun Star Against Shadow Stone

synopsis: Super Star Wars is the first of a series of three Super Nintendo games based on the original three films of the Star Wars series, released November 30, 1992. In 2009 the game was released on the Wii's Virtual Console and on November 17, 2015, it was ported to PlayStation 4 and Vita. The term Super Star Wars can refer to the first game or the entire series.
title: Super Star Wars

displayTitle: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi
synopsis: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi, sometimes called Super Return of the Jedi, is a platform game for the Super Nintendo Entertainment System (Super Famicom in Japan) and Nintendo Game Boy which follows Super Star Wars and Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back. There is also a simplified version for the Sega Game Gear portable system.
title: Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi

displayTitle: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back
synopsis: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back, sometimes called Super The Empire Strikes Back or simply Super Empire Strikes Back is a platform game for the Super Nintendo (Super Famicom in Japan) which follows Super Star Wars and precedes Super Star Wars: Return of the Jedi. It is largely based on the second film of the original Star Wars trilogy, Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back.
title: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

synopsis: "Surface Details" is a short story published in the developer blog for Star Wars: The Old Republic on August 14, 2014. In it, a Selkath customs official Varko working at the Mercantile Plaza on Manaan unknowingly provides information to Theron Shan and Lana Beniko on the movement of Colonel Darrok and Darth Arkous. The short story is a prelude to the Manaan Flashpoint released as part on Update 2.9 on August 19, which itself is part of the Forged Alliances story arc.
title: Surface Details

synopsis:
The story opens with a meeting between Talon Karrde and Luke and Mara Jade Skywalker. Karrde's people had intercepted an urgent transmission addressed to Luke, sent from Admiral Voss Parck on the planet Nirauan, but the message was stolen by one of Karrde's own operatives, Dean Jinzler. Fearing the transmission might be connected to the unknown menace Parck and Baron Soontir Fel had warned them of years before, Luke and Mara travel to Nirauan.

There they learn the message actually came from the Chiss Aristocra Chaf'orm'bintrano, called Formbi. The Chiss have located the remains of *Outbound Flight*, the pioneering Jedi expedition destroyed decades earlier by Grand Admiral Thrawn, and wish to hand their find over to the New Republic. Luke and Mara join an odd delegation aboard the *Chaf Envoy*: a squad of stormtroopers, a remnant of an alien people called the Geroons who claim a debt to those aboard Outbound Flight, and a man posing as a New Republic ambassador. As they voyage deeper into Chiss space through a treacherous star cluster known as the Redoubt, mounting sabotage and theft deepen the Skywalkers' unease, and Mara feels torn between her loyalty to the New Republic and her respect for the Imperial Remnant.

When the expedition reaches the wreck of Outbound Flight, Mara senses life aboard: survivors, perhaps hundreds of them. The supposedly peaceful Geroons are then revealed to be the bloodthirsty Vagaari, bent on revenge against the Chiss and Outbound Flight for their defeat by Thrawn. Luke, Mara, the Chiss, the Imperials, and the embittered survivors must fight together to live. Though they defeat the boarding force, the Vagaari sabotage the *Chaf Envoy* and seize one of the derelict Dreadnaughts, striking back through the Redoubt toward the Chiss command center. Recovering a Delta-Twelve Skysprite, Luke and Mara pursue the rogue ship, board it, and use a false signal to trick the main Vagaari carrier before turning the captured Dreadnaught against the enemy fleet and helping the Chiss win the day. The survivors are entrusted to the Empire of the Hand, and Luke and Mara are left free to settle into their marriage, though Mara cannot shake the feeling that the whole affair was too neatly orchestrated, as if Thrawn, a master manipulator, might have prepared other clones of himself hidden across the Unknown Regions.
title: Survivor's Quest

displayTitle: Legends Epic Collection: The Original Marvel Years: Droids & Ewoks
synopsis: A complete collection of all-ages adventures starring the droids and Ewoks of *Star Wars*. Join Wicket and his Ewok friends Teebo and Princess Kneesaa in their forest home of Endor for colorful capers filled with rainbow bridges and rites of power. And in stories set years before they find a home with the Rebels, follow C-3PO and R2-D2 on their search for a master, a quest that stretches across space and beyond as a chronal crossover sees the droids meet the Ewoks for the very first time. The volume also offers a fresh perspective on the events of the original film in a tale called "Star Wars According to the Droids."
title: Legends Epic Collection: The Original Marvel Years: Droids & Ewoks

displayTitle: Tag & Bink: Revenge of the Clone Menace
synopsis: Behold, the long-awaited prequel to the Tag & Bink saga! Many a Star Wars fan has lain awake at night, plagued by suspense and wondering just how this infamous Rebel pair came to be. Well wonder no more, faithful readers! Your plague has ended with this tell-all issue that caps the space-opera legend, courtesy of fan-favorite Star Wars creators Kevin Rubio and Lucas Marangon. The origin story of our hapless heroes begins on their respective planets, Alderaan and Corellia. These future soldiers of the Rebel Alliance are sized up for Padawan potential, and we are treated to a rare inside look at
title: Tag & Bink: Revenge of the Clone Menace

displayTitle: Tales from a Galaxy Far, Far Away: Aliens
synopsis: In a bit of a return to form to the “Tales” books of Legends EU (Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina, Jabba’s Castle, etc), Landry Q. Walker wrote this anthology to flesh out the backstories of some of the ancillary creatures seen on screen in The Force Awakens. While not a particularly consequential book, it’s an absolute blast and will enhance your appreciation for the new characters established in Episode VII. He was able to switch between several different genres with the various short stories: a classic pirate adventure story about Sidon Ithano (the red alien Finn almost splits with), a horror story about what’s happening in the basement of Man Kanata’s castle, even a Clint Eastwood-esque Western starring infamous peg warmer Constable Zuvio in place of Clint Eastwood. While labeled as “Volume I,” a Volume II is yet to follow (though perhaps Canto Bight is a spiritual successor for The Last Jedi).
title: Tales from a Galaxy Far, Far Away: Aliens

synopsis: In the dusty heat of twin-sunned Tatooine lives the wealthiest gangster in a hundred worlds, master of a vast crime empire and keeper of a vicious, flesh-eating monster for entertainment (and disposal of his enemies). Bloated and sinister, Jabba the Hutt might have made a good joke—if he weren't so dangerous. A cast of soldiers, spies, assassins, scoundrels, bounty hunters, and pleasure seekers have come to his palace, and every visitor to Jabba's grand abode has a story. Some of them may even live to tell it....
title: Tales from Jabba's Palace

synopsis: "Mos Eisley Spaceport. You'll never find a more wretched hive of scum and villainy." With those words, Obi-Wan Kenobi introduced the most famous, not to mention dangerous, drinking establishment in the history of science fiction. Now, you can get a table of your own. Belly up to the bar and let the patrons regale you with a tale of Tatooine, a story of interstellar romance, and a saga of Rebel triumph. And please be sure to check your droids and your blaster at the door.
title: Tales from Mos Eisley

synopsis: Culled from the pages of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, one of the most popular Star Wars magazines in the field today, comes this exciting new short-story collection. Here are stories from such award-winning and New York Times bestselling authors as Timothy Zahn, Michael A. Stackpole, and Kathy Tyers as well as exciting newcomers, including Erin Endom, Laurie Burns, and Patricia A. Jackson. From the desperate flight of a civilian mail courier carrying vital Rebel intelligence through an Imperial blockade, to a suicidal commando raid on an impregnable Imperial prison, to a Corellian smuggler
title: Tales from the Empire

displayTitle: Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina
synopsis: In a far corner of the universe, on the small desert planet of Tatooine, there is a dark, nic-i-tain–filled cantina where you can down your favorite intoxicant while listening to the best jizz riffs in the universe. But beware your fellow denizens of this pangalactic watering hole, for they are cutthroats and cutpurses, assassins and troopers, humans and aliens, gangsters and thieves....
title: Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina

synopsis: Written by some of today's leading science fiction writers, these tales sweep us into a world where the only laws are cunning, force, and power—and only the bravest, craziest, and deadliest dare to tread. Here mercs and smugglers, gangsters and warriors fight toe to toe, side by side, and behind each other's backs in the backwaters of a universe ripped apart by war.... On Zelos II a man and a woman are held prisoner in a dark cell, each fighting in their own way for survival at the hands of Imperial captors—and a chance for escape that could cost one of them their life. On the tort
title: Tales from the New Republic

displayTitle: Insider: The High Republic: Survivors of the Stormwall
synopsis: A Phase III short-story collection on life inside the Occlusion Zone after Starlight Beacon's destruction and the rise of Marchion Ro's Stormwall; follows the crew of the Republic Longbeam Aurie, lost in the Zone, while a Jedi Padawan searches for a route through the barrier.
title: Star Wars Insider: The High Republic: Survivors of the Stormwall

displayTitle: Tales From The Occlusion Zone: Family Ties
synopsis: "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Pathogen" is a High Republic short story by Lydia Kang that was published as part of Tales from the Occlusion Zone in Star Wars Insider #226.

synopsis: "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: Survivors" is a High Republic short story by Lydia Kang that was published as part of Tales from the Occlusion Zone in Star Wars Insider #224.

displayTitle: Tales From The Occlusion Zone: The Fate Of Eriadu
synopsis: "Tales from the Occlusion Zone: The Fate of Eriadu" is a short story by Lydia Kang published as part of Tales from the Occlusion Zone in Star Wars Insider 229, on December 10, 2024.
title: Tales From The Occlusion Zone: The Fate Of Eriadu

synopsis:
A Canon graphic novel published by Dark Horse Comics. Set in Jabba's Palace the comic contains three stories and continues the tradition of Halloween comics begun by Scott's Tales from Vader's Castle written for IDW Publishing.
A galaxy of fear!
Its a dark and stormy night on Tatooine as Jabba the Hutts victim hangs perilously above the rancor pit with only his collection of scary stories to save him. New York Times bestseller Cavan Scott (Star Wars: The High Republic, Tales from Vaders Castle) teams up with with fan-favorites artists Nick Brokenshire, Juan Samu, Rafael Pérez, and Puste for a trio of terrifying tales just in time for Halloween. Experience clanking droid ghosts, explore the chilling wampa caves of Hoth and go monster hunting with Saber-For-Hire Ty Yorrick.
All-new all-ages adventures from throughout the galaxy!
title: Tales from the Rancor Pit

synopsis: Tales from Vader’s Castle puts a spooky spin on your favorite characters from a galaxy far, far away…
title: Tales from Vader's Castle

synopsis: You’ve seen Kanan and Hera take on some daring, action-packed adventures. Now they’re dealing with the paranormal. This first issue of Vader’s Castle is a classic ghost story featuring one of your favorite Star Wars duos. Who is the ghost? What does it want? How will these Rebels be free of the curse? Obviously you’ll have to read it for yourself to find out!
title: Star Wars Adventures: Tales From Vader's Castle #1
Tales from Vader's Castle #1

synopsis: Christopher Lee’s history in horror is showcased in this perfectly spooky tale. Cavan Scott wanted to do a classic vampire story mashup with a Star Wars comic, and he succeeds wonderfully with this Goosebumps-esque entry in Tales from Vader’s Castle. If you’re looking for a story to sink your teeth into in order to learn how Dooku ticks, you’ll need to look elsewhere. But if you want a unique and quick adventure of little consequence, this is a go-to. It’s nice to see Lee’s legacy honored in the right format within current Star Wars storytelling.
title: Star Wars Adventures: Tales From Vader's Castle #2

synopsis: Aboard a rebel crew's tense trek toward Darth Vader's castle on Mustafar, Lieutenant Thom Hudd spins a chilling tale set during the Clone Wars. On the planet Bray, Jedi Obi-Wan Kenobi and Adi Gallia arrive to stop a Separatist uprising, only to find that Count Dooku has already taken root there. Dooku has allied himself with Ravna, the Lord of Darkness, a vampiric creature whose bite twists its victims into obedient disciples in body and mind. The horror runs so deep that even the dark side of the Force seems to bend before it. Forced into an uneasy alliance with Dooku himself, Obi-Wan moves to bring the monster down, though whether the evil was truly ended remains uncertain.
title: Tales from Vader's Castle #2

synopsis:
Tales from Vader's Castle puts a spooky spin on your favorite characters from a galaxy far, far away...

This exciting weekly series explores the hidden corners of the Star Wars universe and features Rebels stars Hera, Kanan, and Chopper; Obi-Wan Kenobi and Count Dooku; Han Solo and Chewbacca; the Ewoks; and of course, Darth Vader, all under the shadow of Vader's Castle!

Fans will be excited to discover these creepy stories by fan-favorite writer Cavan Scott with art by the greatest talent from across the galaxy, including Derek Charm, Kelley Jones, Chris Fenoglio, Corin Howell, Robert Hack, and Charles Paul Wilson III!
title: Star Wars Adventures: Tales From Vader's Castle #3

synopsis: Hired to deliver a rare work of art to the planet Rendel for a million credits, Han Solo and Chewbacca arrive to find the wrecked ship of their old friend Karamu, whose failing droid warns them of an Emerald Witch before its power cells die. Venturing on alone, Han is snared by thorny vines and finds Karamu aged far beyond his years. A flower sprays gas that ages Han in an instant as the vines close around them both. The witch reveals she was cast out by her own sister, a queen who feared her, and planted the seed that grew into the deadly growth. As she promises the men will die feeding her plants, blaster fire rings out. Freed, and with Chewbacca now caught by the gas, Han hurls the *Tooka* statue at the witch. It bursts, releasing a gas that withers the vines and undoes her. The spell broken, Han, Chewie, and Karamu return to normal and escape, though a single surviving flower may still be tucked away in the *Millennium Falcon*'s cockpit.
title: Tales from Vader's Castle #3

synopsis:
Tales from Vader's Castle puts a spooky spin on your favorite characters from a galaxy far, far away...

This exciting weekly series explores the hidden corners of the Star Wars universe and features Rebels stars Hera, Kanan, and Chopper; Obi-Wan Kenobi and Count Dooku; Han Solo and Chewbacca; the Ewoks; and of course, Darth Vader, all under the shadow of Vader's Castle!

Fans will be excited to discover these creepy stories by fan-favorite writer Cavan Scott with art by the greatest talent from across the galaxy, including Derek Charm, Kelley Jones, Chris Fenoglio, Corin Howell, Robert Hack, and Charles Paul Wilson III!
title: Star Wars Adventures: Tales From Vader's Castle #4

synopsis: On the forest moon of Endor, Ewoks returning from a hunt discover that woklings have been vanishing from their village. The chief Buzza blames a Dulok raid, but young Chirpa senses something worse. Chirpa and Logray set out to learn the fate of the missing children and uncover a plot at a deserted Dulok camp: the traitor Makrit has been conspiring with the Duloks to sacrifice the woklings to the Great Devourer, a towering Gorax. In a clearing, the captured woklings are found trapped within a huge wooden effigy raised like a pyre. As the Ewoks work to free them, the Gorax attacks. Ra-Lee sounds a borra horn, and a stampede of the beasts sweeps in below, carrying the Ewoks and rescued woklings to safety. Only Makrit is left behind, standing alone beneath the monster's gaze as the intended sacrifice.
title: Tales from Vader's Castle #4

synopsis:
Tales from Vader's Castle puts a spooky spin on your favorite characters from a galaxy far, far away...

This exciting weekly series explores the hidden corners of the Star Wars universe and features Rebels stars Hera, Kanan, and Chopper; Obi-Wan Kenobi and Count Dooku; Han Solo and Chewbacca; the Ewoks; and of course, Darth Vader, all under the shadow of Vader's Castle!

Fans will be excited to discover these creepy stories by fan-favorite writer Cavan Scott with art by the greatest talent from across the galaxy, including Derek Charm, Kelley Jones, Chris Fenoglio, Corin Howell, Robert Hack, and Charles Paul Wilson III!
title: Star Wars Adventures: Tales From Vader's Castle #5

synopsis: The framing story reaches its climax as Lina Graf and Skritt flee through a hall of armor in Fortress Vader, only for the displays to lurch to life and surround them. The dark figure of Vaneé emerges, branding the thief Hudd an intruder, and recounts a raid on the castle soon after it was built, when Mustafarian warriors seized him and he begged for mercy from Darth Vader himself. As Vaneé's tale ends, Vader confronts the survivors directly, holding up what remains of the droid Crater and deflecting Lina's blaster fire with ease. Drawing on the lessons of each frightening story he has heard, Skritt commandeers an AT-ST and attacks the castle as a distraction. Vader hurls the walker into a lava lake, but Skritt escapes, and Lina pilots a TIE fighter that carries the survivors up and away from Mustafar. As they flee, Lina wonders whether their escape was Vader's design all along, meant to spread fear of the castle and its master across the galaxy.
title: Tales from Vader's Castle #5

synopsis: "Tales of Enlightenment: Last Orders" is a short story set during the High Republic Era, written by George Mann and published as part of The High Republic: Tales of Enlightenment series in Star Wars Insider 219
title: Tales of Enlightenment: Last Orders

synopsis: "Tales Of Enlightenment" is a short story published in 2 parts in Star Wars Insider 213 & 214, written by George Mann.

displayTitle: Tales Of Enlightenment: No Such Thing As A Bad Customer
synopsis: "Tales of Enlightenment: A Different Perspective" is a short story set during the High Republic Era, written by George Mann and published as part of The High Republic: Tales of Enlightenment series in Star Wars Insider 217 & 218.
title: Tales Of Enlightenment: No Such Thing As A Bad Customer

synopsis:
An anthology of stories set during the events of *The Empire Strikes Back* and *Return of the Jedi*, told from the perspective of the bounty hunters Darth Vader hires to hunt down Han Solo and the *Millennium Falcon*. Each tale follows one of the six hunters, uniting them in their lust for profit and their contempt for life. The collection includes:

- "Therefore I Am: The Tale of IG-88"
- "Payback: The Tale of Dengar"
- "The Prize Pelt: The Tale of Bossk"
- "Of Possible Futures: The Tale of Zuckuss and 4-LOM"
- "The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett"
title: Tales of the Bounty Hunters

synopsis: After losing everything, young Morgan Elsbeth navigates the expanding Imperial world toward a path of vengeance, while former Jedi Barriss Offee does what she must to survive a rapidly changing galaxy. The choices they make will define their destinies.
title: Tales of the Empire

synopsis: After losing everything, young Morgan Elsbeth navigates the expanding Imperial world toward a path of vengeance, while former Jedi Barriss Offee does what she must to survive a rapidly changing galaxy. The choices they make will define their destinies.
title: Tales of the Empire Season 1

synopsis: Young Morgan Elsbeth seeks revenge after losing everything.
title: The Path Of Fear

synopsis: Morgan is approached by a potentially powerful ally.
title: The Path Of Anger

synopsis: Offered the chance to stand trial for her crimes, Morgan instead seeks her destiny.

synopsis: Journey into the lives of two distinctly different Jedi from the prequel era, Ahsoka Tano and Count Dooku. Each will be put to the test as they make choices that will define their destinies.
title: Tales of the Jedi

The first 12 pages were previewed in black and white in Dark Horse Insider, Volume 2, issues 15 through 20, with 2-page installments in each issue from March to August, 1993.

title: Tales of the Jedi #3: The Saga of Nomi Sunrider, Part 1

title: Tales of the Jedi #5: The Saga of Nomi Sunrider, Part 3

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #1: Masters and Students of the Force
synopsis: In the remote reaches of the Star Wars universe, an ancient and deadly power has been reawakened by a foolish and arrogant young Jedi. He will begin a quest to become a Dark Lord of the Sith, and every galaxy will be threatened by his power.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #1: Masters and Students of the Force

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith
synopsis:
Darth Vader was a Dark Lord of the Sith. Discover the terror of the Dark Lords who preceded him in this stunning sequel to Tales of the Jedi.
Ulic Qel-Droma and Nomi Sunrider and young Jedi Knights drawn into a battle for the survival of the Old Republic. But as the power of the evil Sith spreads across the galaxy, it threatens the existence of the Jedi themselves, and draws one of their greatest allies over to the dark side!
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #2: The Quest for the Sith
synopsis: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith 2 is the second issue in the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith series of comics. It was published by Dark Horse Comics on November 1, 1994.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #2: The Quest for the Sith

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #3: Descent to the Dark Side
synopsis:
As the Krath declare war on the Jedi, thousands converge on the red planet of Deneba to debate the threat, presided over by the telepathic Master Odan-Urr. Ulic Qel-Droma proposes infiltrating the Krath to destroy them from within, drawing sharp warnings from Master Arca Jeth that the dark path leads only to ruin.

Meanwhile on Korriban, Exar Kun follows the spirit of Freedon Nadd through the Valley of the Dark Lords, hunted by risen Korriban zombies. Deep in a mausoleum, Nadd collapses the cave and crushes Kun, telling him that only the dark side can save him. Alone and dying, Kun opens himself fully to the dark side and is healed, his scream of rebirth echoing across the galaxy. At that moment Deneba is overrun by thousands of Krath war droids; in the ensuing battle Arca Jeth is killed. Passing a final test set by Nadd, Kun claims his power and sets out for Yavin 4, while a grieving Ulic vows to conquer the dark side and press on with his mission.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #3: Descent to the Dark Side

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #4: Death of a Dark Jedi
synopsis:
Following Naga Sadow's trail, Exar Kun arrives on the jungle moon of Yavin 4 and is taken by the Massassi, who hail him as the one they have long awaited and deliver him to the Temple of Fire. There he becomes the center of a blood sacrifice that awakens a monstrous beast forged from Sadow's dark side alchemy.

On Ossus, Ulic Qel-Droma studies the history of the Empress Teta system to prepare for his mission, ignoring the pleas of Nomi Sunrider and his brother Cay and the disapproval of Master Odan-Urr, who shows him a holocron warning of a Jedi seduced while trying to master the dark side from within. Disguised as a smuggler, Ulic travels to Cinnagar, where Satal and Aleema Keto already plot to manipulate him. By intervening in a rebel ambush and cutting down the attackers, he wins Aleema's trust while earning Satal's jealousy. On Yavin 4, Kun channels the dark side through an ancient Sith amulet to incinerate the beast, and the Massassi accept him as their leader. When Nadd schemes to resurrect himself, Kun uses the amulet to destroy his master's spirit forever; in his final moments Nadd warns the Ketos to beware the pretender Exar Kun.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #4: Death of a Dark Jedi

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #5: Sith Secrets
synopsis:
Imprisoned in Cinnagar's Iron Citadel, Ulic Qel-Droma is tortured as a suspected Jedi spy by the jealous Satal Keto. When Ulic insists he has come to learn the dark side, Satal injects him with a Sith poison, and the infatuated Aleema nurses him back to health, hoping to turn him into an ally. On Yavin 4, Exar Kun sets the Massassi to building Sith temples and discovers Naga Sadow's ancient battleship and alchemy laboratory hidden beneath the Temple of Fire.

Seeking proof that Ulic still lives, Nomi Sunrider lets herself be captured by the Krath while Cay Qel-Droma and Tott Doneeta wait aboard the *Nebulon Ranger*. To prove his loyalty, Ulic must execute her, but he secretly plans her rescue and sends word to Ossus revealing the Ketos as the ones behind Arca Jeth's death. Satal intercepts the message and orders both Nomi's execution and Ulic's assassination. Nomi's battle meditation turns the guards against one another, and in a duel Ulic strikes down Satal to avenge his master, giving in to the anger that ignites the Sith poison in his veins. He drives Cay and Tott away with the Force, and the heartbroken Jedi flee into hyperspace as Nomi grieves his apparent fall.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #5: Sith Secrets

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #6: Jedi Assault
synopsis: In the final, climactic issue of this landmark series, two renegade Jedi, Ulic Qel-Droma and Exar Kun, clash in mortal combat. One will be awarded the title of Dark Lord of the Sith, the most feared and powerful force in the galaxy; the other may be awarded a shallow grave. The fate of the entire universe rests on the outcome!
title: Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith #6: Jedi Assault

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Knights of the Old Republic
synopsis: Four thousand years before Luke Skywalker stood as the final Jedi, his predecessors wandered the universe, each playing his own role in keeping the peace and holding back the forces of the dark side. This 136-page collection focuses on Ulic Qel-Droma and Nomi Sunrider, two young novices training to become Jedi warriors. Their training, however, turns into a trial by fire when they prematurely have to face off against reigning crimelord Great Bogga, a Hutt who has killed one of the universe's most powerful Jedi Knights.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Knights of the Old Republic

synopsis: Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Redemption, originally titled Tales of the Jedi: The Redemption of Ulic Qel-Droma, is a Star Wars Legends comic book miniseries written by Kevin J. Anderson. It is the final miniseries in the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi comic series, and it ran from July 22, 1998 to November 25, 1998, following the release of Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Fall of the Sith Empire in 1997.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #1: A Gathering of Jedi
synopsis:
Ten years after the Great Sith War, the broken exile Ulic Qel-Droma travels aboard Hoggon's ship in search of a place to spend his last days. On Yavin 4 he sees only the ghosts of the war, including his dead brother Cay, and rejects it before finally settling on the icy world of Rhen Var. On Ryloth, the Jedi Tott Doneeta is badly burned trying to hold back a heat storm but insists on leaving for the Great Jedi Convocation.

At Exis Station, Master Thon, the Cathar Jedi Sylvar, and Nomi Sunrider gather for the convocation. Nomi's teenage daughter Vima, restless and undertrained, slips away to fly a mining ship, is caught in a magnetic loop, and must eject, only to be rescued by the late-arriving Tott Doneeta. When Nomi scolds her recklessness, Vima retorts that if her mother spent more time training her, she could have handled it herself.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #1: A Gathering of Jedi

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #2: The Search for Peace
synopsis:
At Exis Station, Sylvar addresses the convocation, denouncing Ulic Qel-Droma as the greatest shame of the Jedi for his alliance with Exar Kun and the deaths it caused, including that of her mate Crado. On Rhen Var, Ulic finds his lightsaber nothing but a lifeless tool and falls into a hole in the ground, where a vision of his late master Arca Jeth urges him to live, and he climbs back through the snow.

Hungry for a teacher, Vima Sunrider stows away on Hoggon's ship to find Ulic. Meanwhile Tott Doneeta brings Sylvar to Ryloth to confront the anger inside her; when she tries to rally his clan to revenge, Tott instead brokers peace between the feuding tribes, and Sylvar, unsettled, asks to be taken home to Cathar. Hoggon discovers Vima aboard his ship and drops her on Rhen Var with a transmitter, and she vows not to leave until she has become a Jedi.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #2: The Search for Peace

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #3: Homecoming
synopsis:
Searching an abandoned city on Rhen Var, Vima Sunrider finds Ulic Qel-Droma, who at first mistakes her for her mother Nomi. She begs him to train her, but Ulic refuses, insisting he is no longer a Jedi and can no longer feel the Force, and sends her away. When she is caught in a blizzard, he takes pity and shelters her.

On Cathar, Sylvar is welcomed home as a hero but finds no peace, and the holy man Kharr tells her that her true enemy is the anger within. She sets out on a blood hunt to purge her rage, leading Tott Doneeta into a cave to slaughter a Kiltik queen and her brood; horrified, Tott warns that her fury leads to the dark side. On Rhen Var, Ulic confesses to Vima the full cost of the Jedi path, the losses and betrayals he has suffered, and asks why she still wants him. She answers simply, because he is Ulic. Elsewhere, Hoggon realizes he has been ferrying the wanted criminal Ulic and resolves to warn Nomi at Exis Station, where she learns her daughter intends to be trained by him.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #3: Homecoming

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #4: The Trials of a Jedi
synopsis:
On Cathar's Vast Veldt, Sylvar hunts to purge her hatred of Ulic but finds no relief; when a scout brings word that Vima Sunrider has disappeared, she spares his life and leaves to aid Nomi. On Rhen Var, the powerless Ulic Qel-Droma teaches Vima to build her first lightsaber from her late father Andur's Adegan crystals and trains her in its use, marveling at her progress even as he doubts he has any right to teach her.

Reflecting on her own path, Nomi admits she was forced into the Jedi life by Andur's murder and regrets neglecting Vima, believing it fitting that Ulic is teaching her daughter the true cost of being a Jedi. Sylvar arrives and is appalled that Nomi would let a war criminal near her child, and storms off, only to be intercepted by Hoggon, who offers to lead her to Ulic. On Rhen Var, Ulic and Vima carve great ice sculptures with their lightsabers to honor Arca Jeth and Andur, and Vima tells Ulic that this proves he still has the heart of a Jedi. With the conclave over, Nomi departs to reclaim and train her daughter.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #4: The Trials of a Jedi

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #5: Master
synopsis:
On the snowy wastes of Rhen Var, Ulic Qel-Droma teaches Vima Sunrider that being a Jedi means facing terrible ordeals and life-and-death choices, warning her against overconfidence even as she playfully bests him. Nomi Sunrider arrives to reclaim her daughter, still angry at Ulic, but Vima insists she is staying because Ulic has already made her a Jedi. Vima vouches for him, showing her mother the ice sculptures of Arca Jeth and Andur Sunrider, and Nomi relents; mother and daughter embrace.

As Ulic finally makes peace with his past, Sylvar arrives seeking to kill him for his crimes and the death of her mate Crado. He refuses to fight, urging her to bury her past as a Jedi should, and deactivates his lightsaber. Realizing he is right, Sylvar frees herself of her hatred and thanks him. But Hoggon, craving glory for slaying a notorious Dark Jedi, shoots Ulic through the chest. As Nomi and Vima tend him, Ulic gives Vima his medallion and tells her to remember the possibilities, then becomes one with the Force. Though he had lost his connection to the Force, Vima says, he understood it and had the heart of a Jedi to the end.
title: Tales of the Jedi: Redemption #5: Master

displayTitle: Tales Of The Jedi S01E03: Choices
synopsis: Jedi Knights investigate the mysterious death of one of their own.
title: Tales Of The Jedi S01E03: Choices

synopsis: Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi is a series of animated anthology shorts, released as six episodes on October 26, 2022. Each episode featured Jedi from the era of the prequel trilogy; half of the season focused on Count Dooku, while the other half focused on Ahsoka Tano, with other Jedi featuring as supporting characters in the episodes. Created and written by Dave Filoni, the series is animated in the style of Star Wars: The Clone Wars and Star Wars: The Bad Batch. A part of the Star Wars Tales television series, a sequel season, Star Wars: Tales of the Empire, was released on May 4, 2024.
title: Tales of the Jedi Season 1

synopsis: A child goes through a rite of passage with unexpected results.
title: Life And Death

synopsis: Two Jedi are dispatched to resolve a hostage situation on a distant planet.
title: Justice

synopsis: A Jedi master makes a troubling discovery.
title: The SIth Lord

synopsis: A Jedi Padawan is given a seemingly impossible exercise.
title: Practice Makes Perfect

synopsis: A former Jedi is faced with the truth that they cannot be something they are not.
title: Resolve

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire
synopsis: Jori Daragon is on trial for offenses against Empress Teta's government. But far from being a lawbreaker, Jori is trying to reach the Empress, to warn her of the dark plans of the Sith Empire. If she is found guilty, she will be confined to a colony planet. And then nothing will stand between the Empress and the Sith.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #1: Desperate Measures
synopsis: The Sith Empire has rallied its forces for an all-out battle for control of the galaxy. Their goal: Crush the Republic. And with so many Sith masters amongst them, the goal is not unachievable. As Naga Sadow readies his fleet to attack, one lone person threatens to undermine the entire campaign -- Jori Daragon. She isn't a Jedi. She doesn't come from a military background or have a history of fighting. She's an explorer, a mapper of hyperspace who stumbled on the plans of the Sith. And she just may be the savior of the Republic.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #1: Desperate Measures

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #2: Forces in Collision
synopsis: Jori Daragon is on trial for offenses against Empress Teta's government. But far from being a lawbreaker, Jori is trying to reach the Empress, to warn her of the dark plans of the Sith Empire. If she is found guilty, she will be confined to a colony planet. And then nothing will stand between the Empress and the Sith.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #2: Forces in Collision

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #3: First Encounter
synopsis: Before the Empire held sway over the galaxy… before the Rebel forces rose up to create the New Republic… there was the time of the Old Republic. The story of how that grand oligarchy fell from power begins here, in an episode titled "First Encounter".
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #3: First Encounter

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #4: The Dogs of War
synopsis: The Sith invasion rages on, as the dark side of the Force takes the upper hand over the Old Republic. But Dark Lord Naga Sadow is about to learn that winning a battle is not the same as winning a war!
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #4: The Dogs of War

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #5: End of an Empire
synopsis: Featuring the historic confrontation between the Old Republic and the Empire, The Fall of the Sith Empire cannot end but with the victory of the Old Republic. But even winners have losses, and the Sith Empire walks away from this battle with a victory of its own… one which will later spell doom for the Old Republic.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire #5: End of an Empire

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising
synopsis: Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Freedon Nadd Uprising is a story arc of the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi comic book series. It ran from August 1, 1994 to September 1, 1994. HighBridge Audio released a Tales of the Jedi audio drama of the comic.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising #1
synopsis:
After the Beast Wars, the new Onderonian monarchy and their Jedi allies under Master Arca Jeth prepare to transport the sarcophagi of the dark siders Freedon Nadd and Amanoa to a sealed tomb on the moon of Dxun. Before the remains can be loaded, a groundborer disgorges hundreds of Naddists led by Warb Null, who seize the sarcophagi and vanish into the depths of Iziz. Tracing the dark side to King Ommin's life-support facility, Arca is ambushed; the spirit of Freedon Nadd empowers Ommin, who kidnaps the Jedi Master while Ulic Qel-Droma slays Warb Null. With the palace fallen, Ulic and his companions flee to Modon Kira's fortress and call for Republic and Jedi reinforcements.

On Ossus, Master Thon presents his new apprentice Nomi Sunrider and her daughter Vima to Master Vodo-Siosk Baas. After building her first lightsaber and training for weeks, Nomi joins four fellow students on a mission to rescue Arca. Meanwhile the Krath heirs Satal and Aleema Keto steal a Sith book from the Galactic Museum on Coruscant and, unable to read it, run the Republic blockade of Onderon to seek out the Sith magicians of Iziz.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising #1

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising #2: Initiates of the Sith
synopsis:
Answering Ulic Qel-Droma's call, Republic rocket jumpers descend on Onderon to break the Freedon Nadd uprising, and Satal and Aleema Keto crash their yacht in the streets of Iziz. The Jedi task force led by Nomi Sunrider breaks the Naddist siege of Modon Kira's fortress, though Kith Kark is killed. When Nomi's battle meditation is struck down by an unseen adversary, Ulic reveals it to be King Ommin. Seeking help translating their Sith book, the Ketos win an audience with Ommin, who gives Satal a Sith amulet and shows them his prized captive, the tormented Master Arca Jeth.

Seven Jedi fight into Ommin's subterranean stronghold; Cay Qel-Droma loses an arm to a Sith war droid but cuts it down. Ulic severs the king's mechanical armature, leaving Ommin to collapse without its support, while the Ketos escape with the Sith tome, given to them by Freedon Nadd's Force ghost along with a talisman and Sith swords. After Ommin dies and Arca is freed, Nadd taunts the Jedi that they have already lost before vanishing. With the uprising crushed, the remains of Nadd, Amanoa, and Ommin are entombed on Dxun, and Arca explains that Nadd spoke of an ancient prophecy that the Dark Lords of the Sith would return, urging the Jedi to hold to the light.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising #2: Initiates of the Sith

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith
synopsis: The history of the Old Republic has become legend: how the bold explorations of young Gav and Jori Daragon brought two galactic empires crashing together, how Odan-Urr became a powerful Jedi, and how the seven worlds of the Koros system were finally united by the wise and skilled Empress Teta. Five millennia before the birth of Luke Skywalker and the fall of Palpatine's Empire, our story unfolds…
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #0: Conquest and Unification
synopsis: Five millennia before Luke Skywalker was born — and 1,000 years before two young Jedi reawakened a long-dormant evil — the dark power of The Sith held unquestioning rule over the galaxy. For those black-souled, corrupt Jedi, it was a golden age. For the rest of the galaxy, it was a nightmare.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #0: Conquest and Unification

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #1: Into the Unknown
synopsis: Navigators and explorers are a special breed — they blaze the paths others will follow. They must have strength, drive, and hope, and be more than a little fearless. But nowhere is it written that they must have luck. Gav and Jori Daragon make their living charting the routes of hyperspace and selling those routes to traders and haulers. When a starswarm cluster batters their ship, they land on the planet Cinnagar, destitute and forlorn, and convince the relatively amiable Aarrba the Hutt to make the necessary repairs.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #1: Into the Unknown

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #2: Funeral for a Dark Lord
synopsis: Marka Ragnos ruled the galaxy with an iron fist. He was the Dark Lord of the Sith -- the most powerful of the most powerful. But now he is dead. And two innocent hyperspace explorers, Gav and Jori Daragon, may follow suit if they get caught between the warring factions hoping to fill the Dark Lord void.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #2: Funeral for a Dark Lord

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #3: The Fabric of an Empire
synopsis: When Gav and Jori Daragon set off to map the uncharted regions of hyperspace, they knew they were risking their lives. But they thought it would be an unsuspected cluster of debris that would take them out of the game, not their successful discovery of a series of hyperspace "short cuts." The Sith Empire wants to know where these routes are -- wants to hold the only map to these routes -- so that it can maintain an iron grip on the galaxy. And the lives of a couple of explorers mean very little to these masters of the dark side of the Force.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #3: The Fabric of an Empire

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #4: Pawns of a Sith Lord
synopsis: When Gav and Jori Daragon were first captured by the evil Sith, they feared for their lives. Now, Jori fears for her brother's spirit, as would-be Dark Lord Naga Sadow begins to initiate him into the ways of the dark side of the Force. Naga Sadow is playing a deadly game of chess, and the Daragon siblings are his pawns.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #4: Pawns of a Sith Lord

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #5: The Flight of Starbreaker 12
synopsis: Gav and Jori Daragon knew they could be killed in their chosen line of work -- mapping out uncharted hyperspace flight paths could easily result in a deadly meeting with an unexpected meteorite shower or an unknown world. But it's neither comets nor planets that threaten them now. Rather, it's their success that may cost them their lives. They've successfully mapped a hyperspace shortcut -- a shortcut which could prove strategically imperative in the event of war. And war is just what Gav and Jori have found themselves in the middle of. Trapped between two factions of the Sith, each vying for control of the galaxy, this sibling duo of hyperspace pioneers has become a pawn to the dark side of Sith magic.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Golden Age of the Sith #5: The Flight of Starbreaker 12

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War
synopsis: Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - The Sith War is a story arc of the Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi comic book series. It ran from August 1, 1995 to January 1, 1996 and was published by Dark Horse Comics.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #1: Edge of the Whirlwind
synopsis: This issue kicks off the thrill-a-minute follow-up to the best-selling Dark Lords of the Sith saga. Picking up six months after our story left off, Exar Kun and Ulic Qel-Droma are implementing their insidious plan for revitalizing the Golden Age of the Sith. While insinuating his darkside teachings to Jedi students on the planet Ossus, it becomes clear that Kun is only concerned with securing a powerful Sith Holocron from its sealed repository in the great library of Ossus. Ulic has devoted himself to developing the darkside military strategy -- the key to which may just be winning the loyalty of the ferocious warrior race led by a masked mysteryman named Mandalore.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #1: Edge of the Whirlwind

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #2: The Battle of Coruscant
synopsis: Ulic Qel-Droma spent years learning to control the Force. He wanted to be on the side of the good, a member of the Jedi elite. But it is not the Force one must control to become a Jedi; rather, one must learn to control oneself. Ulic Qel-Droma has tasted power, and now he cannot fight the dark hunger inside him…
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #2: The Battle of Coruscant

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #3: The Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma
synopsis: Ulic Qel-Droma has fallen victim to the spell of the dark side, and now he must stand before the Jedi masters to answer for his crimes. But another from within the Jedi ranks has taken up his mantle of evil. Exar Kun is turning his doting apprentices into Sith-possessed assassins and sending them out to kill the Jedi masters, and the fate of the entire Jedi force hangs in the balance!
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #3: The Trial of Ulic Qel-Droma

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #4: Jedi Holocaust
synopsis:
Following the disastrous raid on Coruscant, Ulic Qel-Droma, Exar Kun, and Mandalore return to Yavin 4, where Ulic learns from Mandalore that Aleema Keto betrayed him. Concealing his anger, Ulic grants Aleema the "honor" of leading the next attack, secretly intending to let her manipulations destroy her.

Meanwhile, Kun's corrupted Jedi acolytes move to murder their own masters across the galaxy. On Ambria, Nomi Sunrider, her daughter Vima, and Sylvar visit Master Thon, only to be ambushed by Crado and the Sith-possessed Oss Wilum, who summons dark side Hssiss. The Jedi fight them off, Nomi using battle meditation to defeat the beasts, and Sylvar scars the fleeing Crado. Elsewhere the acolytes succeed in slaying eight Jedi Masters, alerting the surviving Jedi at the Great Library of Ossus to the scale of the threat.

To draw the Republic fleet away from Ossus, Kun sends Aleema and Crado to attack Kemplex Nine aboard Naga Sadow's warship *Corsair*. Using its crystals, Aleema tears the core from a star in the Cron Cluster and hurls it at the pursuing Jedi, incinerating them, but the blast triggers a chain reaction that detonates all ten stars. Aleema and Crado are killed in the Cron Supernova, and with Ossus left defenseless, Ulic and Exar Kun set course to plunder its Great Library.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #4: Jedi Holocaust

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #5: Brother Against Brother
synopsis: It's Jedi against Jedi, brother against brother, and blood will spill. Brothers Cay and Ulic Qel-Droma lead their respective Jedi forces against one another -- Cay with his honorable but unproven students, Ulic with the ascendant Dark Jedi horde. As the forces clash on the planet Ossus, the right to the powerful historical artifacts of the ancient Jedi await the victor.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #5: Brother Against Brother

displayTitle: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #6: Dark Lord
synopsis: The storm clouds of battle gather over Yavin Four, and that entire world could be a casualty! To make his final stand against Jedi Nomi Sunrider, the betrayer Ulic Qel-Droma has let slip the dogs of war in a final, climactic confrontation.
title: Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War #6: Dark Lord

Former assassin and bounty hunter Asajj Ventress is given a new chance at life and must go on the run with an unexpected new ally, while outlaw Cad Bane faces his past when he confronts an old friend, now a Marshal on the opposite side of the law.
title: Tales of the Underworld - Season 1

synopsis: Star Wars: Target Vader is a Marvel Comics miniseries focusing on the character Beilert Valance during his time as a bounty hunter. The series is written by Robbie Thompson with art by Marc Laming and was published starting July 2019.
title: Target Vader

synopsis:
**The Hunter Becomes the Hunted!**

Valance and Dengar track Darth Vader while also seeking out the perfect weapon to kill the Sith Lord... which they'll need as Vader turns the tables on the hunters and corners them! And both hunter and hunted uncover dark secrets behind the criminal syndicate known only as the Hidden Hand.
title: Target Vader #2

synopsis:
Above Heva, an *Imperial II*-class Star Destroyer under General Gollin reports that the *Formidable* and its TIE fighters have been destroyed by bot-drones, leaving Darth Vader alone on the surface and surrounded by the bounty hunters hunting him. Gollin declines to scramble a rescue, confident Vader will kill his pursuers first.

On the ground, Dengar urges Beilert Valance's team to strike, but Valance and Chio Fain hold back to avoid revealing their positions. Fain attacks first; Vader deflects the blaster bolts and a ricochet wounds Valance. Gita, disguised as a Tusken Raider, reveals herself as she rushes to help him. Vader cuts down Fain, hurling him into Honnah's battle-ax, and kills Honnah as well. As Valance charges Vader with an electro-pulse but fails to activate it in time, Dengar reveals himself as Vader's informant and electrocutes Valance.
title: Target Vader #4

synopsis:
**The Great Escape!**

Valance has been betrayed, captured, and now stands alone. But nothing will stop the Hunter from finishing his prey... Darth Vader, who is closing in on his own target, the Hidden Hand, a mysterious syndicate working with scum, villainy... and the Rebellion? Can Valance use his training as a former Imperial to escape... or will he find out he has more in common with the Dark Lord than he could ever have imagined?!
title: Target Vader #5

synopsis:
**Showdown!**

Valance has escaped the clutches of the Empire, and finally has a lead on who's really behind the mysterious crime syndicate, the Hidden Hand. In the process, he's uncovered a mystery about his own past. Darth Vader... and members of the Rebellion, desperate for one last arms deal with The Hidden Hand. Will Valance be able to finish the job and kill Vader? Will Vader crush the Rebellion once and for all? Or will The Hidden Hand continue to walk between the raindrops and escape them all? Check out the thrilling finale to find out!
title: Target Vader #6

synopsis: Before Grand Moff Tarkin ordered the first galaxy-altering demonstration of the Death Star, he was forced to prove to the Emperor, and himself, that he had what it took to govern with merciless force.
title: Tarkin

synopsis: Han Solo and Leia Organa take center stage, and stunning revelations from the past play a critical role in shaping the future, as the New York Times bestselling author of Star Wars: The New Jedi Order: Star by Star now turns to a crucial chapter in the classic Star Wars saga. The deaths of Darth Vader and Emperor Palpatine and victory at the Battle of Endor by no means spelled the end of the Empire. In the aftermath, the New Republic has faced a constant struggle to survive and grow. And now a new threat looms: a masterpiece of Alderaanian art, lost in transit after the planet's destruction, h
title: Tatooine Ghost

synopsis:
The story opens in the ravaged underworld of Coruscant, where the deformed Twi'lek Alema Rar hunts Jacen Solo in revenge for their past duels during the Dark Nest Crisis. Alema realizes she is herself being stalked by the Sith Lumiya and follows her back to Jacen, who is conferring with the World Brain about the Galactic Alliance Guard's raids on Corellian terrorists. Alema's attempt to kill Jacen with a blowgun misses and strikes the World Brain instead; Lumiya, rather than let Jacen give in to rage, urges him to master his emotions and spare his attacker.

Meanwhile, Han and Leia Solo, having sided with the Corellian insurgents against the Galactic Alliance, are brought aboard a Corellian flagship to meet Dur Gejjen and his allies. The Corellians want the Solos to persuade Hapan Queen Mother Tenel Ka Djo to bring the Hapes Consortium over to Corellia's side, a difficult task given Hapes's loyalty to the Alliance. But when the Solos discover that the plan actually depends on Hapan nobles murdering their pro-Alliance queen and her daughter, they resolve to protect Tenel Ka instead.

As the conflict widens, Luke Skywalker grows fearful that his son Ben and his nephew Jacen are being corrupted by Lumiya, and sends the Jedi Master Tresina Lobi to spy on them. Alema and Lumiya intercept and kill Lobi to push Jacen further toward the dark side. The Solos' effort to save the queen cannot undo the consequences of their choices, which turn family against family and set Luke on a collision course with his fiercest enemy, even as Jacen's growing power draws Han and Leia into peril at the hands of their own son.
title: Tempest

synopsis: "Temple of Phantoms" is a comic story by Christopher Cooper featured in the ninth issue of the Star Wars Comic magazine, released in August of 2014. The story centers on a young Anakin Skywalker fighting a dark-side entity inside the Jedi Temple.
title: Temple of Phantoms

synopsis: The Acolyte is a mystery-thriller that will take viewers into a galaxy of shadowy secrets and emerging dark-side powers in the final days of the High Republic era. A former Padawan reunites with her Jedi Master to investigate a series of crimes, but the forces they confront are more sinister than they ever anticipated
title: The Acolyte

synopsis: Jedi Padawans Jedi and Yord do not like each other very much. But accompanying their respective masters on an important mission means they’re going to have to tolerate each other. When they become involved in a dangerous competition on a distant world, however, tolerating each other won’t be enough; they’ll have to depend on each other to make it out alive.
title: The Acolyte: The Crystal Crown

synopsis:
**Kelnacca From the Hit Disney+ Series Debuts!**

Cavan Scott and Marika Cresta bring the action as Wookiee Jedi Kelnacca steps into the pages of his own issue! Delve deep into the history of Kelnacca and the world of the High Republic in this bombastic one-shot! Includes an exclusive interview with Kelnacca actor Joonas Suotamo.
title: The Acolyte: Kelnacca

synopsis: A one-shot comic featuring Jedi Master Kelnacca from the Star Wars Acolyte TV Series

synopsis: Star Wars: The Acolyte, titled on-screen as The Acolyte, is a Star Wars television series developed by Leslye Headland for Disney+. Headland wrote, executive produced, and served as the series' showrunner. It takes place a century before the events of Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace, and focuses on the emergence of dark side powers in the final days of the High Republic Era. The Acolyte premiered on June 4, 2024, concluded its first season on July 16, 2024, and was reported as canceled on August 19, 2024, which was later confirmed by Disney executives. In spite of that ending, details have emerged that the series was intended to run for several seasons.
title: The Acolyte - Season 1

synopsis: "The Apprentice" is a short story written by Elaine Cunningham and is the thirteenth installment of the New Jedi Order series set in the Star Wars universe. The story by Elaine Cunningham was first published in Star Wars Gamer 8 on the 1st of January 2002 by Wizards of the Coast and was shortlisted for the Prix de la Plume Noire by the French Committee for Experimental Literature.

synopsis: The series follows the elite and experimental clones of the Bad Batch (first introduced in The Clone Wars) as they find their way in a rapidly changing galaxy in the immediate aftermath of the Clone War. Members of Bad Batch, a unique squad of clones who vary genetically from their brothers in the Clone Army, each possess a singular exceptional skill, which makes them extraordinarily effective soldiers and a formidable crew. In the post-Clone War era, they will take on daring mercenary missions as they struggle to stay afloat and find new purpose.
title: The Bad Batch

synopsis:
The Bad Batch: Hunted! is a young readers graphic novel in the Screen Comix series. The book adapts the episode "Bounty Lost" from the first season of the Disney+ series Star Wars: The Bad Batch.

A graphic novel-style retelling with full-color images and dialogue from Star Wars: The Bad Batch on Disney+!

After refusing to follow Order 66 and destroy the Jedi, Clone Force 99 and their new friend Omega are on the run from bounty hunters Fennec Shand and Cad Bane, the Empire, and even their old teammate Crosshair! Will the Bad Batch be able to escape? This 80-page graphic novel-style retelling of Episode 9: Bounty Lost from Star Wars: The Bad Batch features final frames and dialogue from the animated series on Disney+ in vibrant full color. It's perfect for boys and girls ages 8 to 11 and fans of Star Wars: The Bad Batch and Star Wars: The Clone Wars.

Star Wars: The Bad Batch follows the elite and experimental troopers of Clone Force 99 (first introduced in Star Wars: The Clone Wars) as they find their way in a rapidly changing galaxy in the immediate aftermath of the Clone Wars. Members of the Bad Batch, as they prefer to be called, a unique squad of clones who vary genetically from their brothers in the Clone Army, each possess a singular exceptional skill, which makes them extraordinarily effective soldiers and a formidable crew.
title: The Bad Batch: Hunted!

synopsis:
"The Ballad of 264" (German title "Die Ballade von 264") is a comic strip written by Jeremy Barlow, drawn by Bob Molesworth and edited by Gunther Nickel. The story was published in the 25th issue of the German Star Wars Rebels Magazine, which was released on November 23, 2016.
In a Twitter message, Barlow placed this comic between "The Call" and "Homecoming."
It has never received an official English translation.
title: The Ballad of 264

synopsis: Ewoks: The Battle for Endor is a 1985 television film written and directed by brothers Jim and Ken Wheat from a story by George Lucas. It focuses on Cindel, a little girl who joins her friends Noa, Wicket and the other Ewoks in facing off against a band of Marauders. The film is a follow-up to 1984's Caravan of Courage: An Ewok Adventure.
title: The Battle For Endor

synopsis: "The Battle of Cadinth" is a short story written by Bill Smith. It was first published in Star Wars Galaxy #2 by Topps in Winter 1995. It takes place 6 ABY, and also features roleplaying game stats for various droids and vehicles to use the story in a role-playing game. On February 23, 2007, the story was published on Hyperspace. The story features droids and vehicles based on concepts created by Kenner for a proposed toy line set after Return of the Jedi called The Epic Continues, which was ultimately rejected by Lucasfilm. Many of these toy concepts were featured in an article published in t
title: The Battle of Cadinth

synopsis: "The Beast Within" (German title "Das versteckte Monster") is a comic strip written by Alec Worley, drawn by Bob Molesworth and edited by Gunther Nickel. The story was published in the 3rd issue of the German Star Wars Rebels Animation-Magazine, which was released on July 11, 2018. It was first published in English in the November 2019 issue of the Australian magazine K-Zone.
title: The Beast Within

title: The Book Of Boba Fett Season 1

displayTitle: The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett
synopsis: When legendary bounty hunter Boba Fett met his untimely end at the Pit of Carkoon, the Rebel Alliance discovered a secure, blast-proof bounty hunter's case holding Boba Fett's personal items. For years it remained hidden away. But now all who wish may unlock the secrets held within… Press the buttons, insert the data card, and the case opens with sounds and lights. Housed within is a text of great import. At some point in his career Boba Fett bound together the latest edition of the Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook and a manifesto from Death Watch, the secretive splinter group of Mandalori
title: The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett

displayTitle: The Bounty Hunter (Lost Jedi Adventure Game Book)
synopsis: The Bounty Hunter is a "choose your own adventure"-style roleplaying book, written by Paul Cockburn and published, in the United Kingdom only, by Boxtree in 1994. It was the second (and last) part of The Lost Jedi adventure game series. The story was later collected along with Jedi Dawn in The Lost Jedi Adventure Game Book.
title: The Bounty Hunter (Lost Jedi Adventure Game Book)

synopsis: Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi heads to the planet Cestus to try and stop the Separatists from overtaking the struggling world.
title: The Cestus Deception

synopsis: After the Republic’s victory on Christophsis, Anakin and his new apprentice Ahsoka Tano must rescue the kidnapped son of Jabba the Hutt. Political intrigue complicates their mission.
title: The Clone Wars

synopsis: Set in the time between Attack of the Clones and Revenge of the Sith, this new series will focus on the legendary Clone Wars! Some of your favorite Jedi: Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, Master Yoda, Master Mace Windu, and others, will be joined by a few new faces as they fight to maintain the true spirit of the Republic. In addition to the lead story, each issue will feature a backup story by one of the artists from the TV series! This new series will be a compliment to Lucasfilm's highly anticipated CGI-animated The Clone Wars movie and television programs!
title: The Clone Wars #01

synopsis: Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi have discovered the dastardly plot of Count Dooku—the entire population of the planet Kiros are in danger! Along with fresh new Padawan Ahsoka Tano—Anakin and Obi-Wan have liberated the droid-occupied planet and halted its destruction, but the native Togruta people have disappeared… Now, our Jedi must find the missing citizens, before Count Dooku can carry out his master plan! Featuring cover art by Kilian Plunkett, one of the artists working on The Clone Wars TV show!
title: The Clone Wars #02

synopsis: Eighty million Togruta from the planet of Kiros have suddenly disappeared! It's up to Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Togruta Padawan Ahsoka Tano to find the lost population. A clue leads the Jedi to the Zygerria system: massive slave auctions have been held there in the past, and will be taking place again soon -- with the unfortunate Togruta on the auction block! This new series is a complement to Lucasfilm's highly anticipated CGI-animated Star Wars: The Clone Wars television series!
title: The Clone Wars #03

synopsis: Anakin Skywalker goes undercover as a slave trader - and Ahsoka is his slave! Fearing that the entire population of the planet Kiros may have been forced into slavery, the team has traveled to the Zygerria system to attend the first slave auction in years. Anakin must work his charm on the Queen of Zygerria, while "slave" Ahsoka and "slave traders" Rex and Obi-Wan search for clues. Can our heroes pull off this incredible deception? This issue also features a backup story by one of the artists from the TV series!
title: The Clone Wars #04

synopsis: Anakin Skywalker has become a slave once again! But he's not just any slave - he's the personal pet of the beautiful Queen Miraj of Zygerria. While attempting to prevent the Zygerrian auction of millions of Togruta people into slavery, Jedi Knights Anakin, Obi-Wan, and Padawan Ahsoka Tano have been thrown into slavery themselves! In captivity, the Jedi have unearthed the supplier behind this newly revitalized slave trade - Count Dooku and the Separatists!
title: The Clone Wars #05

synopsis: In this thrilling conclusion to "Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Slaves of the Republic," Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano fight to free themselves and the kidnapped population of Kiros from a life of slavery - and it's not only the Zygerrian slavers they must battle... Count Dooku has arrived with Asajj Ventress with hopes of salvaging the new slave trade that has been fueled by Separatist efforts.
title: The Clone Wars #06

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #07: In Service of the Republic, Part 1
synopsis: In the first part of a three-issue story, the Republic's air superiority on the world of Khorm is rendered moot by storms of unnatural origin. Grounded, Jedi Kit Fisto and Plo Koon plan a daring assault mission with a squad of tougher-than-nails Republic Commandos. But if going up against determined and dug-in Separatist forces wasn't enough, the Jedi must also contend with a glory-seeking young Republic officer in their midst, Captain Kendal Ozzel.
title: The Clone Wars #07: In Service of the Republic, Part 1

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #08: In Service of the Republic, Part 2
synopsis: Faced with the worst storm in the ice-planet Khorm's history, Jedi Masters Plo Koon and Kit Fisto embark on an impossible mission: to destroy the Separatists' mountain fortress. But their assignment has been made harder still by information divulged by a Republic captive, and the arrival of Asajj Ventress and her elite commando droids!
title: The Clone Wars #08: In Service of the Republic, Part 2

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #09: In Service of the Republic, Part 3
synopsis: Jedi Plo Koon and Kit Fisto have successfully removed one of the obstacles holding back their mission to free the Khormai people. If only they didn't have to deal with a cowardly Captain, who can't seem to keep out of trouble - and the Separatists' assassin, Asajj Ventress, who is on their trail!
title: The Clone Wars #09: In Service of the Republic, Part 3

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #10: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 1
synopsis: Anakin Skywalker, Jedi Knight and hero of the Republic, is challenged by a fledgling hero of the Confederacy! The Confederacy is being provided with newer and more advanced ships from a world that has claimed neutrality in the Clone Wars. Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi fly to Valahari to negotiate with the planet's rulers, but as events unfold, they find that diplomacy may have to give way to war!
title: The Clone Wars #10: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 1

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #11: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 2
synopsis: Blamed for the death of a member of the royal family of Valahari, Anakin Skywalker is exiled to a remote outpost on Ryloth. Meanwhile, Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrates the royal family's castle searching for the truth - which leads him from an unexpected encounter to a deadly one! But even finding the truth may come too late to save a noble hero of the Confederacy who has become Republic Enemy Number One!
title: The Clone Wars #11: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 2

displayTitle: The Clone Wars #12: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 3
synopsis: Anakin Skywalker is recalled to active duty to deal with a Separatist pilot who has been wreaking havoc on the Republic's fleets. But Anakin's chance to prove himself comes at a high price: the enemy ace is a friend. The costs of war have never been higher, and even Obi-Wan Kenobi's desperate attempts to find a path to peace may come too late.
title: The Clone Wars #12: Hero of the Confederacy, Part 3

displayTitle: The Clone Wars: A Jedi Adventure in 3D
synopsis: This amazing 3-D storybook told from the point of view of Jedi Padawan Ahsoka Tano features the best battles and action scenes from The Clone Wars animated series. Complete with perforated 3-D glasses, this 64-page storybook adds a whole new dimension to the Star Wars universe.
title: The Clone Wars: A Jedi Adventure in 3D

displayTitle: The Clone Wars: Ackbar's Underwater Army
synopsis: The watery world of Mon Calamari is under attack! Can Captain Ackbar save his species?
title: The Clone Wars: Ackbar's Underwater Army

displayTitle: The Clone Wars - Act On Instinct
synopsis: The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct is a webcomic in the Star Wars: The Clone Wars webcomic series taking place concurrently with The Clone Wars television series' second season. It began on September 30, 2009 and was updated semi-regularly until May 7, 2010, when its final chapter was published. The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct was written by Pablo Hidalgo. The art of the comic was done by Thomas Hodges, Grant Gould, Jeffery Carlisle, and Daniel Falconer. Special thanks were given to Sue Rostoni, Leland Chee, and Dave Filoni.
title: The Clone Wars - Act On Instinct

synopsis: The Clone Wars: Agenda is a short, six-page webcomic in The Clone Wars webcomic series. Posted on StarWars.com on October 14, 2008, Agenda leads directly into The Clone Wars television series episode "Destroy Malevolence." The comic was written by Pablo Hidalgo, illustrated and colored by Katie Cook, and lettered by Grant Gould. Agenda is about the Naboo Senator Padmé Amidala being assigned by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine of the Republic on a secret mission to the Kaliida Nebula to negotiate with the surrender of the InterGalactic Banking Clan.
title: The Clone Wars: Agenda

synopsis: In this full-color graphic novel, Jedi Master Yoda is on a secret diplomatic mission when his ship is ambushed by Count Dooku. The diminutive Jedi Master and three clone troopers must then face off against Dooku's dreaded assassin Ventress and her massive droid army.
title: The Clone Wars: Ambush

displayTitle: The Clone Wars: Anakin in Action!
synopsis: Get your child hooked on reading as they meet the Jedi Knight Anakin from the new Star Wars Clone Wars series. Designed to engage even the most reluctant reader, this fun-packed Clone Wars Reader is all about your child's favorite hero, Jedi Knight Anakin. Watch as your child meets Anakin and his young apprentice Ahsoka. They'll find out all about their dangerous missions battling fearsome foes from battle droids to the Sith Assassin, Ventress. Plus, they'll meet the Jedi, the clone soldiers who fight alongside them and check out the Clone Wars vehicles, spaceships and much more. Packed with p
title: The Clone Wars: Anakin in Action!

displayTitle: The Clone Wars Anthology: Stories of Light and Dark
synopsis: Clone Wars: Stories of Light and Dark is a fresh take on the animated series that has become a often-binged staple of Star Wars fans young and old. A talented stable of authors has adapted iconic episodes and story-arcs for inclusion in this anthology. Some are from a different character’s perspective from what we see in the tv show: the Senate hostage crisis is told from Anakin’s point of view rather than Padmé’s, for example. Other stories are told in a first-person-POV so as to lend a heavily-stylized slant to the familiar story. This is at the same time interesting, entertaining, and surprising, especially when we get inside the mind of a character such as Count Dooku! Plus, it includes one original story written exclusively for inclusion in this collection. Great for Clone Wars fans or for young readers looking to get started with Star Wars books.
title: The Clone Wars Anthology: Stories of Light and Dark

synopsis: "The Clone Wars: Bait" is a six-page webcomic and the eleventh installment in the Star Wars: The Clone Wars webcomic series. Its story takes place prior to the Star Wars: The Clone Wars television series episode "Dooku Captured." Posted on StarWars.com January 2, 2009, "Bait" was written by Pablo Hidalgo and illustrated and lettered by Grant Gould. "Bait" shows how Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker was captured while attempting to board a Separatist frigate. This set up Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi's rescue of Skywalker, shown in "Dooku Captured".
title: The Clone Wars: Bait

displayTitle: The Clone Wars: Battle At Teth
